- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
DN-064 fix incorrectly opens SessionLoggerModal (form) on exercise tap; original behavior opens the timer/counter logger view, and Codex previously reverted the same UX (DN-065 revert) per user direction.

Context:
- Commit 321fda1 added logger.openCreate(selected) inside handleExerciseSelect, wiring exercise tap → SessionLoggerModal form
- Commit 9b586a9 fixed a TDZ crash from the same change
- Reviewing public/index.html shows the original flow: exercise tap → selectExercise() → showView('logger') — a full-screen timer/counter view (Phase 4d), NOT a form modal
- Codex previously added the same tap-to-open behavior for DN-065, then reverted it per user direction (commit 59d4486)
- We re-introduced the same reverted UX via DN-064
- Phase 4c (SessionLoggerModal rebuild) and 4d (TimerPanel) are both in_progress with Codex (ptrebuild-weq, ptrebuild-ryf)

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do NOT re-add logger.openCreate() on exercise tap without explicit user approval
- Exercise tap should only SELECT the exercise (setSelectedExerciseId, setSelectedExercise, setActiveExercise) until TimerPanel is wired
- Wait for Codex 4c + 4d to complete before re-wiring
- TDZ fix (moving logger declaration above handleExerciseSelect) should be KEPT — it is correct regardless
- onPointerUp must remain (iOS-safe)

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] logger.openCreate() is NOT called from handleExerciseSelect
- [ ] Exercise tap still selects/highlights the exercise (setSelectedExerciseId etc.)
- [ ] SessionLoggerModal does not open on exercise tap
- [ ] No TDZ crash on SSR (logger declaration stays above handleExerciseSelect)
- [ ] Exercise tap → TimerPanel flow wired correctly once ptrebuild-ryf is done

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: ptrebuild-bdf (DN-064)
- blocks: nothing directly; must be resolved before 4i cutover
- waiting-on: ptrebuild-weq (DN-048), ptrebuild-ryf (DN-049)

{"description":"Source Reference: n/a (discovered this session)
Status: blocked
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: claude
Tags: nextjs,migration,ui

File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
DN-064 fix incorrectly opens SessionLoggerModal (form) on exercise tap; original behavior opens the full-screen TimerPanel (counter/timer), and Codex previously reverted the same UX (DN-065 revert) per user direction.

Context:
Original flow (public/index.html):
- Exercise tap → full-screen logger view (showView('logger')) = TimerPanel
  - Exercise name + dosage header
  - Side selector (Left/Right)
  - Large circular rep counter (tap to count) OR timer (for hold/duration exercises)
  - Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises

Two in-app logging paths within TimerPanel:
- Next Set (main path): user taps counter during exercise → Next Set confirmation modal
  (shows reps counted, form_data dropdown, side) → Cancel / Edit / Log & Next
- Log Set (hands-occupied path): for exercises where user can't tap counter (e.g. Dead Bug —
  hands in the air) → Log Set form modal (reps spinbox, form_data dropdown, side) → Save Set
  Both are real-time in-app flows, not retrospective.

What our DN-064 fix did wrong:
- Commit 321fda1 added logger.openCreate(selected) inside handleExerciseSelect, wiring
  exercise tap directly to SessionLoggerModal form (skipping TimerPanel entirely)
- Commit 9b586a9 fixed a TDZ crash from the same change (logger.openCreate ref before decl)
- Codex had already done the same thing for DN-065 and reverted it per user direction (commit 59d4486)
- We re-introduced the same reverted UX via DN-064

Phase 4c (ptrebuild-weq) and 4d (ptrebuild-ryf) are both in_progress with Codex.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do NOT wire exercise tap to SessionLoggerModal or any modal — tap opens TimerPanel
- Exercise tap in current state should only SELECT the exercise until TimerPanel is ready
- Wait for Codex 4c + 4d to complete before re-wiring
- TDZ fix (moving logger declaration above handleExerciseSelect) — KEEP regardless
- onPointerUp must remain (iOS-safe)
- SessionLoggerModal is the 'Log Set' and 'Next Set' modals used from WITHIN TimerPanel

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] logger.openCreate() is NOT called from handleExerciseSelect
- [ ] Exercise tap selects/highlights the exercise only
- [ ] SessionLoggerModal does NOT open on exercise tap
- [ ] No TDZ crash on SSR (logger declaration stays above handleExerciseSelect)
- [ ] Exercise tap → TimerPanel, TimerPanel → Log Set / Next Set modals

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: ptrebuild-bdf (DN-064)
- blocks: nothing directly; must be resolved before 4i cutover
- waiting-on: ptrebuild-weq (DN-048), ptrebuild-ryf (DN-049)

Reactivation Trigger:
Codex closes ptrebuild-weq and ptrebuild-ryf (4c + 4d complete)"}

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
DN-064 fix incorrectly opens SessionLoggerModal (form) on exercise tap; original behavior opens the full-screen TimerPanel (counter/timer), and Codex previously reverted the same UX (DN-065 revert) per user direction.

Context:
Original flow (public/index.html):
- Exercise tap → full-screen logger view (showView('logger')) = TimerPanel
  - Exercise name + dosage header
  - Side selector (Left/Right)
  - Large circular rep counter (tap to count) OR timer (for hold/duration exercises)
  - Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises

Two logging paths within TimerPanel:
- Log Set = enter data myself (manual reps/params entry — any reason: retrospective,
  hands occupied, counter not used). Form modal: reps spinbox, form_data dropdown, side → Save Set
- Next Set = use app data (what counter/timer recorded) and move on. Confirmation modal:
  shows recorded reps, form_data dropdown, side → Cancel / Edit / Log & Next

What our DN-064 fix did wrong:
- Commit 321fda1 added logger.openCreate(selected) inside handleExerciseSelect, wiring
  exercise tap directly to SessionLoggerModal (skipping TimerPanel entirely)
- Commit 9b586a9 fixed a TDZ crash from the same change
- Codex had already done the same for DN-065 and reverted per user direction (commit 59d4486)
- We re-introduced the same reverted UX via DN-064

Phase 4c (ptrebuild-weq) and 4d (ptrebuild-ryf) are both in_progress with Codex.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do NOT wire exercise tap to SessionLoggerModal or any modal — tap opens TimerPanel
- Exercise tap in current state should only SELECT the exercise until TimerPanel is ready
- Wait for Codex 4c + 4d to complete before re-wiring
- TDZ fix (moving logger declaration above handleExerciseSelect) — KEEP regardless
- onPointerUp must remain (iOS-safe)
- SessionLoggerModal = Log Set and Next Set modals used from WITHIN TimerPanel

- waiting-on: ptrebuild-weq (DN-048), ptrebuild-ryf (DN-049)

{"description":"Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, sync, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js

Issue:
App-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced offline queue entries. The 'online' event listener only fires when the device comes back online mid-session, not on initial mount.

Context:
The offline write queue (pt_offline_queue_${userId}) correctly accumulates sessions when offline and syncs when the 'online' event fires. But if the app is opened fresh while already online with queued items, sync never triggers — entries stay stranded until the user manually refreshes or goes offline/online.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve idempotency — sync already uses client_mutation_id and treats 409 as success. Do not re-introduce /api/sync. Queue is user-scoped; must check userId before syncing.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
In the useEffect that registers the 'online' event listener, add a mount-time sync check:
  - After registering the listener, check if navigator.onLine === true AND queueRef.current.length > 0
  - If so, call sync() immediately
  - This runs once on mount (after queue loads from localStorage), catching stranded entries
Note: queue loads in a separate useEffect dependent on userId — mount-time sync must fire after that useEffect runs. Best approach: add a separate useEffect dependent on [queue, sync] that checks navigator.onLine on the first render once queue is populated.

Validation Checklist:
- Queue items present + app opened online → sync fires on mount, items cleared
- Queue empty + app opened online → no unnecessary sync call
- App opened offline → no sync attempted (navigator.onLine false)
- Online event mid-session still works as before

Dependencies: child of ptrebuild-nyn"}

{"id":"ptrebuild-kj5","title":"[DN-067] Offline resilience parity gap: app-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced entries, and offline cold-open can fail with "Offline and no cached data available" when cache hydration has not previously occurred.","description":"Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: codex,claude

Issue:
Offline resilience parity gap: app-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced entries, and offline cold-open can fail with "Offline and no cached data available" when cache hydration has not previously occurred.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/public/index.html, pt-rebuild/pages/index.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js, pt-rebuild/public/js/offline.js

Context:
User confirmed this can be deferred to nextjs implementation if that is lower-friction. Current static behavior attempts cache fallback but cannot serve first offline open without prior hydration, and sync is currently online-event/manual driven rather than on-open.

Constraints/Caveats:
Default plan: implement in nextjs path first, then decide if static hotfix is still needed before cutover. Preserve idempotency and user-scoped queue semantics. Do not re-introduce /api/sync.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T01:59:39Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-06T01:59:39Z","external_ref":"DN-067"}

From the in-panel exercise logger, 'Log Set' is the manual-entry path and 'Next Set' is the app-recorded path; exercise tap itself does not jump straight into SessionLoggerModal. On production, the logger panel owns the exercise execution flow first, and logging choices happen from within that panel. Current Next.js remains a partial handoff implementation because TimerPanel still exposes 'Use This Value' / 'Open Full Log Form' rather than the production 'Log Set' / 'Next Set' paths. Keep ptrebuild-cva blocked until this panel-level handoff is aligned.

Keep ptrebuild-cva blocked until Next.js matches that panel-first flow.

old_value:
{"id":"ptrebuild-no3","title":"Auth sign-in modal flashes on page navigation and stays up / fails when offline","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered during offline investigation, reported by user 2026-03-13)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium — affects all pages, every navigation
Scope: All Next.js pages (index.js, rehab.js, pt-view.js, program.js)
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: auth, offline, flash, ux, nextjs, migration, reliability
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Issue:
Two related auth problems:
1. (Online) Sign-in modal briefly flashes when navigating between pages or opening the app, then disappears. Occurs even when the user is authenticated.
2. (Offline) Sign-in modal appears when app is opened offline and does not dismiss. Entering credentials shows 'Offline - no cached version available'.

Context:
Root cause (online flash): useAuth.js registers onAuthStateChange which fires events during Supabase token refresh (e.g., TOKEN_REFRESHED). The handler unconditionally calls setSession(sess) — if sess is momentarily null during a refresh cycle and loading is already false, pages with \!session && \!authLoading show AuthForm briefly.

Root cause (offline): supabase.auth.getSession() attempts to refresh an expired/near-expired token via network. When offline, this refresh network call fails, getSession() returns null session, loading is set to false, and all pages show AuthForm with no way to dismiss offline.

Supabase stores the session in localStorage under key: sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token. This stored session is available offline and contains a valid refresh_token. It should be used as an offline fallback.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword offline — it will fail.
- The offline session is potentially stale (token expired) but the user IS authenticated. Allow read-only data access from cache using the stored access_token.
- Do not show AuthForm when offline and a stored session exists — show an offline indicator instead.
- When back online, let Supabase refresh the token normally.
- Must not break normal sign-out flow.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Fix 1 (online flash): In onAuthStateChange handler, only call setSession(null) when event === 'SIGNED_OUT'. For all other events, update session only when the new value is non-null:
  supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, sess) => {
      if (event === 'SIGNED_OUT') { setSession(null); }
      else if (sess) { setSession(sess); }
  });

Fix 2 (offline fallback): In the getSession().then() handler, if sess is null and \!navigator.onLine, attempt to read the stored session from localStorage:
  const STORAGE_KEY = 'sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token';
  If sess is null and offline: parse localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY), use stored session if valid (has access_token + user). Mark as offline session (expose isOffline: boolean from hook). Clear when back online (refresh via getSession() on 'online' event).

Validation Checklist:
- Navigate between pages while signed in → no AuthForm flash
- Open app while signed in → goes directly to tracker, no AuthForm
- Open app offline (session stored) → tracker loads, no AuthForm, offline indicator shown
- Open app offline (no stored session, never signed in) → AuthForm shown (correct)
- Sign out → clears session normally, AuthForm shown
- Come back online after offline session → getSession() re-runs, fresh token loaded

  supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, sess) => {

      else if (sess) { setSession(sess); }
  });

Fix 2 (offline fallback): If getSession() returns null and !navigator.onLine, read stored session from localStorage key sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token and use it as fallback. Expose isOffline: boolean from hook.

- Open app offline (never signed in) → AuthForm shown (correct)
- Sign out → AuthForm shown correctly
- Back online after offline session → fresh token loaded

{"id":"ptrebuild-zb3","title":"Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js pages/index.js offline path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises (and programs/logs) fail to load. The screen shows the 'Offline - no cached version available' message. There is no read-side cache fallback — useIndexData.js only fetches from the API with no localStorage fallback.

Context:
The static app (public/index.html) solved this with IndexedDB via offline.js (hydrateCache + getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs). The Next.js migration has no equivalent. useIndexData.js calls fetchIndexExercises/fetchIndexPrograms/fetchIndexLogs — all pure network calls. On fetch failure, setError is called and exercises stays [].

The offline WRITE path (session queue) is implemented and correct. Only the READ path (loading exercise/program/log data) is missing the cache.

User-reported symptom: app opened offline shows 'Offline - no cached version available' — no exercises displayed.

Constraints/Caveats:
Use localStorage (consistent with offline queue pattern, no async init required, datasets are small). Do NOT use IndexedDB (adds complexity, async init, SSR guards needed). Cache keys must be user-scoped to prevent cross-user leakage: pt_cache_exercises_\, pt_cache_programs_\, pt_cache_logs_\. Clear cache on sign-out. Do not re-introduce /api/sync.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-offline.js — add helpers:
   - saveDataCache(userId, key, data): localStorage.setItem('pt_cache_' + key + '_' + userId, JSON.stringify(data))
   - loadDataCache(userId, key): parse from localStorage, return [] on missing/error
   - clearDataCache(userId): remove all three cache keys on sign-out

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - After successful Promise.all: call saveDataCache for exercises, programs, logs
   - In catch block: attempt loadDataCache for each; if any returns non-empty data, use it (setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs from cache); only call setError if cache is also empty
   - Expose fromCache: boolean so index.js can show an 'offline — showing cached data' indicator if desired

3. hooks/useIndexData.js — on userId change (sign-out): clear cache via clearDataCache

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → exercises load from API → cache written to localStorage
- Open app offline (cache exists) → exercises load from localStorage cache → no error shown
- Open app offline (no cache, first time) → error shown: 'No cached data available'
- Sign out → cache cleared for that user
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated

{"id":"ptrebuild-no3","title":"Auth sign-in modal flashes on app open and page navigation; fails when offline","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered during offline investigation, reported by user 2026-03-13)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium — affects all pages, every navigation
Scope: Static app confirmed. Next.js unverified — must check before implementing.
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: auth, offline, flash, ux, nextjs, migration, reliability
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js (Next.js), pt-rebuild/public/index.html (static)

Issue:
Two related auth problems (observed on static app; Next.js occurrence unverified):
1. (Online) Sign-in modal briefly flashes on app open and when navigating between pages, then disappears. Occurs even when the user is authenticated.
2. (Offline) Sign-in modal appears when app is opened offline and does not dismiss. Entering credentials shows 'Offline - no cached version available'.

IMPORTANT — Verification required before implementing:
User confirmed this was observed on the static app (public/index.html). It may not occur in the Next.js version. Before writing any code, verify on the Next.js preview (https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app):
  - Open app while signed in — does AuthForm flash?
  - Navigate between /rehab, /pt-view, /program — does AuthForm flash?
  - Open app offline — does AuthForm stay up and block access?
If none of these reproduce on Next.js, this is a static-only bug and should be moved to dev_notes.json (non-NextJS tracker).

Context:
Root cause (online flash, Next.js theory): useAuth.js onAuthStateChange unconditionally calls setSession(sess). If sess is momentarily null during a Supabase token refresh cycle and loading is already false, pages with !session && !authLoading show AuthForm briefly.

Root cause (offline, Next.js theory): supabase.auth.getSession() attempts to refresh an expired/near-expired token via network. When offline, this refresh fails, getSession() returns null session, loading is set to false, and all pages show AuthForm.

Supabase stores session in localStorage: sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token.

Constraints/Caveats:
- If confirmed on Next.js: fix useAuth.js only (no page-level changes needed).
- If static-only: move to dev_notes.json and out of Beads.
- Do not show AuthForm when offline and a stored session exists.
- Do not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword offline.

Specific Fix (if Next.js confirmed):
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Fix 1 (online flash): In onAuthStateChange, only set session to null on SIGNED_OUT:
  supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, sess) => {
      if (event === 'SIGNED_OUT') { setSession(null); }
      else if (sess) { setSession(sess); }
  });

Fix 2 (offline fallback): If getSession() returns null and !navigator.onLine, read stored session from localStorage key sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token and use it as fallback. Expose isOffline: boolean from hook.

Validation Checklist:
- Open app signed in → no AuthForm flash (online)
- Navigate between pages signed in → no AuthForm flash
- Open app offline (session stored) → tracker loads, no AuthForm
- Open app offline (never signed in) → AuthForm shown (correct)
- Sign out → AuthForm shown correctly
- Back online after offline session → fresh token loaded

{"description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there."}

{"notes":"2026-03-06: Implemented TimerPanel/useTimerSpeech and wired index exercise tap to open TimerPanel. Added countdown beeps (3/2/1), completion triple-beep, and speech cues (start/pause/milestones/set complete). Build passes locally. Commit: 46ef84c.
2026-03-13: Codex fixed TimerPanel state reset/parity issues: reopening the same exercise now resets timer/counter state, hold mode tracks completed reps explicitly, and 'Use This Value' stays disabled until the timer/counter has actually recorded something. Local next build passes after the change.
2026-03-13: Production parity check on https://pttracker.app using the real patient account confirms 4d entry behavior. Plain reps exercises do NOT open SessionLoggerModal directly; they open the same in-panel exercise logger used for hold exercises. Example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened a panel with side selector, big tappable rep counter, Undo, Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises. Hold exercise 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls added (Rep 1 of 10, countdown, Start/Reset, same lower controls). Pocket Mode is part of this same 4d flow: it opens a full-screen overlay, shows countdown/meta text for timer exercises, and updates hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running. This means current Next.js behavior is still behind production parity: TimerPanel needs to be the entry path for both reps and hold/duration exercises, and Pocket Mode needs explicit overlay behavior rather than being omitted.
2026-03-13: Production check on https://pttracker.app with the real patient account confirmed that reps-only exercises and hold exercises both open the same in-panel logger. Reps example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened side selector + tappable rep counter + Previous/Log Set/Next Set/Done/Pocket Mode/Back controls. Hold example: 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls plus those lower controls. Pocket Mode is part of 4d and is a full-screen overlay; for hold exercises it shows countdown/meta text and changes hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running.
2026-03-13: Next.js branch now routes all exercise taps into TimerPanel to match production panel-first behavior, and the timer state machine was trimmed back to the structure cap while preserving build-green behavior.
2026-03-13 parity review findings still open in 4d: (1) Pocket Mode does not yet reflect real session progress or side-aware progress; current Next.js panel/overlay still uses placeholder-style counts instead of session-backed left/right and sets-left state. (2) Pocket Mode partial-hold handling diverged from static semantics because Next.js set patches were carrying partial_rep=true; static logger keeps partial_rep false in the persisted set payload. These findings stay under 4d because they are part of the timer/pocket execution flow.
2026-03-13: Closed child ptrebuild-f1j after preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Verified Pocket Mode now reflects session-backed progress, reps and hold exercises both stay panel-first, and a captured hold payload after Pocket Mode long-press logging showed manual_log=false and partial_rep=false. ptrebuild-ryf remains open pending broader audio/speech parity confidence, but the panel/pocket parity slice is complete."}

old_value: {"id":"ptrebuild-1qq.1","title":"[DN-056] In-session last-used form_data parity is still missing for panel-seeded/create flows","description":"Static getLastUsedParamValue(exerciseId,paramName,side) checks currentSession before persisted history, and showLogSetModal/showNextSetModal call renderFormParameterFields each time the modal opens. Current Next.js computes default_form_data from logs only when the exercise is selected and then reuses that snapshot for handleTimerApplySet and handleTimerOpenManual. SessionLoggerModal historicalFormParams also comes from collectGlobalParameterValues(logs), which excludes current in-session sets until reload. Result: after a user changes a form parameter earlier in the same session, later create/next-set/panel-seeded logs can keep stale defaults/options instead of the latest in-session value.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:26:45Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:26:45Z"}

{"notes":"2026-03-13 Beads process feedback for later keep/change decision:
- Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for narrowing parity work without bloating the main 4c/4d tasks; closure-block notes made the final state easy to recover; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is synced; this can block bd dolt pull with a confusing 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until the metadata table is committed. Concurrent writers also make auto-push non-fast-forward failures fairly common.
- Operational lesson: for parallel sessions, implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can happen in separate threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Revisit later with Claude: decide whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active."}

Issue:
Finish a full-source harvest of static `public/index.html` into reconstruction-grade OpenSpec docs so agents can rebuild or delta-check the tracker without direct access to the original 5k-line file.

Context:
Current OpenSpec coverage is improved but still incomplete for a full rebuild standard. The user clarified that OpenSpec should let agents reconstruct static index behavior, UX, timing, copy, auth states, API calls, offline behavior, and edge cases without opening `index.html` or discovering flow truth through Playwright. A partial source sweep has already harvested major domains including auth bootstrap, role/viewing context, picker/logger/history shell, timer/counter execution, Pocket Mode, manual log-set and next-set flows, messages, history maintenance, offline queue sync, and delegated action handling. Remaining work is to continue harvesting the full source systematically, keep a visible coverage ledger, and revise the OpenSpec until it functions as the canonical static contract for migration deltas.

Constraints/Caveats:
Do not implement application code in this issue. This is documentation and planning work only. Treat uncovered parts of `public/index.html` as undocumented until harvested from source. Prefer structured inventories (surfaces, state, actions, APIs, timing, copy, edge cases, source coverage) over broad prose summaries. The resulting docs must be usable by agents who do not have `index.html` available.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] OpenSpec records shell surfaces, controls, labels, and modal ownership in enough detail to rebuild the static tracker shell
- [ ] OpenSpec records state, action, API, auth, timing, and offline contracts from the full source harvest rather than assumption
- [ ] OpenSpec includes functionally important copy, blocked states, and edge cases that would otherwise be discovered late in runtime testing
- [ ] Source coverage ledger makes it clear which `index.html` sections have been harvested and what behavior was extracted from each

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-bdf

{"id":"pt-kj5","title":"[DN-067] Offline resilience parity gap: app-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced entries, and offline cold-open can fail with "Offline and no cached data available" when cache hydration has not previously occurred.","description":"Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, sync, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js

Issue:
App-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced offline queue entries. The 'online' event listener only fires when the device comes back online mid-session, not on initial mount.

Context:
The offline write queue (pt_offline_queue_${userId}) correctly accumulates sessions when offline and syncs when the 'online' event fires. But if the app is opened fresh while already online with queued items, sync never triggers — entries stay stranded until the user manually refreshes or goes offline/online.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve idempotency — sync already uses client_mutation_id and treats 409 as success. Do not re-introduce /api/sync. Queue is user-scoped; must check userId before syncing.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
In the useEffect that registers the 'online' event listener, add a mount-time sync check:
  - After registering the listener, check if navigator.onLine === true AND queueRef.current.length > 0
  - If so, call sync() immediately
  - This runs once on mount (after queue loads from localStorage), catching stranded entries
Note: queue loads in a separate useEffect dependent on userId — mount-time sync must fire after that useEffect runs. Best approach: add a separate useEffect dependent on [queue, sync] that checks navigator.onLine on the first render once queue is populated.

Validation Checklist:
- Queue items present + app opened online → sync fires on mount, items cleared
- Queue empty + app opened online → no unnecessary sync call
- App opened offline → no sync attempted (navigator.onLine false)
- Online event mid-session still works as before

Static app hides side selector for bilateral exercises (pattern !== 'side') and logs side=null. Next.js modal must match: hide selector for bilateral, omit side from payload.

Discovered during DN-058 side checklist review (2026-03-03). Confirmed via backup JSON (823 sets): distinct side values are 'right', 'left', null — 'both' never appears. Static index.html: isSided = exercise.pattern === 'side'; bilateral exercises get currentSide=null and side selector hidden. Next.js SessionLoggerModal always shows left/right/both when hasSide is true — incorrect for bilateral pattern. - Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for narrowing parity work without bloating the main 4c/4d tasks; closure-block notes made the final state easy to recover; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is synced; this can block bd dolt pull with a confusing 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until the metadata table is committed. Concurrent writers also make auto-push non-fast-forward failures fairly common.
- Operational lesson: for parallel sessions, implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can happen in separate threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Revisit later with Claude: decide whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T01:59:49Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:06:21Z","external_ref":"DN-045"}

Issue:
Resolve duplicate permissive SELECT policies on patient_activity_logs, patient_activity_sets, patient_activity_set_form_data, and vocab_* tables.

Context:
Supabase performance advisor flags 9 tables with two SELECT policies for `authenticated` on the same table. Both policies are evaluated per query. The older `_select_own` / `patient_activity_*_select` policies predate the broader `activity_logs_select` / `activity_sets_select` / `set_form_data_select` policies. The broader policies cover all cases the older ones do, plus therapist access. The older narrow ones can likely be dropped — but access behavior must be verified first.

{"id":"pt-zdk.18","title":"[DN-022] Offline queue is stored under a global localStorage key (`pt_offline_queue`) and is not cleared/scoped on sign-out, risking cross-user data carryover on shared devices.","description":"Source Reference: legacy DN-022 from docs/dev_notes.json
Status: open

Scope: deferred
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: offline,auth,security,reliability
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html

Issue:
Offline queue is stored under a global localStorage key (`pt_offline_queue`) and is not cleared/scoped on sign-out, risking cross-user data carryover on shared devices.

Context:
Queue load/save are key-based only and `signOut()` only ends Supabase auth session. A subsequent user on the same device/browser profile can inherit prior unsynced sessions.

Preserve behavior during migration from dev_notes to Beads.

{"id":"pt-nyn.1","title":"Fix index date/time parity for pre-midnight entries","description":"Source Reference: user-reported parity bug on 2026-03-16
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,parity,datetime,logging,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html

Issue:
Next.js index date/time tracking does not match static parity around midnight boundaries. Entries recorded just before midnight should be interpreted as yesterday, matching the static tracker behavior.

Context:
User reported a parity gap in the index logging flow: the Next.js branch needs the same date/day handling as static when a session is logged before midnight. The static tracker treats a pre-midnight entry as belonging to the previous day/yesterday. The Next.js path needs to preserve that same rule in create, display, and any derived history/adherence logic that depends on the logged local date.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve local-time semantics. Do not introduce UTC/date-shift regressions in datetime-local handling. Verify both manual entry and any default/seeded date behavior against static index flow. If helper logic is shared with history or adherence calculations, keep the rule consistent across those consumers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Logging an entry before local midnight stores/displays it as yesterday, matching static behavior
- [ ] Logging after midnight still maps to the current local day
- [ ] Datetime-local roundtrip does not shift the intended local date due to timezone conversion
- [ ] History/adherence or related index summaries use the same effective day rule

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-nyn

Issue:
The static index already applies a calendar-day rule for date/time labeling and tracking, but the Next.js build has not preserved that behavior. Entries from yesterday must remain yesterday after local midnight, even when they are less than 24 hours old.

Context:
This is a static-parity carryover bug, not a new product rule. The current Next.js behavior appears to use a rolling 24-hour window instead of local calendar-day boundaries. Example: at 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is being shown as "today". Static behavior explicitly handles this as yesterday. The Next.js path needs to port that existing rule in create, display, and any derived history/adherence logic that depends on the logged local date.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve local-time semantics. Do not introduce UTC/date-shift regressions in datetime-local handling. Verify against the explicit static index implementation rather than inferring behavior from current Next.js output. If helper logic is shared with history or adherence calculations, keep the rule consistent across those consumers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Logging an entry before local midnight stores/displays it as yesterday, matching static behavior
- [ ] Logging after midnight still maps to the current local day
- [ ] Datetime-local roundtrip does not shift the intended local date due to timezone conversion
- [ ] History/adherence or related index summaries use the same explicit static day rule

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-nyn

{"description":"Source Reference: static index.html parity rule reported by user on 2026-03-16
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,parity,datetime,logging,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html

Issue:
The static index already applies a local calendar-day rule for date/time labeling and tracking, but the Next.js build has not preserved that behavior. The effective day must be determined by the local date boundary at midnight, not by whether the timestamp is within the last 24 hours.

Context:
This is a static-parity carryover bug, not a new product rule. Current Next.js behavior appears to use a rolling 24-hour window instead of local calendar-day boundaries. Example: at 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is incorrectly shown as "today". Static behavior explicitly treats that entry as yesterday because its local calendar date is yesterday.

Expected Behavior:
- If the logged local date is today, show today.
- If the logged local date is yesterday, show yesterday, even if fewer than 24 hours have elapsed.
- Do not classify entries by elapsed hours alone.
- Use the same effective-day rule anywhere index logging, history summaries, or adherence/status labeling depend on the day of the session.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve local-time semantics. Do not introduce UTC/date-shift regressions in datetime-local handling. Verify against the explicit static index implementation rather than inferring behavior from current Next.js output. If helper logic is shared with history or adherence calculations, keep the rule consistent across those consumers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] At 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is shown/labeled as yesterday
- [ ] Logging an entry before local midnight stores/displays it as yesterday after midnight passes
- [ ] Logging after midnight still maps to the current local day
- [ ] Datetime-local roundtrip does not shift the intended local date due to timezone conversion
- [ ] History/adherence or related index summaries use the same explicit static day rule

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-nyn

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a"}

{"id":"pt-ant","title":"Fix iPhone dropdown/select failure on /program","description":"Source Reference: user-confirmed iPhone bug on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,program,ios,ui,editor,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.module.css
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCore.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCues.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.module.css
- pt-rebuild/styles/globals.css

Issue:
The Next.js exercise editor on `/program` has dropdown/select controls that do not work on iPhone/iOS.

Context:
User confirmed the bug on a real iPhone. Code review confirms `/program` does not use a custom dropdown widget; it relies on native `<select>` controls in the page-level selector and inside the ExerciseForm sections. The likely failure mode is iOS Safari interaction with the current container/CSS setup rather than missing data or business logic.

Observed Implementation Notes:
- `pages/program.js:213-224` renders the top-level exercise selector as a native `<select>`.
- `components/ExerciseFormCore.js:133-143` renders native selects for category and pattern.
- `components/ExerciseFormCues.js:183-229` renders native selects for role fields.
- `components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js:42-49` and `components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js:82-91` render native lifecycle selects.
- `components/ExerciseFormCore.js`, `components/ExerciseFormCues.js`, and `components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js` place those controls inside native `<details>` sections.
- `components/ExerciseForm.module.css:68-78`, `pages/program.module.css:57-65`, and `styles/globals.css:57-68` apply `touch-action: manipulation` to the form/select stack and globally on `body`.

Hypothesis:
This is likely an iOS Safari compatibility issue involving native selects inside the current details/summary form structure and/or the broad `touch-action: manipulation` styling on the select stack. There is no evidence of custom JavaScript preventing the dropdowns from opening.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve iOS-safe tap behavior for buttons while fixing native select usability. Do not replace native selects with a custom dropdown unless necessary. Verify on actual iPhone/Safari after the fix.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Top-level exercise selector on `/program` opens and changes value on iPhone
- [ ] Form dropdowns inside ExerciseForm sections open and allow selection on iPhone
- [ ] Any fix preserves other touch interactions on `/program`
- [ ] No regression for desktop or Android/native browser select behavior

Dependencies:
- none

Reactivation Trigger:

{"description":"Source Reference: user-confirmed iPhone bug on 2026-03-17, initially reported on `/program`
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ios,ui,editor,dropdown,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCore.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCues.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/styles/globals.css

Issue:
Dropdown/select behavior across the Next.js app needs one shared iPhone-safe pattern instead of page-by-page implementations. The immediate confirmed bug is on `/program`, but the fix should be organized as shared dropdown behavior with per-page follow-through.

Context:
User confirmed the bug on a real iPhone. Code review confirms the Next.js app currently uses multiple native `<select>` and datalist/select patterns across `/program`, `/index`, and `/pt-view`. `/index` already contains the most proven dropdown behavior because its form-parameter flow supports both standard native selects and an `Other...` path that reveals a text input. That working pattern should become the reuse baseline rather than creating another one-off fix.

Observed Implementation Notes:
- `/program` uses native selects in the page-level selector and throughout the exercise editor form.
- `/index` uses native select behavior in `SessionLoggerModal` and already supports `Other...` fallback for form parameters.
- `/pt-view` uses a simpler native select for exercise filtering.
- Current select behavior is distributed across multiple components and CSS modules instead of one shared pattern.

Desired Direction:
- Reuse the working Next.js index dropdown pattern.
- Shared pattern must cover both plain native selects and native selects with an `Other...` option that reveals a text input.
- Prefer a small shared component or shared select behavior/pattern over a custom dropdown widget.
- Keep native selects unless they prove impossible to make reliable on iPhone.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve iOS-safe tap behavior for buttons while fixing native select usability. Do not replace native selects with a custom dropdown unless necessary. Verify on actual iPhone/Safari after fixes.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] `/program` selectors work on iPhone
- [ ] `/index` keeps its working dropdown behavior while being reused/shared
- [ ] `/pt-view` filter select is compatible with the same shared pattern
- [ ] No regression for desktop or Android/native browser select behavior

Dependencies:
- none

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a","title":"Standardize iPhone-safe dropdown/select behavior across Next.js pages"}

{"description":"Source Reference: pt-ant

- pt-rebuild/lib/session-form-params.js
- pt-rebuild/components/

Issue:
Extract the working `/index` dropdown behavior into a shared reusable select UI component/module in `components/` that other pages can import.

Context:
`/index` is the current proof point because `SessionLoggerModal` already supports both plain native selects and an `Other...` path for form parameters. This issue is not just to copy that structure elsewhere. It is to turn the proven index behavior into a reusable implementation that centralizes the JSX/CSS/interaction rules for iPhone-safe selects.

Required Output:
- A shared reusable component/module in `components/`
- It must support:
  - plain native select fields
  - native select plus `Other...` fallback text entry
- It may use small supporting pure helpers, but the reuse must be at the UI-component/module level, not just helper functions.

Constraints/Caveats:
Keep the behavior native-select-first. Preserve current working index behavior while extracting it. Shared implementation must be suitable for adoption on `/program` and `/pt-view`.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current `/index` dropdown behavior is preserved
- [ ] Shared reusable select component/module exists in `components/`
- [ ] `Other...` fallback remains supported through the shared implementation

{"description":"Source Reference: user-confirmed iPhone bug on 2026-03-17, initially reported on `/program`
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ios,ui,editor,dropdown,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCore.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCues.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/styles/globals.css

Issue:
Dropdown/select behavior across the Next.js app must be consolidated into a shared reusable UI implementation, not re-created page by page. The immediate confirmed bug is on `/program`, but the correct fix is to extract a shared iPhone-safe select component/module and then adopt it on each page that uses dropdowns.

Context:
User confirmed the bug on a real iPhone. Code review confirms the Next.js app currently uses multiple native `<select>` and datalist/select patterns across `/program`, `/index`, and `/pt-view`. `/index` already contains the most proven dropdown behavior because its form-parameter flow supports both standard native selects and an `Other...` path that reveals a text input. That working behavior should become the source for a shared reusable implementation rather than being copied manually into each page.

Required Architecture Direction:
- Implement a shared reusable select UI module/component in `components/` rather than repeating select markup/CSS/behavior per page.
- The shared implementation must support both:
  - plain native select fields
  - native select fields with an `Other...` option that reveals a text input
- Keep the implementation native-select-first for iPhone/Safari compatibility.
- Do not treat this as a pure `lib/` extraction only; the reuse must cover the JSX/CSS/interaction layer, not just helper functions.

Observed Implementation Notes:
- `/program` uses native selects in the page-level selector and throughout the exercise editor form.
- `/index` uses native select behavior in `SessionLoggerModal` and already supports `Other...` fallback for form parameters.
- `/pt-view` uses a simpler native select for exercise filtering.
- Current select behavior is distributed across multiple components and CSS modules instead of one shared component/module.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve iOS-safe tap behavior for buttons while fixing native select usability. Do not replace native selects with a custom dropdown unless necessary. Verify on actual iPhone/Safari after fixes.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A shared reusable select component/module exists in `components/`
- [ ] `/program` selectors use the shared implementation and work on iPhone
- [ ] `/index` keeps its working dropdown behavior while being routed through the shared implementation
- [ ] `/pt-view` filter select is aligned to the same shared implementation
- [ ] No regression for desktop or Android/native browser select behavior

Dependencies:
- none

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a"}

- [ ] `Other...` can be enabled or disabled per use site

{"id":"pt-x57","title":"Add shared input normalization modes for reusable fields","description":"Source Reference: follow-up design request from user on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,forms,input,normalization,refactor,ui
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/lib/
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Design and implement a shared input-normalization mechanism so reusable form fields can explicitly choose how free-text input should be normalized.

Context:
This came up while defining the shared select-plus-`Other...` component/module. Some fields may allow free-text entry and need a consistent normalization rule, such as lowercase, title case, sentence case, or no normalization. That rule should not be reimplemented ad hoc inside each page or field.

Required Direction:
- Add a shared mechanism that lets a field declare its normalization mode.
- Expected modes include at least:
  - `none`
  - `lower`
  - `title`
  - `sentence`
- This should be designed as a separate concern from dropdown interaction behavior, but it must be easy to use together with shared select/`Other...` components.
- Prefer a small shared helper/module plus a clear component API over page-local string manipulation.

Examples:
- A select-with-`Other...` field may allow `Other...` and normalize entered text to lowercase.
- Another field may allow free text but preserve title case.
- A plain text field may opt into the same normalization mechanism without using a dropdown at all.

Constraints/Caveats:
Do not silently force one normalization rule on all fields. The call site must explicitly choose the mode. Preserve the ability for some fields to opt out entirely.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Shared normalization mechanism exists
- [ ] Field/API surface allows explicit per-field normalization choice
- [ ] Works cleanly with shared select/`Other...` field behavior
- [ ] Fields can opt out with `none`

Dependencies:
- related: pt-ant

- ExerciseForm selects adopt the shared implementation where applicable

- no page-local copy of the old dropdown behavior remains unless there is a documented exception

- [ ] ExerciseForm selects work on iPhone

- [ ] Fields that should not offer `Other...` keep it disabled

- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/design.md

- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-messages-and-history-parity/spec.md
- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/design-extract.md
- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/baseline-design.md

Issue:
Complete the split capability specs until implementation and Beads-slicing agents no longer need to open public/index.html or reread design-extract.md to understand or rebuild static tracker behavior.

Context:
This is the main anti-loss step in the chain. The user’s target is a true never-need-to-look-at-index baseline that captures every last feature, button, UX rule, process, flow, API call, state clear/reset, timing rule, and copy detail needed for full migration and reconstruction. The extraction harvest exists as evidence, but agents should assume they will not go back to it during Next.js work; the normative WHAT contract must therefore carry forward every extraction detail that would matter during implementation or Beads slicing.

Constraints/Caveats:
Specs must define WHAT the system does in normative, scenario-based terms. Do not turn the spec into migration design. Do not assume details are safe just because they exist in design-extract or baseline-design; if an implementation agent or Beads author needs them, the spec layer must carry them explicitly. Preserve named values verbatim when they affect parity, including copy strings, field names, helper names, storage keys, mutation identifiers, exact clear/reset targets, API outcomes, and ordered sequences that change visible behavior.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Split specs are complete enough that an agent can explain and slice static tracker behavior without opening public/index.html
- [ ] Beads can be authored from the canonical spec package without rediscovering behavior in design-extract.md
- [ ] User-visible flows, timing, ordering, role context, save behavior, offline behavior, copy, API interactions, and exact clear/reset behavior are explicit in the specs
- [ ] No migration-critical tracker behavior exists only in design-extract or baseline-design without a usable contract path in the split specs
- [ ] Runtime testing is no longer required to discover baseline truth; it is only used to validate implementation

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-7fy

Status: open
Priority: P1

Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,governance,beads
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/.beads/
- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/tasks.md

Issue:
Review the active Next.js migration Beads and add cross-references anywhere a parity Bead and an implementation/refactor Bead overlap in behavior surface but differ in purpose.

Context:
The migration is partially complete, so the tracker currently contains both spec-derived parity verification Beads and older implementation/refactor Beads. Some parity Beads will require code changes, others are verification-only, and some shared implementation Beads support multiple parity tasks. We need explicit cross-references so agents do not duplicate work or miss a dependency while the migration is in flight.

Required Direction:
- Add related links for overlapping behavior surfaces first.
- Keep parity Beads as the acceptance surface.
- Keep shared implementation/refactor Beads separate when they support multiple parity tasks.
- Do not merge or supersede unless the overlap is clearly same-purpose and one issue no longer adds independent value.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Existing overlapping parity and implementation/refactor Beads are identified
- [ ] Appropriate related links are added for same-behavior/different-purpose overlaps
- [ ] No merge/supersede is done unless overlap is truly redundant
- [ ] Follow-up prune candidates are noted where overlap may later be consolidated

- discovered-from: pt-477

{"description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,logging-flow,notes,backdate,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
Next.js index lacks the static tracker finish-session flow that collects session notes and optional backdate before saving the session.

Context:
Static parity baseline lives in public/index.html. The legacy tracker keeps a route-level currentSession, blocks finish when no sets are logged, opens notesModal from finishSession(), allows optional backdate defaulted to session start time via toggleBackdate(), applies the chosen backdate to the session and each set in saveSession(), enqueues the whole session for offline sync, updates local history immediately, then returns to the picker view. Current Next.js index route renders SessionLoggerModal, TimerPanel, and NextSetConfirmModal, but the route shell does not show an equivalent tracker-level finish-session notes/backdate step, and its current logging path appears to submit through the modal directly rather than finalizing a tracker-owned session object.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html, especially finishSession(), toggleBackdate(), and saveSession(). Preserve existing offline queue safeguards and do not simplify away the user-facing finish step without explicit approval. Keep any eventual implementation aligned with tracker UX flow, not just data persistence.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Next.js tracker blocks finish/save parity when no sets are logged
- [ ] Next.js tracker offers session notes and optional backdate before final save
- [ ] Backdate, when used, updates the saved session timestamp and associated set timestamps consistently
- [ ] Saved session returns the user to the tracker picker/history state with immediate local history/adherence refresh

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-bdf

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-spg.9) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation work; pt-spg.9 remains the final acceptance surface for session-finalization parity."}

{"description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,adherence,time,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-history.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js

Issue:
Next.js index adherence logic computes recency differently from static index, so `Done today` and day-bucket labels can be wrong.

Context:
Static index.html computes recency with getDaysDiff(), which normalizes both dates to local midnight before comparing calendar days. It then uses exact buckets: `0 days = Done today`, `1-3 days = green X day(s) ago`, `4-7 days = orange X days ago`, `8+ days = red X days ago`. Current Next.js lib/index-history.js instead computes `daysSince` as a rolling elapsed-hours floor using `(Date.now() - performed_at) / 24h`, then uses different threshold logic (`0-6 good`, `7-13 due`, `14+ overdue`) before mapping text. This can mislabel sessions around midnight and diverges from the original adherence semantics even when data is correct.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo(). Preserve the existing ExercisePicker-facing API shape if possible, but the underlying calculation and bucket boundaries must match static parity exactly. Treat local calendar date semantics as functional behavior, not display polish.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A session logged before local midnight and viewed after local midnight is not still labeled `Done today`
- [ ] `Done today` is based on local calendar date normalization, not rolling elapsed hours
- [ ] Recency buckets match static exactly: 0, 1-3, 4-7, 8+
- [ ] ExercisePicker badges and adherence text match static index for the same log timestamps

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-flq

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity beads (pt-eg2.10, pt-780.6) are verified or closed.
- This bead covers the concrete adherence bug; the linked parity beads remain the final acceptance surface for adherence and time semantics."}

{"description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity."}

{"description":"Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, sync, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js

Issue:
App-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced offline queue entries. The 'online' event listener only fires when the device comes back online mid-session, not on initial mount.

Context:
The offline write queue (pt_offline_queue_${userId}) correctly accumulates sessions when offline and syncs when the 'online' event fires. But if the app is opened fresh while already online with queued items, sync never triggers — entries stay stranded until the user manually refreshes or goes offline/online.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve idempotency — sync already uses client_mutation_id and treats 409 as success. Do not re-introduce /api/sync. Queue is user-scoped; must check userId before syncing.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
In the useEffect that registers the 'online' event listener, add a mount-time sync check:
  - After registering the listener, check if navigator.onLine === true AND queueRef.current.length > 0
  - If so, call sync() immediately
  - This runs once on mount (after queue loads from localStorage), catching stranded entries
Note: queue loads in a separate useEffect dependent on userId — mount-time sync must fire after that useEffect runs. Best approach: add a separate useEffect dependent on [queue, sync] that checks navigator.onLine on the first render once queue is populated.

Validation Checklist:
- Queue items present + app opened online → sync fires on mount, items cleared
- Queue empty + app opened online → no unnecessary sync call
- App opened offline → no sync attempted (navigator.onLine false)
- Online event mid-session still works as before

Dependencies: child of ptrebuild-nyn

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.3) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of mount-time sync/reconnect behavior; pt-780.3 remains the final acceptance surface for reconnect parity."}

2026-03-13: Codex fixed TimerPanel state reset/parity issues: reopening the same exercise now resets timer/counter state, hold mode tracks completed reps explicitly, and 'Use This Value' stays disabled until the timer/counter has actually recorded something. Local next build passes after the change.
2026-03-13: Production parity check on https://pttracker.app using the real patient account confirms 4d entry behavior. Plain reps exercises do NOT open SessionLoggerModal directly; they open the same in-panel exercise logger used for hold exercises. Example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened a panel with side selector, big tappable rep counter, Undo, Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises. Hold exercise 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls added (Rep 1 of 10, countdown, Start/Reset, same lower controls). Pocket Mode is part of this same 4d flow: it opens a full-screen overlay, shows countdown/meta text for timer exercises, and updates hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running. This means current Next.js behavior is still behind production parity: TimerPanel needs to be the entry path for both reps and hold/duration exercises, and Pocket Mode needs explicit overlay behavior rather than being omitted.
2026-03-13: Production check on https://pttracker.app with the real patient account confirmed that reps-only exercises and hold exercises both open the same in-panel logger. Hold example: 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls plus those lower controls. 2026-03-13: Next.js branch now routes all exercise taps into TimerPanel to match production panel-first behavior, and the timer state machine was trimmed back to the structure cap while preserving build-green behavior.
2026-03-13 parity review findings still open in 4d: (1) Pocket Mode does not yet reflect real session progress or side-aware progress; current Next.js panel/overlay still uses placeholder-style counts instead of session-backed left/right and sets-left state. (2) Pocket Mode partial-hold handling diverged from static semantics because Next.js set patches were carrying partial_rep=true; static logger keeps partial_rep false in the persisted set payload. {"description":"Create pt-rebuild/README.md as the canonical landing doc for the project. It must honestly document both structures that coexist in this folder: the legacy vanilla JS app and the in-progress Next.js migration.

## Sections to include

### What This Is
One paragraph: PT tracker app for physical therapy session logging. Two users (admin/therapist). Deployed on Vercel + Supabase.

### Two Structures in This Folder
Honest explanation: legacy vanilla JS pages live in public/ and api/; Next.js migration lives in pages/, components/, hooks/. Both are active. The migration is incremental (strangler fig) — old HTML pages are retired one at a time as Next.js versions are verified. Branch: nextjs. See docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md for migration status.

### Running Locally
npm run dev → http://localhost:3000

### Folder Structure
Annotated tree of pt-rebuild/ — what each top-level folder and key file is for. Cover: pages/, components/, hooks/, lib/, api/, public/, styles/, supabase/, docs/. Do NOT invent structure — read the actual filesystem.

### Shared Components (components/)
Registry pulled from the Shared Architecture section of docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md. For each component: file name, what it does, when to use it, basic usage. Components to document: NavMenu.js, AuthForm.js, NativeSelect.js, ExercisePicker.js, SessionLoggerModal.js (and any others found in components/).

### Shared Utilities (lib/)
Same treatment. Lib files to document: supabase.js, text-format.js, rehab-coverage.js, and any others found in lib/ that are Next.js-layer files. Do NOT document lib/auth.js or lib/db.js here — those are legacy API-layer files, note them separately as 'Legacy API layer (do not use in Next.js pages)'.

### Shared Hooks (hooks/)
Same treatment. useAuth.js, and any others in hooks/.

### Key Docs
Bullet list pointing to: docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md (migration status + decisions), docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md (authoring rules for new files), docs/AGENTS.md (workflow rules for agents), docs/BEADS_TEMPLATE.md (issue tracker template).

### Deployment
Vercel project: pt-rehab. Production: https://pttracker.app. Preview (nextjs branch): https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Supabase project: zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz.

## After writing README.md
- In docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md: replace the Shared Architecture section body with 'See pt-rebuild/README.md — Shared Components and Shared Utilities sections.' Keep the section heading so the doc structure is intact.
- Commit with a Beads ID in parentheses: (pt-t4l)

## Rules
- Document what EXISTS, not what is planned
- Do not copy planned/future sections from migration docs
- Read the actual filesystem before writing the folder structure section
- Keep it factual and navigable — this is a reference, not a tutorial","title":"Write pt-rebuild/README.md — honest codebase reference"}
