{"id":"pt-no3","title":"Auth sign-in modal flashes on app open and page navigation; fails when offline","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered during offline investigation, reported by user 2026-03-13)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium — affects all pages, every navigation
Scope: Static app confirmed. Next.js unverified — must check before implementing.
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: auth, offline, flash, ux, nextjs, migration, reliability
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js (Next.js), pt-rebuild/public/index.html (static)

Issue:
Two related auth problems (observed on static app; Next.js occurrence unverified):
1. (Online) Sign-in modal briefly flashes on app open and when navigating between pages, then disappears. Occurs even when the user is authenticated.
2. (Offline) Sign-in modal appears when app is opened offline and does not dismiss. Entering credentials shows 'Offline - no cached version available'.

IMPORTANT — Verification required before implementing:
User confirmed this was observed on the static app (public/index.html). It may not occur in the Next.js version. Before writing any code, verify on the Next.js preview (https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app):
  - Open app while signed in — does AuthForm flash?
  - Navigate between /rehab, /pt-view, /program — does AuthForm flash?
  - Open app offline — does AuthForm stay up and block access?
If none of these reproduce on Next.js, this is a static-only bug and should be moved to dev_notes.json (non-NextJS tracker).

Context:
Root cause (online flash, Next.js theory): useAuth.js onAuthStateChange unconditionally calls setSession(sess). If sess is momentarily null during a Supabase token refresh cycle and loading is already false, pages with !session && !authLoading show AuthForm briefly.

Root cause (offline, Next.js theory): supabase.auth.getSession() attempts to refresh an expired/near-expired token via network. When offline, this refresh fails, getSession() returns null session, loading is set to false, and all pages show AuthForm.

Supabase stores session in localStorage: sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token.

Constraints/Caveats:
- If confirmed on Next.js: fix useAuth.js only (no page-level changes needed).
- If static-only: move to dev_notes.json and out of Beads.
- Do not show AuthForm when offline and a stored session exists.
- Do not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword offline.

Specific Fix (if Next.js confirmed):
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Fix 1 (online flash): In onAuthStateChange, only set session to null on SIGNED_OUT:
  supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, sess) => {
      if (event === 'SIGNED_OUT') { setSession(null); }
      else if (sess) { setSession(sess); }
  });

Fix 2 (offline fallback): If getSession() returns null and !navigator.onLine, read stored session from localStorage key sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token and use it as fallback. Expose isOffline: boolean from hook.

Validation Checklist:
- Open app signed in → no AuthForm flash (online)
- Navigate between pages signed in → no AuthForm flash
- Open app offline (session stored) → tracker loads, no AuthForm
- Open app offline (never signed in) → AuthForm shown (correct)
- Sign out → AuthForm shown correctly
- Back online after offline session → fresh token loaded

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-zb3, child of ptrebuild-nyn","notes":"2026-03-13 source-only QA note: runtime verification is still required before implementation, but the Next.js auth flow does have the shape described in the bead. useAuth marks loading false immediately after getSession() resolves at hooks/useAuth.js:24-29, and onAuthStateChange unconditionally sets session to sess at hooks/useAuth.js:31-34. pages/index.js shows AuthForm whenever !session once authLoading is false at pages/index.js:180-183, and the same pattern exists on the other migrated pages. That makes the offline/static-session and null-session-flash theory plausible from source, but I have not verified reproduction on preview yet.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T05:24:20Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:06:21Z"}

{"id":"pt-9v1.8","title":"Build structured logger timer and cue state machine core","description":"Source Reference: architecture decision approved on 2026-03-17 after static cue trace and migration review
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,architecture,logging,timer,audio,speech,state-machine
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js (new)
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/components/PocketModeOverlay.js

Issue:
Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:
- Shared mechanism, explicit per-mode rules
- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode
- The machine must be able to express:
  - counter tap beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state
- Step 5: validate against the child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until linked child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1 that it is meant to satisfy are verified or closed
- This bead is the implementation core; the ptrebuild-9v1 subtree remains the acceptance surface

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] New machine file exists and owns timer/counter transition logic
- [ ] Hooks are adapters, not primary owners of cue business rules
- [ ] TimerPanel / PocketModeOverlay dispatch intents and render machine state rather than encoding cue rules inline
- [ ] Static cue differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode can be expressed without special-case sprawl across multiple files
- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T23:29:42Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T23:29:42Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers \u003e= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target \u003e 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts."}

{"notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers >= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target > 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts.
2026-03-17 comparison-speech decision: do not wire live comparison speech in the first machine implementation. Keep the architecture capable of adding it later, but leave it disabled/unhooked for now. Rationale: simple previous-set comparison creates misleading plus/minus swings across multi-set progressions (example progression: 15,15 -> 20,10 -> 25,5 is overall progress but naive per-set comparison reads as mixed improvement/regression). Preserve an extension point or clear code comment for future implementation rather than shipping misleading comparison speech now.
2026-03-17 future comparison-speech direction from user: if comparison speech is added later, prefer progress signals that do not punish multi-set redistribution. Candidate rules: (1) announce improvement when reps in any current set exceed the largest rep count from any comparable set in the last instance; (2) announce improvement/regression using total reps across all sets for the relevant worked side/context rather than naive immediate previous-set comparison. For sided exercises, totals should respect the side actually worked so right-only or left-only progress is not distorted.
2026-03-17 refined future comparison-speech model: for the relevant exercise and side context, compare current session against the comparable prior session using two metrics: (1) prior_best_set / current_best_set = max reps in any comparable set; (2) prior_total_reps / current_total_reps = sum of reps across all comparable sets. Suggested future speech priority: first, if current_best_set > prior_best_set, announce positive best-set improvement (for example, X more reps than your best set last time); second, if current_total_reps < prior_total_reps, announce negative total-volume regression (for example, Y fewer total reps than last time); third, optionally if current_total_reps > prior_total_reps and no stronger best-set cue fired, announce positive total-volume improvement. For sided exercises, compute and compare per side so right-only or left-only work is not mixed together."}

Counter increment/decrement, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, and pocket long-press partial cue decisions now route through lib/logger-timer-machine.js, with hooks acting as adapters and useTimerAudio executing named effect intents. {"id":"pt-9v1.8","title":"Build structured logger timer and cue state machine core","description":"Source Reference: architecture decision approved on 2026-03-17 after static cue trace and migration review
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium

Agent Owner: codex

Tags: nextjs,migration,architecture,logging,timer,audio,speech,state-machine

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js

- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/components/PocketModeOverlay.js

Issue:
Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:
- Shared mechanism, explicit per-mode rules
- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode
- The machine must be able to express:
  - counter tap beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state
- Step 5: validate against the child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until linked child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1 that it is meant to satisfy are verified or closed
- This bead is the implementation core; the ptrebuild-9v1 subtree remains the acceptance surface

- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it","acceptance_criteria":"Implementation must be expandable into a fuller logger execution architecture without replacement. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. {"id":"pt-t4l","title":"Write pt-rebuild/README.md — honest codebase reference","description":"Create pt-rebuild/README.md as the canonical landing doc for the project. It must honestly document both structures that coexist in this folder: the legacy vanilla JS app and the in-progress Next.js migration.

## Sections to include

### What This Is
One paragraph: PT tracker app for physical therapy session logging. Two users (admin/therapist). Deployed on Vercel + Supabase.

### Two Structures in This Folder
Honest explanation: legacy vanilla JS pages live in public/ and api/; Next.js migration lives in pages/, components/, hooks/. Both are active. The migration is incremental (strangler fig) — old HTML pages are retired one at a time as Next.js versions are verified. Branch: nextjs. See docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md for migration status.

### Running Locally
npm run dev → http://localhost:3000

### Folder Structure
Annotated tree of pt-rebuild/ — what each top-level folder and key file is for. Cover: pages/, components/, hooks/, lib/, api/, public/, styles/, supabase/, docs/. Do NOT invent structure — read the actual filesystem.

### Shared Components (components/)
Registry pulled from the Shared Architecture section of docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md. For each component: file name, what it does, when to use it, basic usage. Components to document: NavMenu.js, AuthForm.js, NativeSelect.js, ExercisePicker.js, SessionLoggerModal.js (and any others found in components/).

### Shared Utilities (lib/)
Same treatment. Lib files to document: supabase.js, text-format.js, rehab-coverage.js, and any others found in lib/ that are Next.js-layer files. Do NOT document lib/auth.js or lib/db.js here — those are legacy API-layer files, note them separately as 'Legacy API layer (do not use in Next.js pages)'.

### Shared Hooks (hooks/)
Same treatment. useAuth.js, and any others in hooks/.

### Key Docs
Bullet list pointing to: docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md (migration status + decisions), docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md (authoring rules for new files), docs/AGENTS.md (workflow rules for agents), docs/BEADS_TEMPLATE.md (issue tracker template).

### Deployment
Vercel project: pt-rehab. Production: https://pttracker.app. Preview (nextjs branch): https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Supabase project: zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz.

## After writing README.md
- In docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md: replace the Shared Architecture section body with 'See pt-rebuild/README.md — Shared Components and Shared Utilities sections.' Keep the section heading so the doc structure is intact.
- Commit with a Beads ID in parentheses: (pt-t4l)

## Rules
- Document what EXISTS, not what is planned
- Do not copy planned/future sections from migration docs
- Read the actual filesystem before writing the folder structure section
- Keep it factual and navigable — this is a reference, not a tutorial","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:24:20Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T22:32:55Z"}

{"notes":"2026-03-06: Implemented TimerPanel/useTimerSpeech and wired index exercise tap to open TimerPanel. Added countdown beeps (3/2/1), completion triple-beep, and speech cues (start/pause/milestones/set complete). Build passes locally. Commit: 46ef84c.
2026-03-13: Codex fixed TimerPanel state reset/parity issues: reopening the same exercise now resets timer/counter state, hold mode tracks completed reps explicitly, and 'Use This Value' stays disabled until the timer/counter has actually recorded something. Local next build passes after the change.
2026-03-13: Production parity check on https://pttracker.app using the real patient account confirms 4d entry behavior. Plain reps exercises do NOT open SessionLoggerModal directly; they open the same in-panel exercise logger used for hold exercises. Example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened a panel with side selector, big tappable rep counter, Undo, Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises. Hold exercise 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls added (Rep 1 of 10, countdown, Start/Reset, same lower controls). {"notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers >= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target > 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts.
2026-03-17 comparison-speech decision: do not wire live comparison speech in the first machine implementation. Keep the architecture capable of adding it later, but leave it disabled/unhooked for now. Rationale: simple previous-set comparison creates misleading plus/minus swings across multi-set progressions (example progression: 15,15 -> 20,10 -> 25,5 is overall progress but naive per-set comparison reads as mixed improvement/regression). Preserve an extension point or clear code comment for future implementation rather than shipping misleading comparison speech now.
2026-03-17 future comparison-speech direction from user: if comparison speech is added later, prefer progress signals that do not punish multi-set redistribution. Candidate rules: (1) announce improvement when reps in any current set exceed the largest rep count from any comparable set in the last instance; (2) announce improvement/regression using total reps across all sets for the relevant worked side/context rather than naive immediate previous-set comparison. For sided exercises, totals should respect the side actually worked so right-only or left-only progress is not distorted.
2026-03-17 refined future comparison-speech model: for the relevant exercise and side context, compare current session against the comparable prior session using two metrics: (1) prior_best_set / current_best_set = max reps in any comparable set; (2) prior_total_reps / current_total_reps = sum of reps across all comparable sets. Suggested future speech priority: first, if current_best_set > prior_best_set, announce positive best-set improvement (for example, X more reps than your best set last time); second, if current_total_reps < prior_total_reps, announce negative total-volume regression (for example, Y fewer total reps than last time); third, optionally if current_total_reps > prior_total_reps and no stronger best-set cue fired, announce positive total-volume improvement. For sided exercises, compute and compare per side so right-only or left-only work is not mixed together.
2026-03-17 implementation progress: machine core is now active in code. Counter increment/decrement, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, and pocket long-press partial cue decisions now route through lib/logger-timer-machine.js, with hooks acting as adapters and useTimerAudio executing named effect intents. Next implementation step is to align machine behavior with newer product decisions from this session (start-confirmation beep >=5s, pause speech >5s or Pocket Mode, richer duration completion speech when the machine has enough session context).
2026-03-17 implementation progress: start/pause/reset/tick/partial timer paths are now routed through lib/logger-timer-machine.js via useExerciseTimer adapter. Pocket Mode now syncs into the machine state so pause behavior can depend on overlay context. useTimerAudio executes named machine effects, including the new start-confirmation beep effect. useTimerSpeech now passes sessionProgress into the adapter layer so duration completion speech can be upgraded from plain Set complete to set X of Y complete (including side when relevant). next build passes after these changes.
2026-03-17 milestone: machine behavior now reflects the newer session decisions instead of the older static defaults in key places. Start no longer speaks; it issues a confirmation beep for timers >= 5s. Pause speech now follows the updated rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Pocket Mode open/close state is fed into the machine through TimerPanel so overlay context can influence cue behavior. Duration completion speech is now shaped in the adapter layer from sessionProgress context rather than hard-coded to plain Set complete."}

Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:

- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode

  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state

- [ ] TimerPanel / PocketModeOverlay dispatch intents and render machine state rather than encoding cue rules inline
- [ ] Static cue differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode can be expressed without special-case sprawl across multiple files
- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it","acceptance_criteria":"Implementation must be expandable into a fuller logger execution architecture without replacement. Issue:
Match static hold-timer completion behavior exactly. Every timed rep completion plays the triple completion beep, advances rep state, and then speaks milestone phrases based on repsLeft after the timed rep completes.

Static Source Anchors:
- initTimerMode(): public/index.html around lines 2692-2718
- startTimer(): public/index.html around lines 2775-2803
- playCompletionSound(): public/index.html around lines 2894-2903

Static Rules:
- Hold mode uses currentExercise.seconds_per_rep and currentExercise.current_reps
- When remaining hits zero, play completion triple beep
- Then compute repsLeft = totalReps - currentRep
- Speak Set complete when repsLeft <= 0
- Speak Last rep when repsLeft === 1
- Speak 3 reps left when repsLeft === 3
- Speak 5 reps left when repsLeft === 5
- Advance currentRep and reset display to full target duration for the next rep

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the static moment at which Last rep is spoken, even if it differs from counter mode interaction timing
- Do not close until linked parity beads ptrebuild-spg.3 and ptrebuild-spg.10 are verified or closed

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Triple completion beep fires on every hold-rep timer completion
- [ ] Hold milestone speech uses repsLeft 5/3/1/0 after timed rep completion
- [ ] Last rep timing matches static hold behavior exactly
- [ ] Timer resets to full target duration and advances to the next rep without appending an accepted set","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:52:08Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T22:52:08Z"}

Match static Pocket Mode audio behavior exactly. Pocket Mode inherits the normal counter/timer cues from the active logger session and adds one unique cue: a distinct partial-rep confirmation beep after a 700ms long press in timer mode. It does not add pocket enter/exit speech.

Issue:
Verify that timer mode counts down from target, hold mode advances reps within a set, duration mode is single-run, and the five-part surface is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Timer mode must count down (not up) from the configured target. Hold exercises: each completed countdown rep advances live rep progress, resets the timer display to full target duration, auto-pauses on completion, and defers set acceptance until the configured rep count is satisfied or the user manually logs. Duration exercises: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds — single-run semantics, not multi-rep. The five-part timer surface must include: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, and reset button. Reset clears live timer progress without deleting accepted sets.

3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER

- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN

- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Pocket Mode is part of this same 4d flow: it opens a full-screen overlay, shows countdown/meta text for timer exercises, and updates hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running. This means current Next.js behavior is still behind production parity: TimerPanel needs to be the entry path for both reps and hold/duration exercises, and Pocket Mode needs explicit overlay behavior rather than being omitted.
2026-03-13: Production check on https://pttracker.app with the real patient account confirmed that reps-only exercises and hold exercises both open the same in-panel logger. 2026-03-13: Next.js branch now routes all exercise taps into TimerPanel to match production panel-first behavior, and the timer state machine was trimmed back to the structure cap while preserving build-green behavior.
2026-03-13 parity review findings still open in 4d: (1) Pocket Mode does not yet reflect real session progress or side-aware progress; current Next.js panel/overlay still uses placeholder-style counts instead of session-backed left/right and sets-left state. (2) Pocket Mode partial-hold handling diverged from static semantics because Next.js set patches were carrying partial_rep=true; static logger keeps partial_rep false in the persisted set payload. 2026-03-13: Closed child ptrebuild-f1j after preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Verified Pocket Mode now reflects session-backed progress, reps and hold exercises both stay panel-first, and a captured hold payload after Pocket Mode long-press logging showed manual_log=false and partial_rep=false. ptrebuild-ryf remains open pending broader audio/speech parity confidence, but the panel/pocket parity slice is complete.
2026-03-17 active refactor progress: logger timer/cue behavior is being moved into the new expandable machine core at pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js. Counter cues, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, pocket partial cue, Pocket Mode context, and duration-completion speech shaping are now routing through the machine/adapters rather than staying as scattered inline rules. next build passes after the current slice; ptrebuild-ryf still stays open pending parity verification and any remaining cue-alignment cleanup.

{"notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers >= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target > 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts.
2026-03-17 comparison-speech decision: do not wire live comparison speech in the first machine implementation. Keep the architecture capable of adding it later, but leave it disabled/unhooked for now. Rationale: simple previous-set comparison creates misleading plus/minus swings across multi-set progressions (example progression: 15,15 -> 20,10 -> 25,5 is overall progress but naive per-set comparison reads as mixed improvement/regression). Preserve an extension point or clear code comment for future implementation rather than shipping misleading comparison speech now.
2026-03-17 future comparison-speech direction from user: if comparison speech is added later, prefer progress signals that do not punish multi-set redistribution. Candidate rules: (1) announce improvement when reps in any current set exceed the largest rep count from any comparable set in the last instance; (2) announce improvement/regression using total reps across all sets for the relevant worked side/context rather than naive immediate previous-set comparison. For sided exercises, totals should respect the side actually worked so right-only or left-only progress is not distorted.
2026-03-17 refined future comparison-speech model: for the relevant exercise and side context, compare current session against the comparable prior session using two metrics: (1) prior_best_set / current_best_set = max reps in any comparable set; (2) prior_total_reps / current_total_reps = sum of reps across all comparable sets. Suggested future speech priority: first, if current_best_set > prior_best_set, announce positive best-set improvement (for example, X more reps than your best set last time); second, if current_total_reps < prior_total_reps, announce negative total-volume regression (for example, Y fewer total reps than last time); third, optionally if current_total_reps > prior_total_reps and no stronger best-set cue fired, announce positive total-volume improvement. For sided exercises, compute and compare per side so right-only or left-only work is not mixed together.
2026-03-17 implementation progress: machine core is now active in code. Counter increment/decrement, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, and pocket long-press partial cue decisions now route through lib/logger-timer-machine.js, with hooks acting as adapters and useTimerAudio executing named effect intents. Next implementation step is to align machine behavior with newer product decisions from this session (start-confirmation beep >=5s, pause speech >5s or Pocket Mode, richer duration completion speech when the machine has enough session context).
2026-03-17 implementation progress: start/pause/reset/tick/partial timer paths are now routed through lib/logger-timer-machine.js via useExerciseTimer adapter. Pocket Mode now syncs into the machine state so pause behavior can depend on overlay context. useTimerAudio executes named machine effects, including the new start-confirmation beep effect. useTimerSpeech now passes sessionProgress into the adapter layer so duration completion speech can be upgraded from plain Set complete to set X of Y complete (including side when relevant). next build passes after these changes.
2026-03-17 milestone: machine behavior now reflects the newer session decisions instead of the older static defaults in key places. Start no longer speaks; it issues a confirmation beep for timers >= 5s. Pause speech now follows the updated rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Pocket Mode open/close state is fed into the machine through TimerPanel so overlay context can influence cue behavior. Duration completion speech is now shaped in the adapter layer from sessionProgress context rather than hard-coded to plain Set complete.
2026-03-18 machine smoke check via direct Node import of lib/logger-timer-machine.js: counter mode emitted play_soft_tick on every increment, with speech at tap 5 (5 reps left), tap 7 (3 reps left), tap 9 (Last rep), and completion triple-beep + Set complete at tap 10 for a 10-rep target. Hold mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 10-second hold timer, countdown warnings at remaining 3/2/1, and completion triple-beep at zero for the first timed rep of a 3-rep hold set (no milestone speech yet because repsLeft became 2). Duration mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 12-second timer, countdown warning at remaining 3, and completion triple-beep + raw Set complete at zero. Note: duration completion wording is then upgraded in the adapter layer from raw Set complete to the richer set X of Y complete form when sessionProgress context is available.
2026-03-18 implementation progress: wired delayed comparison speech into the Next Set confirm flow using the approved future model. Added pure helper lib/logger-progress-comparison.js plus sessionStartedAt tracking in usePanelSessionProgress so current-session saves do not compare against logs created after the panel opened. Index page now schedules delayed speech after successful submitSeedSet with side-aware session metrics and prior-session cutoff."}

{"id":"pt-9v1.8","title":"Build structured logger timer and cue state machine core","description":"Source Reference: architecture decision approved on 2026-03-17 after static cue trace and migration review
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,architecture,logging,timer,audio,speech,state-machine
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js (new)
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/components/PocketModeOverlay.js

Issue:
Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:
- Shared mechanism, explicit per-mode rules
- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode
- The machine must be able to express:
  - counter tap beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state
- Step 5: validate against the child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until linked child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1 that it is meant to satisfy are verified or closed
- This bead is the implementation core; the ptrebuild-9v1 subtree remains the acceptance surface

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] New machine file exists and owns timer/counter transition logic
- [ ] Hooks are adapters, not primary owners of cue business rules
- [ ] TimerPanel / PocketModeOverlay dispatch intents and render machine state rather than encoding cue rules inline
- [ ] Static cue differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode can be expressed without special-case sprawl across multiple files
- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it","acceptance_criteria":"Implementation must be expandable into a fuller logger execution architecture without replacement. The core introduced here must be directly incorporable into a future broader state-machine or event-routed logger domain; future work may extend it, but must not require throwing it away and rebuilding from scratch.","notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Status: open

- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
Match static progress-comparison speech behavior after set confirmation. Static logger speaks delayed comparison feedback such as X more reps than last time or X fewer reps than last time based on the most recent comparable prior set.

Static Source Anchors:
- announceProgressComparison(): public/index.html around lines 3479-3509
- call site after set confirmation: public/index.html around lines 3455-3460

Static Rules:
- Comparison speech is delayed after set confirmation rather than spoken during live timer/counting interaction
- Positive diff uses ${diff} more rep(s) than last time
- Negative diff uses ${Math.abs(diff)} fewer reps than last time
- Negative comparison only speaks when the drop is greater than 2 reps

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve static delayed timing if this behavior is implemented in Next.js
- If Next.js intentionally omits comparison speech, the omission must be an explicit product choice rather than accidental loss
- Do not close until linked parity bead ptrebuild-spg.10 is verified or closed

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Positive comparison speech matches static wording and timing
- [ ] Negative comparison speech matches static wording and threshold
- [ ] Comparison feedback is tied to set confirmation, not live counter/timer updates

{"notes":"2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers >= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target > 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts.
2026-03-17 comparison-speech decision: do not wire live comparison speech in the first machine implementation. Keep the architecture capable of adding it later, but leave it disabled/unhooked for now. Rationale: simple previous-set comparison creates misleading plus/minus swings across multi-set progressions (example progression: 15,15 -> 20,10 -> 25,5 is overall progress but naive per-set comparison reads as mixed improvement/regression). Preserve an extension point or clear code comment for future implementation rather than shipping misleading comparison speech now.
2026-03-17 future comparison-speech direction from user: if comparison speech is added later, prefer progress signals that do not punish multi-set redistribution. Candidate rules: (1) announce improvement when reps in any current set exceed the largest rep count from any comparable set in the last instance; (2) announce improvement/regression using total reps across all sets for the relevant worked side/context rather than naive immediate previous-set comparison. For sided exercises, totals should respect the side actually worked so right-only or left-only progress is not distorted.
2026-03-17 refined future comparison-speech model: for the relevant exercise and side context, compare current session against the comparable prior session using two metrics: (1) prior_best_set / current_best_set = max reps in any comparable set; (2) prior_total_reps / current_total_reps = sum of reps across all comparable sets. Suggested future speech priority: first, if current_best_set > prior_best_set, announce positive best-set improvement (for example, X more reps than your best set last time); second, if current_total_reps < prior_total_reps, announce negative total-volume regression (for example, Y fewer total reps than last time); third, optionally if current_total_reps > prior_total_reps and no stronger best-set cue fired, announce positive total-volume improvement. For sided exercises, compute and compare per side so right-only or left-only work is not mixed together.
2026-03-17 implementation progress: machine core is now active in code. Counter increment/decrement, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, and pocket long-press partial cue decisions now route through lib/logger-timer-machine.js, with hooks acting as adapters and useTimerAudio executing named effect intents. Next implementation step is to align machine behavior with newer product decisions from this session (start-confirmation beep >=5s, pause speech >5s or Pocket Mode, richer duration completion speech when the machine has enough session context).
2026-03-17 implementation progress: start/pause/reset/tick/partial timer paths are now routed through lib/logger-timer-machine.js via useExerciseTimer adapter. Pocket Mode now syncs into the machine state so pause behavior can depend on overlay context. useTimerAudio executes named machine effects, including the new start-confirmation beep effect. useTimerSpeech now passes sessionProgress into the adapter layer so duration completion speech can be upgraded from plain Set complete to set X of Y complete (including side when relevant). next build passes after these changes.
2026-03-17 milestone: machine behavior now reflects the newer session decisions instead of the older static defaults in key places. Start no longer speaks; it issues a confirmation beep for timers >= 5s. Pause speech now follows the updated rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Pocket Mode open/close state is fed into the machine through TimerPanel so overlay context can influence cue behavior. Duration completion speech is now shaped in the adapter layer from sessionProgress context rather than hard-coded to plain Set complete.
2026-03-18 machine smoke check via direct Node import of lib/logger-timer-machine.js: counter mode emitted play_soft_tick on every increment, with speech at tap 5 (5 reps left), tap 7 (3 reps left), tap 9 (Last rep), and completion triple-beep + Set complete at tap 10 for a 10-rep target. Hold mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 10-second hold timer, countdown warnings at remaining 3/2/1, and completion triple-beep at zero for the first timed rep of a 3-rep hold set (no milestone speech yet because repsLeft became 2). Duration mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 12-second timer, countdown warning at remaining 3, and completion triple-beep + raw Set complete at zero. Note: duration completion wording is then upgraded in the adapter layer from raw Set complete to the richer set X of Y complete form when sessionProgress context is available.
2026-03-18 implementation progress: wired delayed comparison speech into the Next Set confirm flow using the approved future model. Added pure helper lib/logger-progress-comparison.js plus sessionStartedAt tracking in usePanelSessionProgress so current-session saves do not compare against logs created after the panel opened. Index page now schedules delayed speech after successful submitSeedSet with side-aware session metrics and prior-session cutoff.
2026-03-18 restart handoff: structured timer/cue machine is active in code. Current files in play: pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js, pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js. Start uses confirm beep for timers >=5s and does not speak Start. Pause speech follows the product rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Duration completion speech is upgraded from raw Set complete to set X of Y complete, including side when relevant. Comparison speech is now wired after successful Next Set confirmation using lib/logger-progress-comparison.js plus sessionStartedAt tracking in usePanelSessionProgress so current-session saves do not compare against logs created after the panel opened. Remaining work before closure: rerun next build after the last sessionStartedAt reset change, verify runtime behavior against the pt-9v1 child beads, and commit the refactor."}

Issue:
Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:
- Shared mechanism, explicit per-mode rules
- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode
- The machine must be able to express:
  - counter tap beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state
- Step 5: validate against the child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until linked child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1 that it is meant to satisfy are verified or closed
- This bead is the implementation core; the ptrebuild-9v1 subtree remains the acceptance surface

- [ ] New machine file exists and owns timer/counter transition logic

- [ ] TimerPanel / PocketModeOverlay dispatch intents and render machine state rather than encoding cue rules inline
- [ ] Static cue differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode can be expressed without special-case sprawl across multiple files
- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it","acceptance_criteria":"Implementation must be expandable into a fuller logger execution architecture without replacement. {"id":"pt-ant","title":"Standardize iPhone-safe dropdown/select behavior across Next.js pages","description":"Source Reference: user-confirmed iPhone bug on 2026-03-17, initially reported on `/program`
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ios,ui,editor,dropdown,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCore.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCues.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/styles/globals.css

Issue:
Dropdown/select behavior across the Next.js app must be consolidated into a shared reusable UI implementation, not re-created page by page. The immediate confirmed bug is on `/program`, but the correct fix is to extract a shared iPhone-safe select component/module and then adopt it on each page that uses dropdowns.

Context:
User confirmed the bug on a real iPhone. Code review confirms the Next.js app currently uses multiple native `<select>` and datalist/select patterns across `/program`, `/index`, and `/pt-view`. `/index` already contains the most proven dropdown behavior because its form-parameter flow supports both standard native selects and an `Other...` path that reveals a text input. That working behavior should become the source for a shared reusable implementation rather than being copied manually into each page.

Required Architecture Direction:
- Implement a shared reusable select UI module/component in `components/` rather than repeating select markup/CSS/behavior per page.
- The shared implementation must support both:
  - plain native select fields
  - native select fields with an optional `Other...` mode that can be enabled or disabled per usage
- `Other...` must be configurable, not always on. Call sites need a clear prop/option to allow or disallow the fallback text-entry path.
- Keep the implementation native-select-first for iPhone/Safari compatibility.
- Do not treat this as a pure `lib/` extraction only; the reuse must cover the JSX/CSS/interaction layer, not just helper functions.

Observed Implementation Notes:
- `/program` uses native selects in the page-level selector and throughout the exercise editor form.
- `/index` uses native select behavior in `SessionLoggerModal` and already supports `Other...` fallback for form parameters.
- `/pt-view` uses a simpler native select for exercise filtering.
- Current select behavior is distributed across multiple components and CSS modules instead of one shared component/module.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve iOS-safe tap behavior for buttons while fixing native select usability. Do not replace native selects with a custom dropdown unless necessary. Verify on actual iPhone/Safari after fixes.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A shared reusable select component/module exists in `components/`
- [ ] Shared implementation allows each call site to enable or disable `Other...`
- [ ] `/program` selectors use the shared implementation and work on iPhone
- [ ] `/index` keeps its working dropdown behavior while being routed through the shared implementation
- [ ] `/pt-view` filter select is aligned to the same shared implementation
- [ ] No regression for desktop or Android/native browser select behavior

Dependencies:
- none

{"id":"pt-zb3","title":"Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity.","notes":"2026-03-13 QA evidence: confirmed from source. hooks/useIndexData.js has no read-side cache path at all: reload() only does Promise.all network fetches at hooks/useIndexData.js:12-24, then sets error on failure at hooks/useIndexData.js:25-27. When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. Sign-out clears only the offline queue via clearQueue() in pages/index.js:174-177; there is currently no corresponding cached-read data lifecycle. This bead is accurate as written.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T05:22:38Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:34Z"}

{"id":"pt-41a","title":"Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html for offline cold-open","description":"Source Reference: discovered during offline cache root cause analysis 2026-03-13
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: Next.js PWA service worker (Phase 4h)
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, pwa, service-worker, cache, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/public/sw.js (new Next.js sw, not static app sw.js)

Issue:
The Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html. If it pre-caches /index.html (as static app does), offline cold-open fails — browser navigates to / and caches.match() finds no entry. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' and no JS runs — IndexedDB read cache never reached.

Root Cause (confirmed in static app 2026-03-13):
Static sw.js STATIC_ASSETS includes '/index.html'. Browser navigate requests are for the root URL '/'. Different cache keys. network-first cache.put() stores root URL dynamically but does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Pre-cache '/' in STATIC_ASSETS, not '/index.html'
- Navigate fallback handler must try caches.match('/') before returning offline error
- Do NOT repeat the /index.html pattern from static app sw.js
- This must be resolved before ptrebuild-zb3 (IndexedDB read cache) can work — if page does not load, cache code never runs

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online, close, airplane mode, reopen: app shell loads from SW cache
- Exercises load from IndexedDB (ptrebuild-zb3)
- No 'Offline - no cached version available' on cold-open after prior online visit

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-zb3, child of ptrebuild-v3l, blocks ptrebuild-zb3","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:13:31Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:06:20Z"}

{"id":"pt-flq.1","title":"Next.js index adherence uses wrong 'today' and recency bucket rules","description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,adherence,time,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-history.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js

Issue:
Next.js index adherence logic computes recency differently from static index, so `Done today` and day-bucket labels can be wrong.

Context:
Static index.html computes recency with getDaysDiff(), which normalizes both dates to local midnight before comparing calendar days. It then uses exact buckets: `0 days = Done today`, `1-3 days = green X day(s) ago`, `4-7 days = orange X days ago`, `8+ days = red X days ago`. Current Next.js lib/index-history.js instead computes `daysSince` as a rolling elapsed-hours floor using `(Date.now() - performed_at) / 24h`, then uses different threshold logic (`0-6 good`, `7-13 due`, `14+ overdue`) before mapping text. This can mislabel sessions around midnight and diverges from the original adherence semantics even when data is correct.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo(). Preserve the existing ExercisePicker-facing API shape if possible, but the underlying calculation and bucket boundaries must match static parity exactly. Treat local calendar date semantics as functional behavior, not display polish.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A session logged before local midnight and viewed after local midnight is not still labeled `Done today`
- [ ] `Done today` is based on local calendar date normalization, not rolling elapsed hours
- [ ] Recency buckets match static exactly: 0, 1-3, 4-7, 8+
- [ ] ExercisePicker badges and adherence text match static index for the same log timestamps

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-flq

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity beads (pt-eg2.10, pt-780.6) are verified or closed.
- This bead covers the concrete adherence bug; the linked parity beads remain the final acceptance surface for adherence and time semantics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:57:31Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:33Z"}

Context:

The offline queue is stored under localStorage key pt_offline_queue (user-scoped: pt_offline_queue_${userId}). Each queued session includes the field client_mutation_id set to a stable identity derived from the session id. When syncOfflineQueue() posts a queued session and receives a 409 duplicate response, the session must be removed from the queue and treated as sync success — not left stuck. Queue is cleared item by item on sync success or duplicate-safe handling.

finishSession() blocks with "Please log at least one set before finishing" until at least one accepted set exists. The notes modal is opened (not direct save). Backdate warning fires when the chosen datetime differs from session start by more than two minutes; warning copy is "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." close-notes-modal returns to the active logger without discarding the session. cancel-session asks for confirmation before abandoning. Confirmed discard clears both currentSession and currentExercise. Final save: trim notes → apply backdate (rewrites session timestamp and each set's performed_at) → push to queue → update sync badge → attempt network sync → prepend to local history → clear tracker state → return to picker → re-render adherence → show "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)".

{"id":"pt-eg2.3","title":"Verify authenticated bootstrap order","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-shell
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Issue:
Verify that authenticated bootstrap executes in the exact static-defined order from /api/env through interaction binding.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this startup sequence for an existing authenticated session: load runtime env from /api/env, initialize offline support and install connectivity listeners, restore offline queue state, inspect auth session and set currentUser/authToken/refreshToken, hide auth UI, propagate auth to pt_editor.html, run loadData(), perform first message check, start 30-second polling, and only then bind interactive handlers. Interactive handler and hamburger wiring must happen after the authenticated bootstrap path has established state and queue visibility, not as an early unconstrained concern.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] /api/env is fetched before any authenticated data bootstrap begins
- [ ] Offline manager support is initialized and connectivity listeners installed before loadData() runs
- [ ] Offline queue state is restored before interactive handlers are bound
- [ ] currentUser, authToken, and refreshToken are set from session before auth UI is hidden
- [ ] Auth UI is hidden before loadData() is called
- [ ] pt_editor.html auth propagation occurs at startup and on auth state change
- [ ] First message check is performed immediately after loadData() completes
- [ ] 30-second message polling starts after bootstrap, not before
- [ ] Interactive handlers and hamburger wiring are bound after queue restore and first-pass shell setup

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-bdf.1","title":"Next.js index missing finish-session notes/backdate/save flow from static tracker","description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,logging-flow,notes,backdate,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
Next.js index lacks the static tracker finish-session flow that collects session notes and optional backdate before saving the session.

Context:
Static parity baseline lives in public/index.html. The legacy tracker keeps a route-level currentSession, blocks finish when no sets are logged, opens notesModal from finishSession(), allows optional backdate defaulted to session start time via toggleBackdate(), applies the chosen backdate to the session and each set in saveSession(), enqueues the whole session for offline sync, updates local history immediately, then returns to the picker view. Current Next.js index route renders SessionLoggerModal, TimerPanel, and NextSetConfirmModal, but the route shell does not show an equivalent tracker-level finish-session notes/backdate step, and its current logging path appears to submit through the modal directly rather than finalizing a tracker-owned session object.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html, especially finishSession(), toggleBackdate(), and saveSession(). Preserve existing offline queue safeguards and do not simplify away the user-facing finish step without explicit approval. Keep any eventual implementation aligned with tracker UX flow, not just data persistence.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Next.js tracker blocks finish/save parity when no sets are logged
- [ ] Next.js tracker offers session notes and optional backdate before final save
- [ ] Backdate, when used, updates the saved session timestamp and associated set timestamps consistently
- [ ] Saved session returns the user to the tracker picker/history state with immediate local history/adherence refresh

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-bdf

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-spg.9) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation work; pt-spg.9 remains the final acceptance surface for session-finalization parity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:53:08Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:31Z"}
