{"id":"pt-6vs","title":"Review index.js size cap: determine if exemption is warranted","description":"Source Reference: structure audit 2026-03-18
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,structure,index
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
pages/index.js is at 494 lines — 6 lines under the 500 hard cap. index.html was the largest file in the pre-transition codebase. Before any further growth triggers a forced split, both agents should agree on whether the page genuinely requires cap exemption or whether a clean split exists.

Context:
The structure rules allow exemption if both agents agree the file cannot be cleanly split and the hard cap is unavoidable given the domain. index.js is the main tracker shell — it orchestrates auth, data, offline queue, logging, timer, history, and session finalization. Each concern is already extracted to hooks/components. What remains is orchestration glue. If that glue is genuinely one concern (orchestration), an exemption may be valid. If sub-orchestrators can be extracted (e.g. a useTrackerSession hook), a split is possible.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not split artificially just to hit a number
- Do not grant exemption without both agents reviewing
- If exemption granted, document the reason in this issue and update NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Both agents have reviewed the current index.js shape
- [ ] Agreement reached: exemption or split plan
- [ ] If exemption: NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md updated with documented reason
- [ ] If split: clean domain boundary identified before any code is written","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T19:36:42Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-18T19:36:42Z"}

{"id":"pt-eg2.3","title":"Verify authenticated bootstrap order","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P1

Agent Owner: codex

File Scope:

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Issue:
Verify that authenticated bootstrap executes in the exact static-defined order from /api/env through interaction binding.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this startup sequence for an existing authenticated session: load runtime env from /api/env, initialize offline support and install connectivity listeners, restore offline queue state, inspect auth session and set currentUser/authToken/refreshToken, hide auth UI, propagate auth to pt_editor.html, run loadData(), perform first message check, start 30-second polling, and only then bind interactive handlers. Interactive handler and hamburger wiring must happen after the authenticated bootstrap path has established state and queue visibility, not as an early unconstrained concern.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] /api/env is fetched before any authenticated data bootstrap begins
- [ ] Offline manager support is initialized and connectivity listeners installed before loadData() runs
- [ ] Offline queue state is restored before interactive handlers are bound
- [ ] currentUser, authToken, and refreshToken are set from session before auth UI is hidden
- [ ] Auth UI is hidden before loadData() is called
- [ ] pt_editor.html auth propagation occurs at startup and on auth state change
- [ ] First message check is performed immediately after loadData() completes
- [ ] 30-second message polling starts after bootstrap, not before
- [ ] Interactive handlers and hamburger wiring are bound after queue restore and first-pass shell setup

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-6vs","title":"Review index.js size cap: determine if exemption is warranted","description":"Source Reference: structure audit 2026-03-18
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,structure,index
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
pages/index.js is at 494 lines — 6 lines under the 500 hard cap. index.html was the largest file in the pre-transition codebase. Before any further growth triggers a forced split, both agents should agree on whether the page genuinely requires cap exemption or whether a clean split exists.

Context:
The structure rules allow exemption if both agents agree the file cannot be cleanly split and the hard cap is unavoidable given the domain. index.js is the main tracker shell — it orchestrates auth, data, offline queue, logging, timer, history, and session finalization. Each concern is already extracted to hooks/components. What remains is orchestration glue. If that glue is genuinely one concern (orchestration), an exemption may be valid. If sub-orchestrators can be extracted (e.g. a useTrackerSession hook), a split is possible.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not split artificially just to hit a number
- Do not grant exemption without both agents reviewing
- If exemption granted, document the reason in this issue and update NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Both agents have reviewed the current index.js shape
- [ ] Agreement reached: exemption or split plan
- [ ] If exemption: NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md updated with documented reason
- [ ] If split: clean domain boundary identified before any code is written","notes":"2026-03-18 update: index.js is now at 621 lines after useMessages wiring (pt-oyv, commit 3c4dc18). Extraction 1 — hooks/useManualLog.js (~80 lines):

- Inputs (args): draftSession, selectedExercise, setDraftSession, setIsTimerOpen, showSaveSuccess, maybeAnnounceAllSetsComplete, setPanelResetToken
- Returns: manualLogState and all handlers above

Extraction 2 — hooks/useTrackerSession.js (~150 lines):
- Owns: draftSession, isTimerOpen, panelResetToken, pendingSetPatch, backdateEnabled, backdateValue, sessionStartedAt, abandonDraftSession, handleExerciseSelect, handleFinishSession, handleSaveFinishedSession, handleTimerApplySet, handleTimerOpenManual, handleConfirmNextSet, handleEditNextSet, handleNotesModalClose, handleCancelSession, handleToggleBackdate
- Inputs (args): token, userId, pickerExercises, allLogs, enqueue, sync, reload, showSaveSuccess, speakText, maybeAnnounceAllSetsComplete
- Returns: all state and handlers above

Extraction 3 — hooks/useIndexMessages.js (~50 lines):
- Owns: isMessagesOpen, recipientId, emailEnabled, currentDbId, fetchUsers useEffect, handleEmailToggle
- Inputs (args): token, session
- Returns: all state + handlers + openMessages action
- Domain: messages state and email prefs for the tracker page

Extraction 4 — hooks/usePickerData.js (~35 lines):
- Owns: pickerExercises memo, pickerPrograms memo, historicalFormParams memo
- Inputs (args): exercises, programs, allLogs
- Returns: pickerExercises, pickerPrograms, historicalFormParams
- Domain: data shaping for the exercise picker

Constraints/Caveats:
- Extract only — do not change behavior
- pages/index.js wires all four hooks in; render and prop passing unchanged
- Add JSDoc to all new hook files
- Update README Shared Hooks section with all four entries
- npm run build must pass

Issue:
Verify that authenticated bootstrap executes in the exact static-defined order from /api/env through interaction binding.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this startup sequence for an existing authenticated session: load runtime env from /api/env, initialize offline support and install connectivity listeners, restore offline queue state, inspect auth session and set currentUser/authToken/refreshToken, hide auth UI, propagate auth to pt_editor.html, run loadData(), perform first message check, start 30-second polling, and only then bind interactive handlers. Interactive handler and hamburger wiring must happen after the authenticated bootstrap path has established state and queue visibility, not as an early unconstrained concern.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this startup sequence for an existing authenticated session: load runtime env from /api/env, initialize offline support and install connectivity listeners, restore offline queue state, inspect auth session and set currentUser/authToken/refreshToken, hide auth UI, propagate auth to pt_editor.html, run loadData(), perform first message check, start 30-second polling, and only then bind interactive handlers. Interactive handler and hamburger wiring must happen after the authenticated bootstrap path has established state and queue visibility, not as an early unconstrained concern.

{"id":"pt-rb8","title":"Fix immediate post-save sync missing newly queued tracker sessions","description":"Source Reference: user report 2026-03-19
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,offline,sync,reliability,queue
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
A newly saved tracker session can remain unsynced until the user manually triggers Sync, because the immediate post-save sync attempt can run before the newly enqueued queue item is visible to the sync path.

Context:
User report on 2026-03-19: after logging an exercise, the session did not sync until manual Sync was triggered from the hamburger menu.
Current code path review:
- hooks/useTrackerSession.js enqueues the payload, then immediately calls sync()
- hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js sync() reads queueRef.current
- queueRef.current can still reflect the pre-enqueue queue on the same tick, so the just-added session may be skipped by that immediate sync attempt
This appears distinct from the already tracked mount-time auto-sync-on-open issue and from the existing sync-toast/copy parity bead.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve queue-first save semantics
- Do not regress duplicate-safe 409 handling
- Do not require the user to trigger manual Sync for a newly saved online session
- Keep user-scoped queue behavior intact

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Immediately after queueing a newly saved session while online, the sync path includes that newly queued item
- [ ] Newly saved online sessions do not remain stranded until manual Sync
- [ ] Queue-first save order is preserved (enqueue before network attempt)
- [ ] 409 duplicate responses still drain the queued item successfully
- [ ] User-scoped queue storage behavior remains intact

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
A newly saved tracker session can remain unsynced until the user manually triggers Sync, because the immediate post-save sync attempt can run before the newly enqueued queue item is visible to the sync path.

Context:
User report on 2026-03-19: after logging an exercise, the session did not sync until manual Sync was triggered from the hamburger menu.
Current code path review:
- hooks/useTrackerSession.js enqueues the payload, then immediately calls sync()
- hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js sync() reads queueRef.current
- queueRef.current can still reflect the pre-enqueue queue on the same tick, so the just-added session may be skipped by that immediate sync attempt
This appears distinct from the already tracked mount-time auto-sync-on-open issue and from the existing sync-toast/copy parity bead.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve queue-first save semantics
- Do not regress duplicate-safe 409 handling
- Do not require the user to trigger manual Sync for a newly saved online session
- Keep user-scoped queue behavior intact

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Immediately after queueing a newly saved session while online, the sync path includes that newly queued item
- [ ] Newly saved online sessions do not remain stranded until manual Sync
- [ ] Queue-first save order is preserved (enqueue before network attempt)
- [ ] 409 duplicate responses still drain the queued item successfully
- [ ] User-scoped queue storage behavior remains intact

{"id":"pt-flq.1","title":"Next.js index adherence uses wrong 'today' and recency bucket rules","description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,adherence,time,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-history.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js

Issue:
Next.js index adherence logic computes recency differently from static index, so `Done today` and day-bucket labels can be wrong.

Context:
Static index.html computes recency with getDaysDiff(), which normalizes both dates to local midnight before comparing calendar days. It then uses exact buckets: `0 days = Done today`, `1-3 days = green X day(s) ago`, `4-7 days = orange X days ago`, `8+ days = red X days ago`. Current Next.js lib/index-history.js instead computes `daysSince` as a rolling elapsed-hours floor using `(Date.now() - performed_at) / 24h`, then uses different threshold logic (`0-6 good`, `7-13 due`, `14+ overdue`) before mapping text. This can mislabel sessions around midnight and diverges from the original adherence semantics even when data is correct.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo(). Preserve the existing ExercisePicker-facing API shape if possible, but the underlying calculation and bucket boundaries must match static parity exactly. Treat local calendar date semantics as functional behavior, not display polish.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A session logged before local midnight and viewed after local midnight is not still labeled `Done today`
- [ ] `Done today` is based on local calendar date normalization, not rolling elapsed hours
- [ ] Recency buckets match static exactly: 0, 1-3, 4-7, 8+
- [ ] ExercisePicker badges and adherence text match static index for the same log timestamps

Dependencies:

- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline queue uses localStorage key pt_offline_queue, mutation field client_mutation_id tied to session id, and treats 409 responses as successful queue drain.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
The offline queue is stored under localStorage key pt_offline_queue (user-scoped: pt_offline_queue_${userId}). Each queued session includes the field client_mutation_id set to a stable identity derived from the session id. When syncOfflineQueue() posts a queued session and receives a 409 duplicate response, the session must be removed from the queue and treated as sync success — not left stuck. Queue is cleared item by item on sync success or duplicate-safe handling.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] Each queued session payload includes the field client_mutation_id
- [ ] client_mutation_id is derived from the session id (stable identity)
- [ ] A 409 response during sync causes the queued session to be removed from the queue (treated as success)
- [ ] Queue is cleared item by item (not all at once) as each session syncs successfully
- [ ] Queue is cleared on sign-out (user-scoped key ensures correct isolation)

{"id":"pt-780.2","title":"Verify queue-first save and sync badge parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that session save uses queue-first semantics: queue push before network attempt, sync badge updates immediately, and local history is prepended before network confirms.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this exact save order: trim notes → apply backdate → push to offline queue → update sync badge immediately → attempt immediate network sync → prepend session into local history → clear active tracker state → return to picker → re-render adherence → show "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)". Network success is not the first visible save event — the queue push and UI update must happen first. The user is returned to picker and sees save feedback before sync outcome is known.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Session is pushed into the offline queue before any network sync is attempted
- [ ] Sync badge is updated immediately after queue push (not after network response)
- [ ] Session is prepended into local history immediately after queue push (not after network response)
- [ ] Active tracker state (currentSession, currentExercise) is cleared after local prepend
- [ ] User is returned to picker and sees "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)" before sync outcome
- [ ] Adherence is re-rendered immediately after returning to picker

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-spg.9","title":"Verify session finalization parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that session finalization blocks with no sets, opens notes modal, handles backdate warning, separates cancel vs abandon, and uses queue-first save with immediate return to picker.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
finishSession() blocks with "Please log at least one set before finishing" until at least one accepted set exists. The notes modal is opened (not direct save). Backdate warning fires when the chosen datetime differs from session start by more than two minutes; warning copy is "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." close-notes-modal returns to the active logger without discarding the session. cancel-session asks for confirmation before abandoning. Confirmed discard clears both currentSession and currentExercise. Final save: trim notes → apply backdate (rewrites session timestamp and each set's performed_at) → push to queue → update sync badge → attempt network sync → prepend to local history → clear tracker state → return to picker → re-render adherence → show "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)".

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Done is blocked with "Please log at least one set before finishing" when currentSession.sets is empty
- [ ] Notes modal opens on Done — no direct save without notes flow
- [ ] Backdate warning shows "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." when datetime differs by more than two minutes
- [ ] close-notes-modal returns to the logger view without discarding the in-progress session
- [ ] cancel-session asks for confirmation before abandoning and clearing currentSession/currentExercise
- [ ] Backdate rewrites both the session timestamp and each individual set's performed_at before queueing
- [ ] Queue push happens before network sync attempt — queue-first semantics
- [ ] User is returned to picker immediately after queue push; "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)" is shown

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Tags: nextjs,messages,ui,badge,header
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useMessages.js
- pt-rebuild/components/MessagesModal.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.module.css

Issue:
The tracker messages badge counts a message the signed-in user sent as new/unread, and the messages icon is positioned incorrectly in the tracker header.

Context:
User report on 2026-03-19: the tracker shows "1 new" even when the newest message is one the current user sent rather than received. The header-level messages icon placement is also wrong on the Next.js tracker page.
The unread badge should reflect inbound unread messages for the current viewer, not outbound messages they authored themselves. The layout issue appears to be in the header action placement on the migrated tracker shell.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not count self-authored outbound messages as unread/new for the sender
- Preserve received-message unread behavior and badge clearing when the modal is opened
- Fix header placement without regressing refresh/menu alignment on mobile

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Outbound messages authored by the signed-in user do not increment the unread/new badge
- [ ] Received unread messages still increment the badge correctly
- [ ] Opening the messages modal clears only the appropriate unread state
- [ ] The messages icon is positioned correctly in the tracker header on mobile-sized layouts
- [ ] Refresh, messages, and menu controls remain aligned after the layout fix

- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Current Next.js offline behavior is not reliable: the tracker offline queue is still stored in localStorage, the root service worker is still legacy-static oriented, API/data bootstrap is network-only, and offline navigation falls back to /index.html instead of the current Next.js routes. Required scope: (1) replace localStorage queue persistence with IndexedDB for the tracker/offline write path, (2) define route-aware offline support for /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab, (3) redesign the root service worker so it supports both legacy static surfaces and current Next.js routes during migration, (4) move service-worker registration to a shared Next.js entry point, and (5) document the architecture and rollout constraints. Constraints: do not introduce new localStorage offline persistence; preserve the existing static app while migration is in progress; avoid overlapping Claude's toast work unless coordination is needed. {"id":"pt-rb8","title":"Fix immediate post-save sync missing newly queued tracker sessions","description":"Source Reference: user report 2026-03-19
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,offline,sync,reliability,queue
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
A newly saved tracker session can remain unsynced until the user manually triggers Sync, because the immediate post-save sync attempt can run before the newly enqueued queue item is visible to the sync path.

Context:
User report on 2026-03-19: after logging an exercise, the session did not sync until manual Sync was triggered from the hamburger menu.
Current code path review:
- hooks/useTrackerSession.js enqueues the payload, then immediately calls sync()
- hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js sync() reads queueRef.current
- queueRef.current can still reflect the pre-enqueue queue on the same tick, so the just-added session may be skipped by that immediate sync attempt
This appears distinct from the already tracked mount-time auto-sync-on-open issue and from the existing sync-toast/copy parity bead.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve queue-first save semantics
- Do not regress duplicate-safe 409 handling
- Do not require the user to trigger manual Sync for a newly saved online session
- Keep user-scoped queue behavior intact

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Immediately after queueing a newly saved session while online, the sync path includes that newly queued item
- [ ] Newly saved online sessions do not remain stranded until manual Sync
- [ ] Queue-first save order is preserved (enqueue before network attempt)
- [ ] 409 duplicate responses still drain the queued item successfully
- [ ] User-scoped queue storage behavior remains intact

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
","notes":"Progress update 2026-03-19:
Implemented a queue-first sync fix so the immediate post-save sync attempt can include the just-enqueued payload instead of relying only on queueRef.current after React state settles.

Change made:
- hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js: sync() now accepts an optional sessions override list
- hooks/useTrackerSession.js: final save passes the newly queued payload directly into sync([payload])

Validation so far:
- npm run build passed
- Code path now preserves enqueue-before-network ordering while removing the race where the new payload could be absent from queueRef.current on the same tick

Remaining:
- Preview or device verification of the end-to-end save-then-sync behavior","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T21:40:46Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-19T21:43:54Z"}

{"id":"pt-vkb","title":"Reorganize pt-rebuild/docs into current, generated, legacy, and historical references","description":"Source Reference: user-directed docs review 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: docs,cleanup,information-architecture,migration,beads
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_PRACTICES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/AI_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_NOTES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/dev_notes.json
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Review and reorganize `pt-rebuild/docs` so current operational docs are easy to find, stale guidance is corrected, generated and legacy files are clearly separated, and historical one-off memos stop competing with living references.

Context:
A review of `pt-rebuild/docs` on 2026-03-20 found several doc-health problems that now make the folder harder to trust and navigate:
- `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md` mixes historical decisions, live migration status, branch strategy, and architecture rules that now overlap with `README.md` and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`; it also contradicts itself by saying all work stays on `nextjs` until full completion while also saying incremental merges to `main` are the active strategy.
- `DEVELOPMENT.md` still describes the app as a static frontend in `public/`, which no longer matches the live repo shape documented in `README.md`.
- `TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md` still says the offline log path queues to `localStorage`, while `README.md` now documents IndexedDB-backed offline storage.
- The top-level `docs` folder mixes living references with generated files (`BEADS_QUICKREF.md`, `DEV_NOTES.md`), legacy archive inputs (`dev_notes.json`), and dated one-off memos (`API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md`, `PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md`, `SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md`) without clear grouping or retention rules.
- `AI_WORKFLOW.md` is now essentially a legacy-transition note; its placement and naming make it easy to mistake for an active workflow doc.

Recommended outcome:
- Define a clearer docs information architecture for current references vs generated files vs legacy archive vs dated analysis memos.
- Update current docs so they reflect the live Next.js-plus-legacy hybrid app accurately.
- Reduce duplicate ownership/status guidance across `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`.
- Add explicit placement/retention rules so future one-off audits and dated memos do not accumulate at the docs root without a home.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve useful historical context; do not delete dated analysis blindly.
- Keep generated files generated, not hand-maintained.
- Respect existing references from `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`; update those references in the same change if files move or are renamed.
- Do not revive `dev_notes` as the active tracker.
- Prefer a small number of clearly named folders or index sections over a deep hierarchy.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current-state docs no longer contradict the live repo architecture or offline storage model
- [ ] `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md` have clearly separated roles with minimal duplicated guidance
- [ ] Generated files and legacy archive files are clearly labeled and, if moved, all references are updated
- [ ] Historical memos/audits have an intentional home and naming pattern
- [ ] A future agent can tell where to look for current workflow guidance in under a minute

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if new docs drift reintroduces contradictory current-state guidance or the docs root starts accumulating uncategorized dated memos again.

{"id":"pt-pbs","title":"Add implementation patterns binder for approved shared helpers and guardrails","description":"Source Reference: user-directed docs follow-up 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: docs,implementation,patterns,guardrails,nextjs
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/docs/README.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_CODE_STRUCTURE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md
- pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md

Issue:
Create a clear implementation-patterns binder that tells agents which shared components/helpers to use, which older patterns to avoid, and where to look for approved do-this-not-that guidance.

Context:
The current docs now separate status, structure, architecture, testing, and Beads workflow, but there is still no single active reference for implementation decisions such as shared dropdown components, formatting helpers, date utilities, offline storage mechanisms, and other approved reuse patterns. Without that binder, agents can still find the files but may not know which one they are expected to use versus avoid.

Required scope:
- Add an active doc focused on implementation patterns and guardrails
- Cover concrete examples such as selects/dropdowns, formatting helpers, date calculations, offline storage, auth/data access, and shared feedback/toast behavior where applicable
- Use direct do-this / do-not-do-this guidance
- Wire the new doc into the docs index and AGENTS canonical references so agents have a clear reason to open it

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep guidance practical and file-specific, not abstract architecture prose
- Do not duplicate long ownership maps from README
- Prefer short pattern bullets with direct file references
- Preserve neutral, inclusive wording

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A new active implementation-patterns doc exists

{"id":"pt-vkb","title":"Reorganize pt-rebuild/docs into current, generated, legacy, and historical references","description":"Source Reference: user-directed docs review 2026-03-20
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: docs,cleanup,information-architecture,migration,beads
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_PRACTICES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/AI_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/DEV_NOTES.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/dev_notes.json
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Review and reorganize `pt-rebuild/docs` so current operational docs are easy to find, stale guidance is corrected, generated and legacy files are clearly separated, and historical one-off memos stop competing with living references.

Context:
A review of `pt-rebuild/docs` on 2026-03-20 found several doc-health problems that now make the folder harder to trust and navigate:
- `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md` mixes historical decisions, live migration status, branch strategy, and architecture rules that now overlap with `README.md` and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`; it also contradicts itself by saying all work stays on `nextjs` until full completion while also saying incremental merges to `main` are the active strategy.
- `DEVELOPMENT.md` still describes the app as a static frontend in `public/`, which no longer matches the live repo shape documented in `README.md`.
- `TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md` still says the offline log path queues to `localStorage`, while `README.md` now documents IndexedDB-backed offline storage.
- The top-level `docs` folder mixes living references with generated files (`BEADS_QUICKREF.md`, `DEV_NOTES.md`), legacy archive inputs (`dev_notes.json`), and dated one-off memos (`API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md`, `PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md`, `SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md`) without clear grouping or retention rules.
- `AI_WORKFLOW.md` is now essentially a legacy-transition note; its placement and naming make it easy to mistake for an active workflow doc.

Recommended outcome:
- Define a clearer docs information architecture for current references vs generated files vs legacy archive vs dated analysis memos.
- Update current docs so they reflect the live Next.js-plus-legacy hybrid app accurately.
- Reduce duplicate ownership/status guidance across `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`.
- Add explicit placement/retention rules so future one-off audits and dated memos do not accumulate at the docs root without a home.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve useful historical context; do not delete dated analysis blindly.
- Keep generated files generated, not hand-maintained.
- Respect existing references from `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`; update those references in the same change if files move or are renamed.
- Do not revive `dev_notes` as the active tracker.
- Prefer a small number of clearly named folders or index sections over a deep hierarchy.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current-state docs no longer contradict the live repo architecture or offline storage model
- [ ] `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md` have clearly separated roles with minimal duplicated guidance
- [ ] Generated files and legacy archive files are clearly labeled and, if moved, all references are updated
- [ ] Historical memos/audits have an intentional home and naming pattern
- [ ] A future agent can tell where to look for current workflow guidance in under a minute

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if new docs drift reintroduces contradictory current-state guidance or the docs root starts accumulating uncategorized dated memos again.

{"id":"pt-flq.1","title":"Next.js index adherence uses wrong 'today' and recency bucket rules","description":"Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,adherence,time,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-history.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js

Issue:
Next.js index adherence logic computes recency differently from static index, so `Done today` and day-bucket labels can be wrong.

Context:
Static index.html computes recency with getDaysDiff(), which normalizes both dates to local midnight before comparing calendar days. It then uses exact buckets: `0 days = Done today`, `1-3 days = green X day(s) ago`, `4-7 days = orange X days ago`, `8+ days = red X days ago`. Current Next.js lib/index-history.js instead computes `daysSince` as a rolling elapsed-hours floor using `(Date.now() - performed_at) / 24h`, then uses different threshold logic (`0-6 good`, `7-13 due`, `14+ overdue`) before mapping text. This can mislabel sessions around midnight and diverges from the original adherence semantics even when data is correct.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo(). Preserve the existing ExercisePicker-facing API shape if possible, but the underlying calculation and bucket boundaries must match static parity exactly. Treat local calendar date semantics as functional behavior, not display polish.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A session logged before local midnight and viewed after local midnight is not still labeled `Done today`
- [ ] `Done today` is based on local calendar date normalization, not rolling elapsed hours
- [ ] Recency buckets match static exactly: 0, 1-3, 4-7, 8+
- [ ] ExercisePicker badges and adherence text match static index for the same log timestamps

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-flq

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity beads (pt-eg2.10, pt-780.6) are verified or closed.
- This bead covers the concrete adherence bug; the linked parity beads remain the final acceptance surface for adherence and time semantics.","notes":"2026-03-18: pt-nyn.1 closed as duplicate. Progress update 2026-03-19:

- updated lib/pt-view.js to use the same shared daysBetween() for needs-attention recency
- updated lib/rehab-coverage.js to consume the shared helper so migrated date math follows one source of truth
- updated README shared utilities section for the new helper

Validation so far:
- npm run build passed

- Move or centralize timer cue behavior if TimerPanel is owning shared hold/rep-complete rules that belong in the timer layer

old_value: {"id":"pt-780.10","title":"Replace localStorage offline persistence and legacy SW routing with IndexedDB-backed Next.js offline architecture","description":"Offline capability is a core product requirement because the PT office may have little or no cell service. Current Next.js offline behavior is not reliable: the tracker offline queue is still stored in localStorage, the root service worker is still legacy-static oriented, API/data bootstrap is network-only, and offline navigation falls back to /index.html instead of the current Next.js routes. This task should define and implement the migration to a unified root service worker plus IndexedDB-backed offline persistence for the Next.js app while preserving the existing static app during migration. Required scope: (1) replace localStorage queue persistence with IndexedDB for the tracker/offline write path, (2) define route-aware offline support for /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab, (3) redesign the root service worker so it supports both legacy static surfaces and current Next.js routes during migration, (4) move service-worker registration to a shared Next.js entry point, and (5) document the architecture and rollout constraints. Constraints: do not introduce new localStorage offline persistence; preserve the existing static app while migration is in progress; avoid overlapping Claude's toast work unless coordination is needed. Validation checklist: [ ] offline-capable app data and pending writes no longer rely on localStorage, [ ] root service worker supports current Next.js route shells without breaking legacy static pages, [ ] offline navigation no longer falls back blindly to /index.html for Next.js routes, [ ] route/bootstrap caching strategy is documented for /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab, [ ] README and tracking docs reflect the new offline architecture.","notes":"2026-03-20 session handoff: landed and pushed IndexedDB offline/bootstrap work for /pt-view, /program, and the tracker queue/auth foundation (commit 34fd98b after 7c5384a). Closed child route-bootstrap beads pt-780.10.1 and pt-780.10.2. Beads cleanup also closed pt-ant, pt-t9s, pt-bdf.1, and pt-bdf; downgraded pt-477.1, pt-1qq.1, pt-1qq.2, and pt-zdk; and added missing related links from pt-41a and pt-zb3 to this offline-architecture parent. Next implementation focus is pt-780.10.3: add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes (exercise CRUD, roles, dosage, vocab) because offline editing in the PT office is a must-have. Keep pt-41a and pt-zb3 open as active index offline-read/cold-open work; they were not completed by the route bootstrap changes landed today.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T23:44:36Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-20T00:52:09Z"}

- DB: patient_activity_logs + patient_activity_sets + patient_activity_set_form_data (queried)

Validation (all pass):

[PASS] Side parity — SessionLoggerModal.js isSided check (line 80) uses exercise.pattern === 'side' only. Side selector hidden for bilateral. Options are left/right only — no 'both' option exists in current code. DB confirms: bilateral exercises (Dead Bug, Ball Squeeze, Double Knee To Chest, Hand Heel Rocking, Supine 90/90) all have side=null. Sided exercises have left/right only. DN-063 ('Both' option) was already fixed in current code.

[PASS] DN-061 form_data in history — GET /api/logs correctly joins patient_activity_set_form_data and groups by set id (api/logs.js lines 110-134). HistoryList.js renders set.form_data[].parameter_value at lines 25 and 67. DB confirms form_data rows exist: Long Arc Quad (weight=8lb), Ankle Eversion/Inversion/Dorsiflexion Theraband exercises (resistance band=black/blue). Values correctly scoped per set.

[PASS] Edit/PATCH flow — openEdit (useSessionLogging.js:37-46) loads log.sets via normalizeSet preserving set_number. PATCH handler (api/logs.js:368-454) deletes+reinserts sets with DN-004 set_number-keyed form_data matching. Both performed_at and notes update correctly. 

[PASS] Idempotency — API handles at two levels: DB unique constraint (error.code 23505 → 409) and RPC duplicate flag (rpcResult.duplicate === true → 409). Client treats 409 as success (useSessionLogging.js:137-141). Live POST test not run (Chrome DevTools MCP unavailable this session); code path is correct.

[PASS] Offline enqueue path — DN-057 (activity_type ReferenceError) is fixed: catch block uses inferActivityType(exercise) directly (line 156). Both offline trigger paths work: navigator.onLine===false check before fetch (lines 122-127) and network failure catch (lines 148-165). Both call onEnqueue() with a valid payload containing activity_type.

[PASS] DN-056 form-parameter behavior — historicalFormParams built from allLogs via collectGlobalParameterValues (index.js:131). Modal renders historical dropdown when values exist, falls back to text input when none. Weight/distance params use numeric input + unit selector. All paths confirmed in SessionLoggerModal.js.

- DN-062 (adherence badge for all exercises shows 'No history') — tracked separately, Codex
- Idempotency live-network test (POST same client_mutation_id twice) — optional, code is correct; can be done when Chrome DevTools MCP is available

{"id":"pt-41a","title":"Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html for offline cold-open","description":"Source Reference: discovered during offline cache root cause analysis 2026-03-13
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: Next.js PWA service worker (Phase 4h)
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, pwa, service-worker, cache, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/public/sw.js (new Next.js sw, not static app sw.js)

Issue:
The Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html. If it pre-caches /index.html (as static app does), offline cold-open fails — browser navigates to / and caches.match() finds no entry. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' and no JS runs — IndexedDB read cache never reached.

Root Cause (confirmed in static app 2026-03-13):
Static sw.js STATIC_ASSETS includes '/index.html'. Browser navigate requests are for the root URL '/'. Different cache keys. network-first cache.put() stores root URL dynamically but does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Pre-cache '/' in STATIC_ASSETS, not '/index.html'
- Navigate fallback handler must try caches.match('/') before returning offline error
- Do NOT repeat the /index.html pattern from static app sw.js
- This must be resolved before ptrebuild-zb3 (IndexedDB read cache) can work — if page does not load, cache code never runs

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online, close, airplane mode, reopen: app shell loads from SW cache
- Exercises load from IndexedDB (ptrebuild-zb3)
- No 'Offline - no cached version available' on cold-open after prior online visit

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-zb3, child of ptrebuild-v3l, blocks ptrebuild-zb3","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:13:31Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-18T18:40:13Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"2026-03-21 runtime investigation with Chrome DevTools found the service worker install path was still failing on preview because /index.html returned 404 on the Next.js route surface while sw.js still included it in STATIC_ASSETS. That leaves the new worker stuck installing with an empty cache and reproduces offline cold-open failure (ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED) even though '/' is listed. Implemented fix: removed /index.html from STATIC_ASSETS, mapped /index.html navigation fallback to '/', and bumped CACHE_NAME to pt-tracker-v13 so the corrected precache can install cleanly. Local verification: npm run build passed.\n2026-03-21 runtime verification on the nextjs preview after cache clear: manually unregistered the old worker, deleted Cache Storage entries, reloaded online, and confirmed a clean pt-tracker-v13 install with 17 cached assets and active controller state. Then switched DevTools to Offline and reloaded /. Result: the root route stayed on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/ and rendered the PT Tracker Sign In shell instead of chrome-error / ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED. This verifies the page-shell cold-open failure was caused by the stale /index.html precache entry and is resolved by the sw.js fix. Remaining read-side data bootstrap validation still belongs to pt-zb3 / pt-780.1."}

{"assignee":"codex","description":"Source Reference: discovered during offline-auth follow-up after pt-41a verification on 2026-03-21
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,offline,auth,ux,program,index,shared,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js
- pt-rebuild/components/AuthForm.js

Issue:
Shared Next.js auth should show clear offline-specific guidance when a user tries to sign in without network connectivity, instead of surfacing a raw fetch failure.

Context:
After the service-worker cold-open fix, the root route can now load offline from cache. However, a fresh sign-in while offline is still expected to fail because Supabase needs network access. The current shared auth path returns the raw SDK/network error string, which does not explain the difference between fresh offline sign-in and warmed-session offline reopen. The shared auth UI should tell the user that signing in requires an internet connection, and that reopening offline only works when a session was already saved on this device.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep existing online sign-in behavior unchanged
- Preserve warmed-session offline reopen behavior; do not clear or bypass stored sessions
- Use neutral, plain-language copy suitable for the operator support contract
- Apply the fix in the shared auth path so /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab stay consistent

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Fresh sign-in attempt while offline shows clear offline-specific guidance instead of a raw fetch failure
- [ ] Online sign-in still returns normal Supabase auth errors when credentials are wrong
- [ ] Warmed-session offline reopen behavior is not regressed
- [ ] Shared auth pages use the same message through useAuth/AuthForm

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-780.10
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-zb3","title":"Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity.","notes":"2026-03-13 QA evidence: confirmed from source. hooks/useIndexData.js has no read-side cache path at all: reload() only does Promise.all network fetches at hooks/useIndexData.js:12-24, then sets error on failure at hooks/useIndexData.js:25-27. When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. Sign-out clears only the offline queue via clearQueue() in pages/index.js:174-177; there is currently no corresponding cached-read data lifecycle. This bead is accurate as written.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T05:22:38Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-18T18:40:13Z"}

{"id":"pt-zb3","title":"Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity.","notes":"2026-03-13 QA evidence: confirmed from source. hooks/useIndexData.js has no read-side cache path at all: reload() only does Promise.all network fetches at hooks/useIndexData.js:12-24, then sets error on failure at hooks/useIndexData.js:25-27. When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. {"id":"pt-4n2","title":"Investigate unexpected Supabase auth key presence in localStorage","description":"Source Reference: discovered during Playwright auth-storage verification on 2026-03-21
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,auth,offline,indexeddb,localstorage,investigation,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/supabase.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
- pt-rebuild/node_modules/@supabase/*

Issue:
During isolated Playwright verification, the browser context exposed a localStorage entry named sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token even though the Next.js Supabase client is intended to persist auth via IndexedDB through authStorage. Investigate whether any active Next.js auth path still writes to localStorage, or whether the observed key is created by Supabase internals for another reason.

Context:
Current code in lib/supabase.js passes auth.storage = authStorage from lib/offline-cache.js. However, browser inspection still surfaced the default Supabase auth key name in localStorage. We need to determine whether this is an actual persistence regression, an auxiliary mirror key from Supabase internals, or a non-app artifact, and fix it if the active Next.js path still relies on localStorage.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use code inspection and runtime evidence; do not assume the source of the key
- Do not break warmed-session offline restore while investigating storage behavior
- Preserve the existing IndexedDB-backed auth path unless runtime evidence shows a real gap
- Update README if ownership or persistence behavior changes

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Source of the localStorage auth key is identified
- [ ] Confirm whether active Next.js auth writes still reach localStorage
- [ ] If needed, active auth persistence is constrained to the intended IndexedDB path
- [ ] Any remaining localStorage usage is documented as intentional legacy or removed

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-780.10
- blocks: n/a

{"notes":"Context for stateless resume:

Root cause: clinical_messages.sender_id stores the users table PK (e.g. '35c3ec8d...'), NOT the Supabase auth UUID (e.g. '11845649...'). In pt-view.js, viewerId is currently set to session.user.id which is the auth UUID — so msg.sender_id === viewerId is always false. Every message appears received, badge overcounts all messages, and sender labels will be wrong.

The fix was already applied to index.js in commit 9471519:
- Added profileId state (= currentUser.id from fetchUsers, i.e. users table PK)
- Added viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist state for sender labels
- Changed useMessages(token, userId) → useMessages(token, profileId)
- Changed viewerId={userId} → viewerId={profileId} in MessagesModal
- Added viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist props to MessagesModal

Apply the identical fix to pt-view.js:
- pt-view.js already calls resolvePatientScopedUserContext() which returns currentUser
- currentUser.id is the profile id needed — extract it and store as profileId state
- pt-view.js lines to fix: 135 (useMessages call), 400 (viewerId prop)
- Also pass viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist to MessagesModal in pt-view.js

Skipped this session because Codex was actively working in pt-view.js — coordinate before starting."}

{"id":"pt-h5o","title":"Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries","description":"Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting.

Important discussion context from the 2026-03-21 handoff:
- Do not treat the documented cap as the real stopping rule. Treat content and ownership clarity as the limit.
- The goal is to make future Codex/Claude work as easy and low-risk as possible; smaller, scoped ownership surfaces are preferred over broad mixed files.
- The user's guidance was explicit: what matters is what is best for future agents to manage, debug, edit, and extend, not whether a file is barely under the hard cap.
- Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems), pt-view at 356 lines is still too large for what it actually owns, which is evidence that its concerns are still too mixed.
- Future-agent rule from discussion: a thin page orchestrator plus scoped hooks/components is better here than asking agents to work inside one 300-400 line mixed page.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] pages/pt-view.js mostly orchestrates auth, route state, modal state, and composition
- [ ] Inline render helpers that form their own page workspaces/panels are extracted to focused components
- [ ] Persisted UI-state and/or note-shaping logic no longer live broadly in the page file
- [ ] Message viewer/profile id wiring remains correct after refactor
- [ ] README points future agents to the new pt-view ownership boundaries

- discovered-from: pt-x2y
- related: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Resume this when returning to route-structure cleanup or when preparing /pt-view for cutover-readiness review.

When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. Sign-out clears only the offline queue via clearQueue() in pages/index.js:174-177; there is currently no corresponding cached-read data lifecycle. This bead is accurate as written.

Current changes add IndexedDB-backed cache writes/fallback to hooks/useIndexData.js after successful exercises/programs/logs fetches, add targeted clearPrograms/clearExercises/clearLogs helpers to lib/offline-cache.js for sign-out cleanup, and update README ownership notes to document tracker read-side fallback on the shared cache path.

2026-03-21 preview check after push: deployed preview loaded successfully, but the fresh isolated Chrome DevTools context did not have a saved authenticated session, so signed-in tracker bootstrap fallback could not be exercised end-to-end in this pass. {"id":"pt-zb3","title":"Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network","description":"Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity.","notes":"2026-03-13 QA evidence: confirmed from source. hooks/useIndexData.js has no read-side cache path at all: reload() only does Promise.all network fetches at hooks/useIndexData.js:12-24, then sets error on failure at hooks/useIndexData.js:25-27. When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. Sign-out clears only the offline queue via clearQueue() in pages/index.js:174-177; there is currently no corresponding cached-read data lifecycle. This bead is accurate as written.

2026-03-21 landed implementation on nextjs in commit fd8ac79. hooks/useIndexData.js now writes exercises/programs/logs into the shared offlineCache after successful fetches, falls back to cached tracker bootstrap data when network fetches fail, exposes fromCache for downstream offline-state UI, and clears tracker read-side stores when auth drops to avoid cross-user leakage. lib/offline-cache.js now includes targeted clearExercises/clearPrograms/clearLogs helpers, and README ownership notes were updated to reflect tracker use of the shared IndexedDB cache.

{"description":"MessagesModal needs viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist/profileId/recipientId on every page that uses messaging. Currently index.js fetches users locally and derives these fields; pt-view.js is missing them entirely. Any future page adding messaging would duplicate this logic again.

Fix: create hooks/useUserContext.js as the single reusable source of truth for user identity and messaging context. Refactor usePtViewData to accept { token, patientId } instead of session (eliminating its internal fetchUsers call). Both index.js and pt-view.js call useUserContext(session) and pass messaging fields to MessagesModal.

Files:
- NEW: hooks/useUserContext.js — fetches users, returns profileId, patientId, recipientId, userRole, emailEnabled, viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist, loading, error
- MODIFY: hooks/usePtViewData.js — change signature to ({ token, patientId }), remove fetchUsers call, return only logs/programs/dataError/offlineNotice
- MODIFY: pages/pt-view.js — call useUserContext, pass messaging props to MessagesModal, local emailEnabled state synced from userCtx
- MODIFY: pages/index.js — replace local fetchUsers useEffect with useUserContext call

emailEnabled toggle pattern: pages keep local state initialized from userCtx.emailEnabled, call patchEmailNotifications + local setter on toggle.

Offline cache: useUserContext handles cacheUsers; usePtViewData handles cacheLogs/cachePrograms."}
