{"id":"pt-h5o","title":"Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries","description":"Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting.

Important discussion context from the 2026-03-21 handoff:
- Do not treat the documented cap as the real stopping rule. Treat content and ownership clarity as the limit.
- The goal is to make future Codex/Claude work as easy and low-risk as possible; smaller, scoped ownership surfaces are preferred over broad mixed files.
- The user's guidance was explicit: what matters is what is best for future agents to manage, debug, edit, and extend, not whether a file is barely under the hard cap.
- Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems), pt-view at 356 lines is still too large for what it actually owns, which is evidence that its concerns are still too mixed.
- Future-agent rule from discussion: a thin page orchestrator plus scoped hooks/components is better here than asking agents to work inside one 300-400 line mixed page.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components

{"id":"pt-h5o","title":"Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries","description":"Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex

Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting.

Important discussion context from the 2026-03-21 handoff:
- Do not treat the documented cap as the real stopping rule. Treat content and ownership clarity as the limit.
- The goal is to make future Codex/Claude work as easy and low-risk as possible; smaller, scoped ownership surfaces are preferred over broad mixed files.
- The user's guidance was explicit: what matters is what is best for future agents to manage, debug, edit, and extend, not whether a file is barely under the hard cap.
- Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems), pt-view at 356 lines is still too large for what it actually owns, which is evidence that its concerns are still too mixed.
- Future-agent rule from discussion: a thin page orchestrator plus scoped hooks/components is better here than asking agents to work inside one 300-400 line mixed page.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

{"id":"pt-r61","title":"Review /index form-parameter value entry parity versus static logger","description":"Source Reference: selector audit 2026-03-19
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,logger,form-parameters,ui,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
The Next.js tracker logger does not hardcode form-parameter names, but its non-unit form-parameter value entry flow differs from the static logger and should be reviewed as shared selector behavior.

Confirmed findings:
- Parameter names are suggestion-backed from historicalFormParams, not hardcoded
- Special unit options are intentionally fixed and match static behavior
- Non-unit values use NativeSelect allowOther or a plain text fallback instead of the static explicit select-plus-Other flow

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not treat fixed unit lists as a bug unless static behavior differs
- Keep logger usability intact for exercise/session flows
- Coordinate with any shared selector helper changes from pt-u71 children

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Non-unit form-parameter value entry behavior is explicitly reviewed against static
- [ ] Any intentional divergence is documented or approved
- [ ] Shared selector behavior stays consistent with the chosen cross-page pattern
- [ ] No hardcoded parameter-name source is introduced

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-u71
- blocks: n/a

{"id":"pt-r61","title":"Review /index form-parameter value entry parity versus static logger","description":"Source Reference: selector audit 2026-03-19
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,logger,form-parameters,ui,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
The Next.js tracker logger does not hardcode form-parameter names, but its non-unit form-parameter value entry flow differs from the static logger and should be reviewed as shared selector behavior.

Confirmed findings:
- Parameter names are suggestion-backed from historicalFormParams, not hardcoded
- Special unit options are intentionally fixed and match static behavior
- Non-unit values use NativeSelect allowOther or a plain text fallback instead of the static explicit select-plus-Other flow

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not treat fixed unit lists as a bug unless static behavior differs
- Keep logger usability intact for exercise/session flows
- Coordinate with any shared selector helper changes from pt-u71 children

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Non-unit form-parameter value entry behavior is explicitly reviewed against static
- [ ] Any intentional divergence is documented or approved
- [ ] Shared selector behavior stays consistent with the chosen cross-page pattern
- [ ] No hardcoded parameter-name source is introduced

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-u71
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if logger form-parameter entry behavior remains ambiguous after the shared selector decision.","notes":"-","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T22:10:46Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T21:31:10Z"}

{"id":"pt-unj.6","title":"C:/Program Files/Git/program select dropdowns missing arrow indicator","description":"Source Reference: discovered during pt-unj.1 preview verification (2026-03-21).

Bug:
The <select> elements on /program (Select Exercise to Edit, Assign Roles, Manage Patient Dosages pickers) render without a dropdown arrow indicator. The tracker page (/) selects show arrows correctly.

Evidence:
Screenshot shows 'Add New Exercise (leave blank)' and 'Choose an exercise' selects with no arrow — just a flat dark box.

Root cause hypothesis:
/program CSS module or ProgramWorkspace components use custom select styles that omit the appearance override + SVG arrow background-image that other pages apply. The select arrow on / comes from globals.css or the ExercisePicker CSS module.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/pages/program.js (or its CSS module), pt-rebuild/components/ (whichever workspace component owns the select elements)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T22:12:18Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T22:12:18Z"}

{"id":"pt-unj.5","title":"C:/Program Files/Git/program NavMenu missing Program Editor link for therapist and admin after pt-unj.1 isAdmin fix","description":"Source Reference: discovered during pt-unj.1 preview verification (2026-03-21).

Bug:
After Codex's pt-unj.1 fix replaced hardcoded isAdmin=true with role-based detection, the NavMenu no longer shows the '📋 Program Editor' link for either therapist (test-therapist@pttracker.app) or admin (cindi@puppyraiser.com) when on /program. Both accounts can still access the editor directly (page renders correctly), but the nav link is hidden.

Evidence:
- test-patient: blocked with 'Therapist or admin access required.' ✓ (correct)
- test-therapist: editor renders, but NavMenu shows only PT Tracker / View History / Coverage Analysis — no Program Editor
- cindi (admin): same — editor renders, NavMenu missing Program Editor link
- The access gate logic is correct; only the NavMenu isAdmin derivation is wrong for /program

Root cause hypothesis:
/program derives isAdmin from the DB role field. Admin user (cindi) has role 'patient' in the DB (she is the sole patient). Therapist has role 'therapist'. isAdmin check may be using role === 'admin' which matches neither. The correct check should be role === 'therapist' || hasAdminPrivilege.

{"id":"pt-1qq","title":"[DN-056] Form parameters (e.g. band_resistance) render as plain text inputs in Next.js SessionLoggerModal. Static index.html renders them as smart dropdowns built from historical log values, with an 'Other...' fallback to free text.","description":"Source DN: DN-056
Status: in_progress
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Agent: codex

Issue:
Form parameters (e.g. band_resistance) render as plain text inputs in Next.js SessionLoggerModal. Static index.html renders them as smart dropdowns built from historical log values, with an 'Other...' fallback to free text.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js, pt-rebuild/pages/index.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js, pt-rebuild/lib/session-form-params.js

Context:
Discovered during DN-048 regression testing (2026-03-03). Codex implemented parity code updates: smart dropdown + 'Other...' for non-unit params, history-driven defaults for create flow (including value+unit parsing like `3 lb`), timezone-safe datetime-local rendering, numeric weight/distance inputs, and select placeholder parity when default exists. This is a DN-048 sub-fix and closes with DN-048 after DN-058 verification passes.

Constraints/Caveats:
No onClick. onPointerUp only. 44px targets. Keep existing weight/distance unit-selector behavior unchanged. 'Other...' custom mode needs per-set-per-param local state in SessionLoggerModal (useState). Pre-existing form_data values (edit flow) that aren't in historicalFormParams must still display — add them as an option or pre-select correctly. ExercisePicker now owns the visible manual-order UI with a six-dot grip handle, drag ghost, and pointer-driven reordering for manual mode. 2026-03-21 follow-up fix: initial preview test exposed an iOS-style drag lifecycle gap where the ghost could enter a stuck state and background content could still receive interaction. Updated ExercisePicker so manual reorder enters full drag mode immediately, locks background touch interaction during drag, routes move/release through the drag overlay plus window fallback, and cancels back to the original order when release happens outside the list bounds. Local next build passed after this fix; fresh preview retest is next.

2026-03-21 persistence follow-up: manual reorder was not surviving reopen because the stored order could be overwritten before the user-scoped sort state finished hydrating. Scoped fix is in useExerciseSortState so localStorage writes wait until the current user's stored values have been read first. Preview retest pending after deploy."}

{"id":"pt-eg2.12","title":"Unblock tracker exercise picker from full history bootstrap","description":"Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 tracker load-time investigation after pt-wde closure
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,tracker,performance,bootstrap,index,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-data.js

Issue:
The Next.js tracker should not block the exercise picker on the full history bootstrap payload. Signed-in preview measurements show /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 is the heaviest initial request and delays the first useful tracker surface even though exercise selection only needs exercises and programs first.

Context:
On 2026-03-21, signed-in preview resource timings showed /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 at about 879 ms and ~521 KB decoded, while /api/exercises and /api/programs were only ~320-400 ms and ~53-57 KB. useIndexData currently waits for all three via one Promise.all before setting tracker state, so the picker cannot render until the history payload finishes.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correctness for logs/history once history finishes loading
- Do not regress offline cache writes or read fallback for tracker bootstrap
- Keep the page orchestrator thin; bootstrap sequencing belongs in hooks/lib
- Avoid broad behavior changes to messaging/history unless directly needed for load-path isolation

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Exercise picker can render from exercises + programs before full history load completes
- [ ] History still loads and populates once the logs request resolves
- [ ] Failed history load does not block exercise selection when exercises/programs succeeded
- [ ] Offline tracker cache behavior is not regressed

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-s81
- parent: pt-eg2","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:34:04Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T23:34:04Z"}

{"id":"pt-eg2.13","title":"Add tracker bootstrap timing marks for observability","description":"Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 tracker load-time investigation after pt-eg2.12 bootstrap split
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,tracker,observability,performance,index,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/tracker-performance.js

Issue:
Tracker load timing needs first-class observability so future agents can see when the picker becomes usable and when history finishes, instead of inferring route latency from raw request timings alone.

Context:
Vercel Speed Insights is already mounted globally, but the tracker route does not expose route-specific bootstrap marks. The current tracker path has multiple overlapping async phases (auth/session, users/messages, exercises/programs, logs, picker render), and DevTools traces do not clearly label which phase is delaying the first useful tracker surface.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep instrumentation lightweight and browser-only
- Prefer stable performance.mark/performance.measure names that future agents can search
- Do not add noisy user-facing UI just for instrumentation
- README should document where tracker timing instrumentation lives

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Tracker bootstrap start, primary-ready, history-ready, and picker-ready marks are visible in the browser performance timeline
- [ ] Marks do not break tracker render or offline fallback
- [ ] README points agents to the timing instrumentation location

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-eg2.12
- parent: pt-eg2","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T23:39:52Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T23:39:52Z"}

{"id":"pt-lvp","title":"Inventory legacy static files to keep vs prune from nextjs","description":"Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 cutover planning discussion
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: shared
Tags: nextjs,cutover,legacy,public,cleanup,agent:shared
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/
- pt-rebuild/README.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/
- deployment/cutover references as needed

Issue:
Determine exactly which legacy static files must remain on the nextjs branch and which should move to the legacy fallback branch/subdomain only, so the branch can eventually be pruned without breaking Next.js deployment.

Context:
The user wants nextjs to become a clean modern branch that can eventually replace main, while the legacy static app remains available on a separate branch and subdomain as fallback. The old static app should not live indefinitely inside nextjs just for parity reference. Legacy code will still exist locally and in git history, so parity comparison does not depend on keeping it in the active nextjs tree.

Current exploration context:
- public currently contains legacy HTML routes, legacy css/js directories, shared icons/manifests, sw.js, and stray temp artifacts.
- Active Next.js references already confirmed include /manifest-tracker.json, /icons/icon.svg, and /sw.js.
- reset-password.html may or may not still be a legitimate nextjs-side asset and needs explicit review.

Deliverable:
A keep/prune map with explicit file lists, rationale, and any required README/doc updates. The output should be concrete enough that a follow-up implementation bead can prune the branch safely.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not delete anything yet; this bead is planning/inventory only.
- Assume the fallback legacy branch/subdomain will exist before pruning begins.
- The keep/prune map must preserve Vercel deployability for nextjs.
- Future agents should be able to tell clearly which remaining public/ files are true browser-served assets for Next.js versus legacy leftovers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Inventory of legacy/static files is explicit
- [ ] Files needed by Next.js are clearly separated from legacy-only files
- [ ] reset-password.html and any other edge-case files have an explicit keep/prune decision
- [ ] README/doc update targets are identified
- [ ] Output is sufficient to drive a prune implementation bead

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Resume when starting the legacy-prune cutover-prep lane.

Context:

{"description":"Source Reference: pt-cf0
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: cutover,deployment,git,vercel

Issue:
Create a static branch from current main and add a temporary redirect so legacy.pttracker.app redirects to pttracker.app.

Context:
The static branch will be deployed via a second Vercel project (pt-static) to legacy.pttracker.app. While the static app is still the primary app at pttracker.app, legacy.pttracker.app should redirect there rather than serve duplicate content. The redirect is removed post-flip (pt-cf0.7).

Implementation:
1. Create static branch from main (git checkout -b static origin/main or equivalent)
2. In pt-rebuild/vercel.json on the static branch, add a redirect rule:
   { "source": "/", "destination": "https://pttracker.app", "permanent": false }
   (or use a catch-all source: "/:path*" so all paths redirect)
3. Push static branch to origin

Constraints/Caveats:
- Branch from main, not from nextjs
- Use a 302 (temporary) redirect, not 301 — will be removed post-flip

{"id":"pt-v4y","title":"Prune legacy static app files from nextjs after fallback split","description":"Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 cutover planning discussion
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: shared
Tags: nextjs,cutover,legacy,public,cleanup,agent:shared
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/
- pt-rebuild/README.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/
- any deployment config touched by the prune

Issue:
After the legacy fallback branch/subdomain is in place and the keep/prune map is settled, remove legacy static app files from nextjs while keeping the branch deployable on Vercel.

Context:
The user wants nextjs to become a clean branch that can eventually replace main. The legacy static app should remain available on its own branch/subdomain as fallback, not indefinitely inside nextjs. This follow-up should happen only after the inventory/planning bead has explicitly identified what must stay.

Scope:

{"id":"pt-780.8","title":"Verify copy-sensitive parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

n/a

Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Queue persistence, optimistic snapshots, toast/error handling, and replay are in place. Likely remaining risk is offline-created exercise follow-up mutations: new ExerciseForm entries use a local UUID, and later queued role.add / program.upsert entries may still target that local exercise id during replay. replayProgramQueue currently replays in order but does not appear to do explicit local-id to server-id remapping. old_value: {"id":"pt-780.10.3","title":"Add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes","description":"Offline editor writes on /program are a required product behavior because the PT office may have 0-1 cell bars. Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. Constraints: do not use localStorage for offline persistence; keep the unified root service worker architecture; preserve the existing static app during migration; README and tracking docs must be updated when ownership changes. Validation checklist: [ ] /program allows create/edit actions while offline, [ ] offline editor mutations persist in IndexedDB across reloads, [ ] queued editor mutations replay in the correct order when back online, [ ] failure/conflict states are visible and recoverable, [ ] no new localStorage-based offline persistence is introduced.","notes":"2026-03-20 auth verification on PR #325 preview: warmed-context offline reopen passes. Fresh-context offline cold-open still fails before auth restore because the page shell itself is not available offline in a brand-new context (Chrome ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), which matches the existing root-cache/service-worker blocker rather than the auth hook.\n2026-03-21 follow-up: improved /program offline queue visibility and recovery guidance. Added queue summary helpers in lib/program-offline.js, surfaced queueSummary from useProgramOfflineQueue, updated /program banner copy to distinguish failed vs pending changes and name the affected mutation area, added a recovery hint telling the operator to reopen and save the affected item to replace a failed queued change, updated README ownership notes, and verified with npm run build.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:29:29Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:18:15Z"}

{"notes":"2026-03-20 auth verification on PR #325 preview: warmed-context offline reopen passes. After authenticating once online, opening a new /program tab in the same browser context while fully offline restored the Supabase session and loaded cached editor data without showing the sign-in form. Fresh-context offline login also behaves predictably: with no stored session, the sign-in form renders and submitting credentials offline returns the visible error 'Failed to fetch'. Fresh-context offline cold-open still fails before auth restore because the page shell itself is not available offline in a brand-new context (Chrome ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), which matches the existing root-cache/service-worker blocker rather than the auth hook.
2026-03-21 follow-up: improved /program offline queue visibility and recovery guidance. Added queue summary helpers in lib/program-offline.js, surfaced queueSummary from useProgramOfflineQueue, updated /program banner copy to distinguish failed vs pending changes and name the affected mutation area, added a recovery hint telling the operator to reopen and save the affected item to replace a failed queued change, updated README ownership notes, and verified with npm run build.
2026-03-22 investigation note: reviewed useProgramOfflineQueue/program-offline/useProgramMutationActions/useProgramVocabActions/ExerciseForm/program.js to identify remaining closure gaps. Queue persistence, optimistic snapshots, toast/error handling, and replay are in place. Likely remaining risk is offline-created exercise follow-up mutations: new ExerciseForm entries use a local UUID, and later queued role.add / program.upsert entries may still target that local exercise id during replay. replayProgramQueue currently replays in order but does not appear to do explicit local-id to server-id remapping. Next step: inspect pt-editor createExercise/API behavior to confirm whether exercise create preserves caller-supplied ids; if not, add remap handling in program-offline replay so later queued mutations for the same offline-created exercise resolve to the server id before execution.
2026-03-22 implementation update: confirmed /api/exercises create preserves the client-provided exercise id, so offline-created /program exercises do not currently require a server-id remap to keep later role/dosage mutations valid. To make that dependency rule explicit and future-safe, lib/program-offline.js now carries an exercise-id map during replay and rewrites later exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations against any remapped created id before execution. README now notes that replay-time exercise-id remapping ownership lives in lib/program-offline.js."}

{"notes":"2026-03-20 auth verification on PR #325 preview: warmed-context offline reopen passes. After authenticating once online, opening a new /program tab in the same browser context while fully offline restored the Supabase session and loaded cached editor data without showing the sign-in form. Fresh-context offline login also behaves predictably: with no stored session, the sign-in form renders and submitting credentials offline returns the visible error 'Failed to fetch'. Fresh-context offline cold-open still fails before auth restore because the page shell itself is not available offline in a brand-new context (Chrome ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), which matches the existing root-cache/service-worker blocker rather than the auth hook.
2026-03-21 follow-up: improved /program offline queue visibility and recovery guidance. Added queue summary helpers in lib/program-offline.js, surfaced queueSummary from useProgramOfflineQueue, updated /program banner copy to distinguish failed vs pending changes and name the affected mutation area, added a recovery hint telling the operator to reopen and save the affected item to replace a failed queued change, updated README ownership notes, and verified with npm run build.
2026-03-22 investigation note: reviewed useProgramOfflineQueue/program-offline/useProgramMutationActions/useProgramVocabActions/ExerciseForm/program.js to identify remaining closure gaps. Queue persistence, optimistic snapshots, toast/error handling, and replay are in place. Likely remaining risk is offline-created exercise follow-up mutations: new ExerciseForm entries use a local UUID, and later queued role.add / program.upsert entries may still target that local exercise id during replay. replayProgramQueue currently replays in order but does not appear to do explicit local-id to server-id remapping. Next step: inspect pt-editor createExercise/API behavior to confirm whether exercise create preserves caller-supplied ids; if not, add remap handling in program-offline replay so later queued mutations for the same offline-created exercise resolve to the server id before execution.
2026-03-22 implementation update: confirmed /api/exercises create preserves the client-provided exercise id, so offline-created /program exercises do not currently require a server-id remap to keep later role/dosage mutations valid. To make that dependency rule explicit and future-safe, lib/program-offline.js now carries an exercise-id map during replay and rewrites later exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations against any remapped created id before execution. README now notes that replay-time exercise-id remapping ownership lives in lib/program-offline.js.
2026-03-22 pushed replay hardening in commit e9a6da7 on nextjs: lib/program-offline.js now carries an explicit replay-time exercise-id map so later queued exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations can follow any created-id remap returned by the server. Current API behavior still preserves the client UUID on exercise create, but the replay path no longer relies on that staying true forever. README ownership note updated in the same commit.
2026-03-22 verification focus for next runtime pass: use an authenticated /program preview context and validate the remaining acceptance surface end-to-end. Exact checks: (1) while offline, create a new exercise and confirm the optimistic exercise appears immediately; (2) while still offline, add at least one role and save dosage for that same newly created exercise so the queue contains dependent follow-up mutations; (3) reload /program offline and confirm the queued exercise, role, and dosage state rehydrates from IndexedDB-backed queue/bootstrap data; (4) reconnect and confirm the queued changes replay successfully in order, the queue clears, and the final server-backed exercise/role/dosage state remains visible after refresh; (5) if replay fails, confirm the failed queue banner names the affected area and preserves a recoverable state instead of dropping the change; (6) confirm no new localStorage-based editor offline persistence is used during this flow. If all checks pass, pt-780.10.3 is likely ready to close."}

Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. {"id":"pt-780.8","title":"Verify copy-sensitive parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-780.1","title":"Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline

- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Acceptance verification still remains open.
2026-03-22 verification handoff for parallel thread: verify the current nextjs preview against commit 283974f with a runtime/browser pass only (no new code unless a clear failing case is confirmed). Focus exact acceptance items: (1) when tracker save falls back to offline queue, the user-facing copy is exactly 'Offline - changes will sync later'; (2) Pocket Mode running timer hint is exactly 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial'; (3) triggering NavMenu manual sync with an empty tracker queue shows exactly 'Nothing to sync!'; (4) offline startup fallback shows exact shell copy 'Offline mode' or 'Using cached data' as appropriate; (5) backdate warning copy in SessionNotesModal is exactly 'Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time.'; (6) toast lifecycle is staged: toast is inserted hidden, animates in after a short delay, remains visible for duration, then fades out before unmount; it should not appear instantly or disappear abruptly. Implementation context already landed in 283974f: pages/index.js manual sync now surfaces 'Nothing to sync!', hooks/useTrackerSession.js now uses exact offline-save copy, hooks/useToast.js now stages animate-in before fade-out. Use preview-first validation and record any failing item with the exact observed copy/behavior and file likely responsible. If everything passes, note that pt-780.8 is ready to close.
2026-03-22 verification pass on preview https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app against nextjs HEAD 283974f96acfee01a80cc7d40ce2cf79f36f62a4 using Chrome DevTools + Playwright.

Passed:
- Connectivity-loss save feedback shows exact toast copy `Offline - changes will sync later` during offline Save & Finish.
- Running Pocket Mode timer shows exact hint `Tap to pause · Hold for partial`.
- Manual sync with empty queue shows exact toast/status copy `Nothing to sync!`.
- Backdate warning shows exact copy `Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time.` once the datetime is changed by more than two minutes.
- Toast lifecycle is staged, not abrupt: runtime mutation log showed toast inserted hidden first, then shown, then later removed after remaining visible rather than disappearing immediately.

Failed:
- Offline startup fallback did not preserve cached shell behavior on a true offline reload. After warming the preview online, switching DevTools network emulation to Offline, and reloading, the page navigated to `chrome-error://chromewebdata/` with `ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED` instead of loading the tracker shell with `Offline mode` or `Using cached data`.

Result: pt-780.8 is not ready to close yet because the offline-startup acceptance item still fails in runtime verification.
2026-03-22 parallel runtime verification result: copy-sensitive items are mostly passing, but the bead is not ready to close because offline startup fallback still fails. After warming the preview online, switching the browser to offline, and reloading, the app navigated to chrome-error://chromewebdata/ with ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED instead of loading the cached tracker shell and showing 'Offline mode' or 'Using cached data'. This keeps the acceptance surface open and points back to the offline shell/bootstrap lane rather than the copy strings themselves.
2026-03-22 Codex shared-browser reproduction after restart: using the new non-isolated chrome_devtools_shared MCP with a warmed authenticated nextjs preview session, the failing path from the earlier report did NOT reproduce. In the warmed shared context at https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/, switching network emulation to Offline and reloading kept the tracker on the cached picker with visible 'Using cached data'. Opening a brand-new tab to the same preview while still offline in that same warmed browser context also loaded the cached authenticated tracker shell successfully. This means the earlier chrome-error failure is environment-specific (likely another browser context, service-worker install state, or non-warmed path), not a universal failure of authenticated offline reload."}

Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-780.1","title":"Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that all copy-sensitive strings match the static baseline exactly and that toast animate-in and fade-out timing matches the static staged lifecycle.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
These strings must match exactly: "Offline - changes will sync later" (connectivity-loss save feedback), "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" (running Pocket Mode timer hint), "Nothing to sync!" (empty queue manual sync), "Offline mode" and "Using cached data" (offline startup feedback), "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." (backdate warning). Toast lifecycle: the toast must animate in after a short timeout and be removed only after its display duration plus fade-out delay — not disappear instantly or at a fixed arbitrary timeout.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline - changes will sync later" is shown exactly when saving without network connectivity
- [ ] "Tap to pause · Hold for partial" is the exact hint shown in running Pocket Mode timer
- [ ] "Nothing to sync!" is shown exactly when manual sync is triggered with an empty queue
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown exactly during offline startup fallback
- [ ] "Date/time changed from now. Session will be logged at the selected time." is the exact backdate warning copy
- [ ] Toast animates in after a short timeout (not shown instantly)
- [ ] Toast is removed only after display duration plus fade-out delay (not instant removal)

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-gpt","title":"Expired session shows empty state instead of re-prompting sign-in","description":"When a stored Supabase session has an expired access token that cannot be refreshed, getSession() returns the stale session object from IndexedDB storage (authStorage), making session truthy. The page skips AuthForm and renders the data view with broken empty state — all API calls return 401 but nothing converts those failures into a SIGNED_OUT event or sign-in prompt.

Root cause: useAuth.js line 37 — getSession() trusts whatever is in IndexedDB without server-side validation. onAuthStateChange only clears session on explicit SIGNED_OUT event, which never fires if Supabase doesn't attempt a refresh cycle.

Expected behavior: expired/unrefreshable session should result in user seeing sign-in form, not broken empty data state.

Fix options:
1. Handle TOKEN_REFRESH_ERROR in onAuthStateChange → call supabase.auth.signOut() to clear stale session
2. Use getUser() (makes network call) in addition to getSession() on mount — if getUser() returns error, sign out
3. Add 401 response handler in API fetch layer that triggers sign-out

Option 1 is lowest risk: add TOKEN_REFRESH_ERROR case to the onAuthStateChange listener in hooks/useAuth.js — call signOut() to clear the stale IndexedDB session and let the page fall through to AuthForm.

File scope: hooks/useAuth.js","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:14:02Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:14:02Z"}

new_value: Fixed in commit ba3f286. useAuth.js now calls getUser() after getSession() to validate the token server-side. On error, signs out to clear stale IndexedDB auth_state and lets page fall through to AuthForm.

{"id":"pt-780.10.3","title":"Add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes","description":"Offline editor writes on /program are a required product behavior because the PT office may have 0-1 cell bars. Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. Constraints: do not use localStorage for offline persistence; keep the unified root service worker architecture; preserve the existing static app during migration; README and tracking docs must be updated when ownership changes. Validation checklist: [ ] /program allows create/edit actions while offline, [ ] offline editor mutations persist in IndexedDB across reloads, [ ] queued editor mutations replay in the correct order when back online, [ ] failure/conflict states are visible and recoverable, [ ] no new localStorage-based offline persistence is introduced.","notes":"2026-03-20 auth verification on PR #325 preview: warmed-context offline reopen passes. After authenticating once online, opening a new /program tab in the same browser context while fully offline restored the Supabase session and loaded cached editor data without showing the sign-in form. Fresh-context offline login also behaves predictably: with no stored session, the sign-in form renders and submitting credentials offline returns the visible error 'Failed to fetch'. Fresh-context offline cold-open still fails before auth restore because the page shell itself is not available offline in a brand-new context (Chrome ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), which matches the existing root-cache/service-worker blocker rather than the auth hook.
2026-03-21 follow-up: improved /program offline queue visibility and recovery guidance. Added queue summary helpers in lib/program-offline.js, surfaced queueSummary from useProgramOfflineQueue, updated /program banner copy to distinguish failed vs pending changes and name the affected mutation area, added a recovery hint telling the operator to reopen and save the affected item to replace a failed queued change, updated README ownership notes, and verified with npm run build.
2026-03-22 investigation note: reviewed useProgramOfflineQueue/program-offline/useProgramMutationActions/useProgramVocabActions/ExerciseForm/program.js to identify remaining closure gaps. Queue persistence, optimistic snapshots, toast/error handling, and replay are in place. Likely remaining risk is offline-created exercise follow-up mutations: new ExerciseForm entries use a local UUID, and later queued role.add / program.upsert entries may still target that local exercise id during replay. replayProgramQueue currently replays in order but does not appear to do explicit local-id to server-id remapping. Next step: inspect pt-editor createExercise/API behavior to confirm whether exercise create preserves caller-supplied ids; if not, add remap handling in program-offline replay so later queued mutations for the same offline-created exercise resolve to the server id before execution.
2026-03-22 implementation update: confirmed /api/exercises create preserves the client-provided exercise id, so offline-created /program exercises do not currently require a server-id remap to keep later role/dosage mutations valid. To make that dependency rule explicit and future-safe, lib/program-offline.js now carries an exercise-id map during replay and rewrites later exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations against any remapped created id before execution. README now notes that replay-time exercise-id remapping ownership lives in lib/program-offline.js.
2026-03-22 pushed replay hardening in commit e9a6da7 on nextjs: lib/program-offline.js now carries an explicit replay-time exercise-id map so later queued exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations can follow any created-id remap returned by the server. Current API behavior still preserves the client UUID on exercise create, but the replay path no longer relies on that staying true forever. README ownership note updated in the same commit.
2026-03-22 verification focus for next runtime pass: use an authenticated /program preview context and validate the remaining acceptance surface end-to-end. Exact checks: (1) while offline, create a new exercise and confirm the optimistic exercise appears immediately; (2) while still offline, add at least one role and save dosage for that same newly created exercise so the queue contains dependent follow-up mutations; (3) reload /program offline and confirm the queued exercise, role, and dosage state rehydrates from IndexedDB-backed queue/bootstrap data; (4) reconnect and confirm the queued changes replay successfully in order, the queue clears, and the final server-backed exercise/role/dosage state remains visible after refresh; (5) if replay fails, confirm the failed queue banner names the affected area and preserves a recoverable state instead of dropping the change; (6) confirm no new localStorage-based editor offline persistence is used during this flow. If all checks pass, pt-780.10.3 is likely ready to close.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:29:29Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-22T21:35:37Z"}

{"id":"pt-780.1","title":"Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-780.10.3","title":"Add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes","description":"Offline editor writes on /program are a required product behavior because the PT office may have 0-1 cell bars. Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. Constraints: do not use localStorage for offline persistence; keep the unified root service worker architecture; preserve the existing static app during migration; README and tracking docs must be updated when ownership changes. Validation checklist: [ ] /program allows create/edit actions while offline, [ ] offline editor mutations persist in IndexedDB across reloads, [ ] queued editor mutations replay in the correct order when back online, [ ] failure/conflict states are visible and recoverable, [ ] no new localStorage-based offline persistence is introduced.","notes":"2026-03-20 auth verification on PR #325 preview: warmed-context offline reopen passes. After authenticating once online, opening a new /program tab in the same browser context while fully offline restored the Supabase session and loaded cached editor data without showing the sign-in form. Fresh-context offline login also behaves predictably: with no stored session, the sign-in form renders and submitting credentials offline returns the visible error 'Failed to fetch'. Fresh-context offline cold-open still fails before auth restore because the page shell itself is not available offline in a brand-new context (Chrome ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED), which matches the existing root-cache/service-worker blocker rather than the auth hook.
2026-03-21 follow-up: improved /program offline queue visibility and recovery guidance. Added queue summary helpers in lib/program-offline.js, surfaced queueSummary from useProgramOfflineQueue, updated /program banner copy to distinguish failed vs pending changes and name the affected mutation area, added a recovery hint telling the operator to reopen and save the affected item to replace a failed queued change, updated README ownership notes, and verified with npm run build.
2026-03-22 investigation note: reviewed useProgramOfflineQueue/program-offline/useProgramMutationActions/useProgramVocabActions/ExerciseForm/program.js to identify remaining closure gaps. Queue persistence, optimistic snapshots, toast/error handling, and replay are in place. Likely remaining risk is offline-created exercise follow-up mutations: new ExerciseForm entries use a local UUID, and later queued role.add / program.upsert entries may still target that local exercise id during replay. replayProgramQueue currently replays in order but does not appear to do explicit local-id to server-id remapping. Next step: inspect pt-editor createExercise/API behavior to confirm whether exercise create preserves caller-supplied ids; if not, add remap handling in program-offline replay so later queued mutations for the same offline-created exercise resolve to the server id before execution.
2026-03-22 implementation update: confirmed /api/exercises create preserves the client-provided exercise id, so offline-created /program exercises do not currently require a server-id remap to keep later role/dosage mutations valid. To make that dependency rule explicit and future-safe, lib/program-offline.js now carries an exercise-id map during replay and rewrites later exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations against any remapped created id before execution. README now notes that replay-time exercise-id remapping ownership lives in lib/program-offline.js.
2026-03-22 pushed replay hardening in commit e9a6da7 on nextjs: lib/program-offline.js now carries an explicit replay-time exercise-id map so later queued exercise.update / role.add / program.upsert mutations can follow any created-id remap returned by the server. Current API behavior still preserves the client UUID on exercise create, but the replay path no longer relies on that staying true forever. README ownership note updated in the same commit.
2026-03-22 verification focus for next runtime pass: use an authenticated /program preview context and validate the remaining acceptance surface end-to-end. Exact checks: (1) while offline, create a new exercise and confirm the optimistic exercise appears immediately; (2) while still offline, add at least one role and save dosage for that same newly created exercise so the queue contains dependent follow-up mutations; (3) reload /program offline and confirm the queued exercise, role, and dosage state rehydrates from IndexedDB-backed queue/bootstrap data; (4) reconnect and confirm the queued changes replay successfully in order, the queue clears, and the final server-backed exercise/role/dosage state remains visible after refresh; (5) if replay fails, confirm the failed queue banner names the affected area and preserves a recoverable state instead of dropping the change; (6) confirm no new localStorage-based editor offline persistence is used during this flow. If all checks pass, pt-780.10.3 is likely ready to close.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T00:29:29Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:25:54Z"}

{"id":"pt-780.10.3.1","title":"ExerciseForm: validate required fields before offline queue","description":"The ExerciseForm allows 'Create Exercise' with pt_category left at the 'Select...' placeholder (empty string). The form queues the mutation offline and shows an optimistic success state, but the API requires a non-empty pt_category and returns 400 on replay. The mutation is then stuck as 'failed' with no path to retry or correct it.

Root cause: pt_category select has value='' as a valid unselected state but no client-side required validation before the form submits to the queue.

Fix: add validation before queueing the exercise.create mutation — if pt_category is empty, show an inline error and block submission. Same check should apply to any other API-required fields (pattern may also be required by the API).

{"id":"pt-780.10.3","title":"Add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes","description":"Offline editor writes on /program are a required product behavior because the PT office may have 0-1 cell bars. Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. Constraints: do not use localStorage for offline persistence; keep the unified root service worker architecture; preserve the existing static app during migration; README and tracking docs must be updated when ownership changes. Validation checklist: [ ] /program allows create/edit actions while offline, [ ] offline editor mutations persist in IndexedDB across reloads, [ ] queued editor mutations replay in the correct order when back online, [ ] failure/conflict states are visible and recoverable, [ ] no new localStorage-based offline persistence is introduced.","notes":"2026-03-22 runtime verification (Claude):

PASS — Replay trigger: POST /api/exercises fired automatically on network restore.
PASS — Failure handling: failed mutation marked status=failed with last_error=Validation failed, not dropped.

Core queue mechanism (storage → optimistic UI → replay → failure state) is working correctly. {"id":"pt-780.1","title":"Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-1xb","title":"Tighten Beads workflow docs and reconcile stale-open parity beads","description":"Source Reference: discovered during bead-state cleanup discussion on 2026-03-22
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: process,beads,docs,tracking,hygiene,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_OPERATIONS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/README.md
- Beads state for stale-open parity / verification issues

Issue:
Strengthen the documented Beads workflow so agents close work promptly and manually, then reconcile stale-open parity and verification beads that already have enough evidence to close or narrow.

Context:
Open beads have been lingering after work was already done, especially verification-only items. That leads to repeated verification work, unclear current state, and extra user cost. The docs need a harder rule, and the current tracker state needs a reconciliation pass so open beads reflect real remaining work instead of stale history.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Manual bead closure is required in the current Dolt workflow; commits do not auto-close issues.
- The rule is close when the scoped work is done, not a blanket slogan about commits.
- Verification-only beads may have no commit and still must close immediately when they pass.
- Only close or narrow beads when commit history, notes, and current status provide enough evidence.
- Do not close broad parent epics unless all required child scope is actually done.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Docs explicitly state manual closure, close-when-done, and verification-only closure rules
- [ ] Generated quick reference is refreshed from operations doc
- [ ] Stale-open parity / verification beads are closed or narrowed where evidence supports it
- [ ] Remaining open beads have explicit current-scope notes instead of vague historical state

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-uf1
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if another reconciliation pass finds open beads that still do not match the actual shipped or verified state.
