old_value: {"id":"pt-780.10.3","title":"Add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes","description":"Offline editor writes on /program are a required product behavior because the PT office may have 0-1 cell bars. Cached read/bootstrap support is not enough. This task covers IndexedDB-backed offline mutation support for /program so exercise-library and patient-program changes can be made offline and synced when connectivity returns. Required scope: (1) queue create/update mutations for exercises, roles, dosage/program assignments, and controlled vocabulary changes, (2) preserve dependency ordering so newly created exercises can receive later role/dosage edits before server IDs exist, (3) provide visible pending/synced/failed feedback in the editor UI, (4) replay queued mutations safely on reconnect, and (5) document conflict/error handling rules. Constraints: do not use localStorage for offline persistence; keep the unified root service worker architecture; preserve the existing static app during migration; README and tracking docs must be updated when ownership changes. Validation checklist: [ ] /program allows create/edit actions while offline, [ ] offline editor mutations persist in IndexedDB across reloads, [ ] queued editor mutations replay in the correct order when back online, [ ] failure/conflict states are visible and recoverable, [ ] no new localStorage-based offline persistence is introduced.","notes":"2026-03-22 verification complete (Claude). {"id":"pt-spg.3","title":"Verify timer mode parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that timer mode counts down from target, hold mode advances reps within a set, duration mode is single-run, and the five-part surface is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Timer mode must count down (not up) from the configured target. Hold exercises: each completed countdown rep advances live rep progress, resets the timer display to full target duration, auto-pauses on completion, and defers set acceptance until the configured rep count is satisfied or the user manually logs. Duration exercises: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds — single-run semantics, not multi-rep. The five-part timer surface must include: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, and reset button. Reset clears live timer progress without deleting accepted sets.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Timer counts down from the configured target, not up
- [ ] Timer surface has five named parts: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, reset button
- [ ] Hold: completed countdown rep auto-pauses and advances live rep count without appending an accepted set
- [ ] Hold: timer display resets to full target duration after each rep completes
- [ ] Hold: set is not accepted until target rep count is satisfied or user manually logs
- [ ] Duration: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds (single-run, not multi-rep hold)
- [ ] Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are unaffected

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-1xb","title":"Tighten Beads workflow docs and reconcile stale-open parity beads","description":"Source Reference: discovered during bead-state cleanup discussion on 2026-03-22
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: process,beads,docs,tracking,hygiene,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_WORKFLOW.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_OPERATIONS.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/README.md
- Beads state for stale-open parity / verification issues

Issue:
Strengthen the documented Beads workflow so agents close work promptly and manually, then reconcile stale-open parity and verification beads that already have enough evidence to close or narrow.

Context:
Open beads have been lingering after work was already done, especially verification-only items. That leads to repeated verification work, unclear current state, and extra user cost. The docs need a harder rule, and the current tracker state needs a reconciliation pass so open beads reflect real remaining work instead of stale history.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Manual bead closure is required in the current Dolt workflow; commits do not auto-close issues.
- The rule is close when the scoped work is done, not a blanket slogan about commits.
- Verification-only beads may have no commit and still must close immediately when they pass.
- Only close or narrow beads when commit history, notes, and current status provide enough evidence.
- Do not close broad parent epics unless all required child scope is actually done.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Docs explicitly state manual closure, close-when-done, and verification-only closure rules
- [ ] Generated quick reference is refreshed from operations doc
- [ ] Stale-open parity / verification beads are closed or narrowed where evidence supports it
- [ ] Remaining open beads have explicit current-scope notes instead of vague historical state

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-uf1
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if another reconciliation pass finds open beads that still do not match the actual shipped or verified state.

Issue:
Verify that counter mode renders a giant tap surface, increments correctly, blocks decrement below zero, and shows milestone feedback.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
The counter surface must be a giant tap surface as its primary interaction model — the main tap increments the visible rep count immediately. A secondary minus/undo button decrements but must not go below zero. Live progress is separate from accepted sets and must not be written to currentSession.sets until Next Set or Log Set is confirmed. The three-element surface must include a counter label, a large tappable display, and a minus/undo button. Milestone speech at "5 reps left", "3 reps left", "Last rep", and "Set complete" must be preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
The spec requires side controls to be shown only for sided exercises and hidden for bilateral exercises. currentSide must be null for bilateral exercises, not "both" or any other value. Side-switch speech must announce the active side (e.g., "Working left side", "Working right side"). The log-set modal has its own logSetSelectedSide which is initialized from the main tracker side when the modal opens and is cleared when the modal closes or a new modal open occurs. Manual log-set saves use modal-local side, not the main tracker side directly.

Status: open

Agent Owner: codex

- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useManualLog.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js

Issue:
When logging a set for a sided exercise, the tracker does not stay on the currently selected side. After each set is logged, the active side resets back to right instead of preserving the user's current side.

Context:
Live runtime report from 2026-03-22: select a sided exercise, switch to left, log a set, and observe that the logger resets to right after the set is recorded. This breaks side-switching parity and can cause repeated extra taps or wrong-side logging flow during active use.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correct behavior for both Log Set and Next Set flows
- Preserve existing non-sided exercise behavior
- Do not regress timer-mode side handling or manual-log side defaults
- Validate against the canonical tracker logging parity spec before closing

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] For sided exercises, logging a set preserves the current side instead of resetting to right
- [ ] Next Set flow preserves the current side
- [ ] Manual log flow preserves or preloads the currently active side correctly
- [ ] Non-sided exercises remain unchanged

{"id":"pt-ejn","title":"Preserve active side after logging a set","description":"Source Reference: discovered during live tracker parity testing on 2026-03-22
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,tracker,side-switching,logging,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useManualLog.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js

Issue:
When logging a set for a sided exercise, the tracker does not stay on the currently selected side. After each set is logged, the active side resets back to right instead of preserving the user's current side.

Context:
Live runtime report from 2026-03-22: select a sided exercise, switch to left, log a set, and observe that the logger resets to right after the set is recorded. This breaks side-switching parity and can cause repeated extra taps or wrong-side logging flow during active use.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correct behavior for both Log Set and Next Set flows
- Preserve existing non-sided exercise behavior
- Do not regress timer-mode side handling or manual-log side defaults
- Validate against the canonical tracker logging parity spec before closing

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] For sided exercises, logging a set preserves the current side instead of resetting to right
- [ ] Next Set flow preserves the current side
- [ ] Manual log flow preserves or preloads the currently active side correctly
- [ ] Non-sided exercises remain unchanged

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-spg.4
- discovered-from: pt-spg.6
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-unj.6","title":"PT program page select dropdowns missing arrow indicator","description":"Source Reference: discovered during pt-unj.1 preview verification (2026-03-21).

Bug:
The <select> elements on /program (Select Exercise to Edit, Assign Roles, Manage Patient Dosages pickers) render without a dropdown arrow indicator. The tracker page (/) selects show arrows correctly.

Evidence:
Screenshot shows 'Add New Exercise (leave blank)' and 'Choose an exercise' selects with no arrow — just a flat dark box.

Root cause hypothesis:
/program CSS module or ProgramWorkspace components use custom select styles that omit the appearance override + SVG arrow background-image that other pages apply. The select arrow on / comes from globals.css or the ExercisePicker CSS module.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/pages/program.js (or its CSS module), pt-rebuild/components/ (whichever workspace component owns the select elements)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T22:12:18Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-21T22:14:22Z"}

Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,logging,side-switch,logger
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
Issue:
When logging a sided exercise through Log Set, the tracker does not preserve the currently selected side after a save. In Playwright preview verification, Long Arc Quad was logged once on right, then the main tracker side was switched to left, Log Set opened with Side=Left as expected, and saving that left-side set returned to the panel showing Working right side instead of staying on left. The left set was recorded (left count incremented), but active side context snapped back to right.
Context:
This was found while verifying pt-spg.4 against the deployed nextjs preview. Existing side behavior that does work: side controls render; switching to left updates the panel label; Log Set modal inherits the current tracker side; manual log saves use modal-local side for the saved set. The remaining bug is persistence of active side context after the modal save completes.
Constraints/Caveats:
Do not change bilateral null-side semantics while fixing this. Preserve the existing correct modal-local side save behavior.
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] After saving a left-side Log Set, the panel remains on left
- [ ] After saving a right-side Log Set, the panel remains on right
- [ ] Saved set side still comes from modal-local side selection
- [ ] Bilateral exercises still keep null side semantics
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-spg.4
Reactivation Trigger:

File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-data.js

Issue:
The Next.js tracker should not block the exercise picker on the full history bootstrap payload. Signed-in preview measurements show /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 is the heaviest initial request and delays the first useful tracker surface even though exercise selection only needs exercises and programs first.

Context:
On 2026-03-21, signed-in preview resource timings showed /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 at about 879 ms and ~521 KB decoded, while /api/exercises and /api/programs were only ~320-400 ms and ~53-57 KB. useIndexData currently waits for all three via one Promise.all before setting tracker state, so the picker cannot render until the history payload finishes.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correctness for logs/history once history finishes loading
- Do not regress offline cache writes or read fallback for tracker bootstrap
- Keep the page orchestrator thin; bootstrap sequencing belongs in hooks/lib
- Avoid broad behavior changes to messaging/history unless directly needed for load-path isolation

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Exercise picker can render from exercises + programs before full history load completes
- [ ] History still loads and populates once the logs request resolves
- [ ] Failed history load does not block exercise selection when exercises/programs succeeded
- [ ] Offline tracker cache behavior is not regressed

Issue:
When logging a set for a sided exercise, the tracker does not stay on the currently selected side. After each set is logged, the active side resets back to right instead of preserving the user's current side.

Context:
Live runtime report from 2026-03-22: select a sided exercise, switch to left, log a set, and observe that the logger resets to right after the set is recorded. This breaks side-switching parity and can cause repeated extra taps or wrong-side logging flow during active use.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correct behavior for both Log Set and Next Set flows
- Preserve existing non-sided exercise behavior
- Do not regress timer-mode side handling or manual-log side defaults
- Validate against the canonical tracker logging parity spec before closing

- [ ] For sided exercises, logging a set preserves the current side instead of resetting to right
- [ ] Next Set flow preserves the current side
- [ ] Manual log flow preserves or preloads the currently active side correctly
- [ ] Non-sided exercises remain unchanged

{"id":"pt-amc","title":"Agent session handoff queue","description":"Shared queue container for next-session agent work in pt-rebuild. {"id":"pt-spg.4","title":"Verify side switching parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that side controls are visible for sided exercises, side announcements play on switch, modal side inheritance works, and bilateral null-side semantics are preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
The spec requires side controls to be shown only for sided exercises and hidden for bilateral exercises. currentSide must be null for bilateral exercises, not "both" or any other value. Side-switch speech must announce the active side (e.g., "Working left side", "Working right side"). The log-set modal has its own logSetSelectedSide which is initialized from the main tracker side when the modal opens and is cleared when the modal closes or a new modal open occurs. Manual log-set saves use modal-local side, not the main tracker side directly.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Side controls are visible for sided exercises and hidden for bilateral exercises
- [ ] currentSide is null for bilateral exercises (not "both" or any string value)
- [ ] Switching side speaks "Working left side" or "Working right side"
- [ ] logSetSelectedSide is initialized from currentSide when the Log Set modal opens
- [ ] logSetSelectedSide is cleared when the Log Set modal closes or a new modal open occurs
- [ ] Log Set saves use logSetSelectedSide for the set's side value, not currentSide directly

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
","notes":"2026-03-22 Playwright verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app using test-patient@pttracker.app: verified that side controls are visible on a sided exercise (Long Arc Quad), switching side updates the active label (Working left side / Working right side), Log Set modal inherits the current tracker side, and the saved set uses the modal-local side value (right count incremented after right save; left count incremented after left save). New failing condition discovered: after saving a left-side Log Set, the panel snapped back to Working right side instead of staying on left. Created discovered bead for that follow-up. Bilateral currentSide=null semantics were not verified in this pass because the test account only exposed a sided exercise.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:19:12Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:26:21Z"}

{"id":"pt-780.3","title":"Verify reconnect behavior parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Connectivity state and sync UI are updated immediately on reconnect regardless of current view
- [ ] Queue sync is attempted when authenticated state and viewingPatientId are both available
- [ ] Full picker data refresh happens only when the picker view is active
- [ ] Logger view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] History view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] After successful sync, history is reloaded and cache is hydrated

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

{"id":"pt-780.3","title":"Verify reconnect behavior parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Connectivity state and sync UI are updated immediately on reconnect regardless of current view
- [ ] Queue sync is attempted when authenticated state and viewingPatientId are both available
- [ ] Full picker data refresh happens only when the picker view is active
- [ ] Logger view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] History view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] After successful sync, history is reloaded and cache is hydrated

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

{"id":"pt-ps9","title":"Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries","description":"Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting. Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems) and the project guidance that pages should mainly compose hooks/components, pt-view still needs another decomposition pass before cutover.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] pages/pt-view.js mostly orchestrates auth, route state, modal state, and composition
- [ ] Inline render helpers that form their own page workspaces/panels are extracted to focused components
- [ ] Persisted UI-state and/or note-shaping logic no longer live broadly in the page file
- [ ] Message viewer/profile id wiring remains correct after refactor
- [ ] README points future agents to the new pt-view ownership boundaries

- blocks: n/a

{"id":"pt-780.3","title":"Verify reconnect behavior parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2

Agent Eligible: codex

File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Connectivity state and sync UI are updated immediately on reconnect regardless of current view
- [ ] Queue sync is attempted when authenticated state and viewingPatientId are both available
- [ ] Full picker data refresh happens only when the picker view is active
- [ ] Logger view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] History view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] After successful sync, history is reloaded and cache is hydrated

- blocks: n/a

{"id":"pt-780.3","title":"Verify reconnect behavior parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open

- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Connectivity state and sync UI are updated immediately on reconnect regardless of current view
- [ ] Queue sync is attempted when authenticated state and viewingPatientId are both available
- [ ] Full picker data refresh happens only when the picker view is active
- [ ] Logger view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] History view is not interrupted by a full picker refresh on reconnect
- [ ] After successful sync, history is reloaded and cache is hydrated

- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

old_value: {"id":"pt-de9","title":"Tracker bootstrap uses auth UUID instead of resolved patient context","description":"Source Reference: discovered during pt-780.3 reconnect verification on 2026-03-22\\nStatus: open\\nPriority: P1\\nRisk: medium\\nScope: current\\nAgent Owner: codex\\nAgent Eligible: codex\\nTags: nextjs,tracker,offline,patient-context\\nFile Scope:\\n- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js\\n- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js\\n- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js\\n\\nIssue:\\nThe Next.js tracker route is still passing session.user.id (Supabase auth UUID) into tracker bootstrap instead of the resolved users.id patient/profile context from useUserContext. That causes /api/programs?patient_id=\u003cauth UUID\u003e to return 403 on preview and blocks truthful offline/reconnect verification.\\n\\nContext:\\nuseUserContext already resolves both profileId and patientId. index-data fetches programs by patient_id and expects the resolved patient context, not the auth UUID. The same route already uses profileId for messages because auth UUID and users.id are different domains.\\n\\nConstraints/Caveats:\\n- Keep queue persistence keyed by auth userId where that is the intentional user-scoped storage key\\n- Use resolved patient/profile context only where tracker data APIs require users.id ownership\\n- Do not regress therapist/patient route behavior\\n\\nValidation Checklist:\\n- [ ] /api/programs no longer requests patient_id=session.user.id\\n- [ ] Tracker loads programs/exercises/logs again on preview for the signed-in test account\\n- [ ] Offline/reconnect verification can proceed against a real populated tracker state\\n\\nDependencies:\\n- discovered-from: pt-780.3\\n- blocks: pt-780.3.1\\n\\nReactivation Trigger:\\nn/a","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:19:15Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-23T00:19:15Z"}

{"notes":"Relevant decomposition guidance copied from pt-ps9 on 2026-03-22:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Keep the page focused on auth/use* hook composition, route-level modal state, and passing props to child components.
- Prefer scoped hooks/components over leaving mixed logic in the page file.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior while extracting bootstrap/state concerns.
- Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where the extracted /program concerns live.
- Treat this as an AI-manageable ownership-boundary refactor, not just a shrink-the-file pass.
Implementation plan for pt-21o as of 2026-03-22:
1. Extract hooks/useProgramPageData.js for /program bootstrap and offline data ownership.
   - Move out of pages/program.js: applyBootstrap(), loadData(), offline cache hydration/fallback, persistProgramSnapshot(), commitProgramSnapshot(), access/load/offline state, snapshot refs/getCurrentSnapshot(), and the session-driven bootstrap effects.
   - Keep behavior unchanged: therapist/admin access gate, patient-scoped user resolution, cached fallback notice, cache writes, and sign-out state clearing.
2. Extract hooks/useProgramPageState.js for /program workspace state ownership.
   - Move out of pages/program.js: search/showArchived/roleSearch/dosageSearch, activeExercise/roleExerciseId/dosageExerciseId/dosageTarget, rolesLoading/vocabSaving, derived filtered option lists, formExercise/roleExercise/dosageExercise/selectedProgram, and wrapper handlers around useProgramMutationActions/useProgramVocabActions.
   - Keep behavior unchanged: selecting an exercise opens the right form, successful save updates role/dosage selectors, dosage modal target closes on save, and role/vocab saving flags still bracket async work.
3. Reduce pages/program.js to route orchestration only.
   - Keep in page: auth guard, NavMenu route shell, calling the extracted hooks, wiring queue banner + child components, and passing props.
   - Do not leave bootstrap logic, filtering logic, snapshot persistence, or mutation-wrapper state in the page.
4. Update pt-rebuild/README.md.
   - Add entries for any new /program hooks with ownership guidance so future agents know where bootstrap/offline logic and page-state/mutation wiring now live.
5. Validate after refactor.
   - Prefer Vercel signal if available; otherwise run local build only if needed.
   - Explicit confidence review pass: verify the queue banner, New button, selector filtering, ExerciseForm open/edit flow, roles workspace, dosage workspace, vocab editor, and DosageModal wiring all remain present in pages/program.js after extraction.
Target outcome:
- pages/program.js under the 500L hard cap and functioning as a true orchestrator.
- New hooks stay at or under the 150L hook cap; if one exceeds cap, split again rather than pushing logic back into the page.
Progress update 2026-03-22:\n- Extracted hooks/useProgramPageData.js for /program bootstrap, access gating, online load, and cached offline fallback.\n- Extracted hooks/useProgramDataSnapshot.js for cached editor-data snapshot writes.\n- Extracted hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js for workspace selection/search/modal state and derived option lists.\n- Extracted hooks/useProgramMutationUi.js for mutation loading flags and UI-facing wrapper handlers.\n- Reduced pages/program.js from 567 lines to 303 lines; page now mainly composes auth, hooks, route shell, queue banner, and child components.\n- Updated README hook ownership notes.\n- Validation: npm run build passed."}

- Extract state/effects into hooks, not lib, because the logic is stateful and page-specific

{"id":"pt-spg.8","title":"Verify undo and reset parity","description":"Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that Previous Set removes only the most recent accepted set, timer reset clears only live progress, blocked undo shows correct copy, and undo feedback names the removed set number.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Previous Set (previous-set action) removes only the most recently accepted set from currentSession.sets. When currentSession.sets is empty, the action must be blocked and show "No sets to undo" rather than silently doing nothing. Undo success feedback must identify the removed set number (not a generic "set removed" message). Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are untouched. Live counter or timer progress is separate from accepted sets and is not affected by Previous Set.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Previous Set removes only the most recently accepted set from currentSession.sets
- [ ] Previous Set when no accepted sets exist shows "No sets to undo" (not a silent no-op)
- [ ] Undo success feedback identifies the removed set number specifically
- [ ] Timer reset clears live timer progress only — currentSession.sets is unaffected
- [ ] Live counter value is not removed by Previous Set — only accepted sets are affected

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

n/a

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Previous Set (previous-set action) removes only the most recently accepted set from currentSession.sets. When currentSession.sets is empty, the action must be blocked and show "No sets to undo" rather than silently doing nothing. Undo success feedback must identify the removed set number (not a generic "set removed" message). Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are untouched. Live counter or timer progress is separate from accepted sets and is not affected by Previous Set.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
The spec requires explicit blocked/failed behavior for: finish with zero accepted sets ("Please log at least one set before finishing"), undo with no accepted sets ("No sets to undo"), zero-value manual entry ("Please enter a value greater than 0"), failed exercise load ("Failed to load exercises. Check your connection."), failed history load ("Failed to load history."), missing patient context (blocks bootstrap), missing message recipient (blocks send with appropriate copy). None of these may be silent no-ops or blank areas.

Constraints/Caveats:

- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] "No sets to undo" is shown when Previous Set is triggered with no accepted sets
- [ ] "Please enter a value greater than 0" is shown when a zero-value set is attempted
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown on failed exercise load with no cache
- [ ] "Failed to load history." is shown on failed history load
- [ ] Missing patient context blocks normal data bootstrap (not a silent pass-through)
- [ ] Missing message recipient blocks send with appropriate copy (not a silent no-op)

Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current

Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that adherence uses local-midnight day-difference with correct bucket colors, formatDateTimeWithZone is used for history timestamps, backdate rewrites both session and set timestamps, and 30-second polling cadence is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
Adherence: normalize both the current time and performed_at to local midnight before computing day difference. 0 days = "Done today". Bucket colors: green for 1-3 days, orange warning for 4-7 days, red warning for 8+ days. History timestamps: formatDateTimeWithZone(...) must be used — no raw ISO strings. Backdate: rewrites both the session-level timestamp and each individual set's performed_at to the chosen value. Polling: first message check immediately after bootstrap, then every 30 seconds while signed in.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Both current time and performed_at are normalized to local midnight before day-difference computation
- [ ] 0-day difference renders as "Done today" (not "0 days ago")
- [ ] 1-3 day bucket renders with green styling
- [ ] 4-7 day bucket renders with orange warning styling
- [ ] 8+ day bucket renders with red warning styling
- [ ] formatDateTimeWithZone(...) is used for all history card timestamps
- [ ] Backdate rewrites both the session-level timestamp and each set's performed_at
- [ ] Message freshness check fires immediately after bootstrap then every 30 seconds

Status: open

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline queue migration normalizes old-format records and collapses hold-bug sessions using the exact static bug-signature collapse rule.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the stored offline queue contains older session formats, the tracker must normalize them. The hold-bug collapse rule: multiple old sets with both reps AND seconds null must be collapsed into one hold set. This exact signature (reps null AND seconds null on multiple sets in the same session) is the trigger for collapse — not a generic "null set" condition. This migration must happen when the queue is loaded, before any sync attempt.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Queue migration runs when the stored queue is loaded, before any sync attempt
- [ ] Old-format queue entries are normalized to the current session shape
- [ ] Multiple sets with both reps: null AND seconds: null in the same session are collapsed into one hold set
- [ ] The collapse rule requires both reps AND seconds to be null (not just one field null)
- [ ] Normal sets (with reps or seconds values) are not affected by the collapse rule
- [ ] Migration handles the case where the queue is empty or absent gracefully

- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

n/a

Status: open
Priority: P2

Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex

Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-offline
File Scope:

- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this call order for authenticated online startup: /api/users first (role and patient context), then /api/programs?patient_id=..., then transform exercises, then /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000&patient_id=..., render history before picker cards, and only then hydrate offline cache in the background. Cache writes must remain a background follow-up and must not block the first trustworthy interactive tracker render.

Constraints/Caveats:

- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass

- [ ] /api/programs?patient_id=... is called after user context is established
- [ ] /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000&patient_id=... is called after programs
- [ ] History is rendered before picker exercise cards are rendered
- [ ] Background cache hydration happens after the shell is interactive, not before
- [ ] No program or history data is used before patient context (viewingPatientId) is resolved

- blocks: n/a

- PT_Backup/beads/docs/PLUGIN.md — on_close hook details for enforcement

bd mail setup questions to investigate:
- messaging.md says bd mail delegates to an external provider via BEADS_MAIL_DELEGATE env var (e.g. 'gt mail' in an orchestrator context)
- 'gt mail' appears to be Gas Town / orchestrator-specific — may not apply here
- Need to check: is there a standalone bd mail setup that works without an orchestrator?
- Or can we use on_close hook directly for cross-agent notification instead of bd mail?
- BEADS_ACTOR env var sets sender identity (replaces deprecated BD_ACTOR)
- Check if bd mail works standalone: bd mail inbox / bd mail send — try with just BEADS_ACTOR set
bd mail requires Gas Town orchestrator — too much overhead, not applicable here. Cross-agent notification path: on_close hook writes bd note to other agent's AMC bead directly. Zero external dependencies. Pursue this instead of bd mail.
claude-handoff = no. Claude Code desktop only, not CLI, and doesn't help Codex anyway. beads-compound = no, Codex not supported. BeadHub = worth investigating for inter-agent messaging — agent agnostic bd wrapper, has async mail + sync chat. Check version compatibility with bd 0.62.0 first. Primary path remains on_close hook (fires for both agents). Secondary: BeadHub if version-compatible.
Community tools investigation complete.

WORTH PURSUING:
- BeadHub (bdh) — explicit Claude Code + Codex support. File locking prevents agent collisions on shared files. Async mail works for both. Codex limitation: cannot self-notify, human must prompt. Docker or hosted at beadhub.ai (free for OSS). Check 0.62.0 compat.
- Foolery — dispatches + monitors both agents from one UI. Supports Claude Code + Codex CLI explicitly. Routes task types per agent. Local server, Node.js only. v0.4.0 (2026-03-11), actively maintained.
- beads-web — human Kanban visibility layer. Windows x64 binary. Dolt-aware. beads-ui is lighter alternative (npm install -g).

RULED OUT: claude-handoff (Claude only), claude-protocol (hooks Claude only), beads-sdk (dev lib), lavra/beads-compound (no Codex).

CUSTOM COMMAND IDEAS from reviewing these tools:
1. /session-start — read + close previous AMC bead, create new one, run bd ready filtered by assignee. Replaces manual session startup ritual.
2. /session-end — bd list in_progress sweep, close done beads, final bd note on AMC bead, git push, bd dolt push. Replaces manual landing-the-plane.
3. /handoff — write structured context note to other agent's AMC bead via bd note. Lightweight version of claude-handoff without the file complexity.
4. /verify-queue — list all browser_verify beads assigned to claude, open preview URL. One command to start a verification pass.
5. on_close hook — check if closing bead was browser_verify, require verification evidence in close reason (enforcement not just convention).
6. file-lock pattern from BeadHub — before touching a shared file, check if other agent has it claimed. Could be a pre-coding step in AGENTS.md even without BeadHub installed."}

- Check if bd mail works standalone: bd mail inbox / bd mail send — try with just BEADS_ACTOR set
bd mail requires Gas Town orchestrator — too much overhead, not applicable here. Cross-agent notification path: on_close hook writes bd note to other agent's AMC bead directly. Zero external dependencies. Pursue this instead of bd mail.
claude-handoff = no. Claude Code desktop only, not CLI, and doesn't help Codex anyway. beads-compound = no, Codex not supported. BeadHub = worth investigating for inter-agent messaging — agent agnostic bd wrapper, has async mail + sync chat. Check version compatibility with bd 0.62.0 first. Primary path remains on_close hook (fires for both agents). Secondary: BeadHub if version-compatible.
Community tools investigation complete.

WORTH PURSUING:
- BeadHub (bdh) — explicit Claude Code + Codex support. File locking prevents agent collisions on shared files. Async mail works for both. Codex limitation: cannot self-notify, human must prompt. Docker or hosted at beadhub.ai (free for OSS). Check 0.62.0 compat.
- Foolery — dispatches + monitors both agents from one UI. Supports Claude Code + Codex CLI explicitly. Routes task types per agent. Local server, Node.js only. v0.4.0 (2026-03-11), actively maintained.
- beads-web — human Kanban visibility layer. Windows x64 binary. Dolt-aware. beads-ui is lighter alternative (npm install -g).

RULED OUT: claude-handoff (Claude only), claude-protocol (hooks Claude only), beads-sdk (dev lib), lavra/beads-compound (no Codex).

CUSTOM COMMAND IDEAS from reviewing these tools:
1. /session-start — read + close previous AMC bead, create new one, run bd ready filtered by assignee. Replaces manual session startup ritual.
2. /session-end — bd list in_progress sweep, close done beads, final bd note on AMC bead, git push, bd dolt push. Replaces manual landing-the-plane.
3. /handoff — write structured context note to other agent's AMC bead via bd note. Lightweight version of claude-handoff without the file complexity.
4. /verify-queue — list all browser_verify beads assigned to claude, open preview URL. One command to start a verification pass.
5. on_close hook — check if closing bead was browser_verify, require verification evidence in close reason (enforcement not just convention).
6. file-lock pattern from BeadHub — before touching a shared file, check if other agent has it claimed. Could be a pre-coding step in AGENTS.md even without BeadHub installed.
BeadHub clarification: does NOT replace beads. bdh is a transparent wrapper over bd — calls bd commands under the hood. Existing beads database, Dolt sync, all beads untouched. Additive only. User runs Docker so server requirement is not a blocker.

FINAL INVESTIGATION CRITERIA before adopting BeadHub or Foolery:
1. Resource usage — how much CPU/RAM does the server consume at idle and under load? (Serena was rejected for this reason — too heavy)
2. MCP or not? — if it adds an MCP server, that means +++ tokens per tool call. Need to know if bdh/Foolery use MCP or pure CLI.
3. Token impact — does it add tokens to every message (like MCP system prompts do), or is it CLI-only (zero token overhead when not actively called)? Net token cost vs. any savings from better coordination.

Serena precedent: rejected because resource/token overhead wasn't worth the benefit. Apply same bar here."}

Issue:
Review the current beads configuration and tracker taxonomy for this repo, decide which settings and issue-type conventions should be formalized, and track the resulting configuration and documentation updates in child beads.
Context:
This repo is maintained entirely by agents. We reviewed updated upstream beads configuration guidance from the PT_Backup clone and compared it with actual open and recently closed beads in pt-rebuild. Current direction so far: keep browser_verify as a custom status, use built-in types more deliberately, consider adding a verification issue type, and update local docs so future agents apply the agreed type meanings consistently.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep the type system small and durable
- Prefer built-in beads concepts unless a custom bucket clearly improves agent workflow
- Align local repo guidance with actual tracker usage and upstream beads support
- Avoid creating duplicate documentation or overlapping tracker policy beads
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Decisions about relevant beads config options are captured in tracker/docs
- [ ] Any new type or status choices are tracked with explicit follow-up beads
- [ ] Local agent guidance reflects the agreed tracker-type policy
- [ ] Remaining config areas to discuss stay linked to this parent bead
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:

- A recommendation is made for or against adding erification as a custom type

- [ ] A recommendation is made for or against adding erification as a custom type

Issue:
Update local repo guidance so agents use beads issue types more deliberately and consistently, including the agreed meanings for bug, feature, task, chore, epic, decision, and any approved repo-specific types.
Context:
Current beads usage in pt-rebuild leans heavily on task and epic, even though the repo already has meaningful behavior-repair, verification, implementation, and maintenance work shapes. Upstream beads guidance supports a small core type system plus optional custom types, so local docs should explain the repo's chosen meanings clearly.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep guidance concise and practical for agent sessions
- Avoid overfitting the taxonomy to one migration phase
- Ensure any type guidance matches the actual configured types in the repo
## Acceptance Criteria
- Local docs clearly define when to use each supported issue type
- Examples reflect current pt-rebuild workflow rather than generic beads examples
- Guidance stays aligned with configured statuses/types in the repo
- Future agents can classify new beads without ad hoc interpretation
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Local docs clearly define when to use each supported issue type
- [ ] Examples reflect current pt-rebuild workflow rather than generic beads examples
- [ ] Guidance stays aligned with configured statuses/types in the repo
- [ ] Future agents can classify new beads without ad hoc interpretation
Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-y7v
- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if agents continue to classify most work as generic tasks after guidance is updated."}

new_value: Problem: verification-only work was getting flattened into generic task usage. Root Cause: repo guidance did not define a first-class verification bucket even though verification beads are a recurring work shape. Change Made: added custom type verification in .beads/config.yaml and updated local guidance to define when to use it. Files Touched: .beads/config.yaml; pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_OPERATIONS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md. Validation: bd types now lists verification under configured custom types. Follow-ups: future verification beads should be typed verification rather than generic task when the bead's main purpose is proof-gathering.
all steps complete

Problem: agents were overusing generic task and lacked repo-specific examples for type selection. Root Cause: local docs described workflow and statuses well but did not give a practical issue-type policy with examples, and the generated quickref did not strongly warn against hand edits. Change Made: added repo-specific type guidance with examples to AGENTS.md and BEADS_OPERATIONS.md; updated the quickref generator header and regenerated BEADS_QUICKREF.md. Files Touched: pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_OPERATIONS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md; pt-rebuild/scripts/generate-beads-quickref.mjs. Validation: regenerated quickref includes the stronger header and the source docs now include type-selection examples. Follow-ups: keep BEADS_OPERATIONS.md as the source of truth for future quickref changes.

Problem: the repo needed an explicit beads config and tracker-type policy review after the upstream beads update. Root Cause: config behavior, custom status usage, and issue-type meanings had evolved without one local tracker item tying the decisions together. Change Made: enabled repo-level beads settings earlier in the session, created child beads for verification type and doc policy, added the verification custom type, and updated local guidance plus quickref generation. Files Touched: .beads/config.yaml; pt-rebuild/AGENTS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_OPERATIONS.md; pt-rebuild/docs/BEADS_QUICKREF.md; pt-rebuild/scripts/generate-beads-quickref.mjs. Validation: bd types shows verification as a configured custom type and docs now define issue-type usage with examples. Follow-ups: none for this review bead; future config changes can open a new child if needed.

new_value: {"notes":"Review-only context added 2026-03-23: No runtime power profiling was performed, but the current tracker code has several plausible battery contributors on iPhone. Index uses window.requestAnimationFrame in pt-rebuild/pages/index.js:214 during exercise selection flow. Messages poll every 30 seconds via useMessages (pt-rebuild/hooks/useMessages.js:21 and :46). Active timer mode runs a 1-second interval in pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js:75. Audio/speech side effects create/resume an AudioContext at pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js:12-15 and queue speech/beep work at :47-59. This does not prove root cause, but it narrows the first investigation targets to polling, timer intervals, speech/audio, and any repeated rerender activity while the tracker stays open on phone. User-reported symptom preserved: about 19 percent battery drain over 43 minutes on iPhone 14 Pro Max."}

new_value: {"notes":"Review-only context added 2026-03-23: I loaded /pt-view successfully on the nextjs preview in a 430x932 mobile viewport and did not reproduce the blank state in that runtime pass, so this still looks intermittent. That means any stalled or prolonged auth bootstrap presents as a completely blank screen with no placeholder. useMessages also starts a recurring poll loop on the page (pt-rebuild/hooks/useMessages.js:21 and :46), but the immediate blank-screen risk is the null render during auth/loading. The most likely code surface is SessionLoggerModal layout: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101 uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)) for the main field grid, :107 uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr)) for formDataGrid, and :113 fixes withUnit to 1fr 90px. On narrower modal content or exercises with more required fields/units, those minimums could force cramped wrapping or overlap. Relevant runtime close surface is pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js:97. Suggested repro: use a phone-width viewport on index, open an exercise with more required form parameters than Long Arc Quad, then inspect Set cards in the Log Set modal for wrap/overlap issues.
Additional user-provided evidence 2026-03-23: screenshot shows the index Log Set modal on phone with the Set 1 card crowded/clipped around the Reps / Side row and Weight row, with the right-side unit control and edge spacing leaving the field area visibly compressed. Treat this as confirmed mobile layout evidence even though my own runtime pass on Long Arc Quad did not fully reproduce the same clipping state. Relevant layout surfaces remain pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, :107, :113, and :155.

Treat this as confirmed mobile layout evidence even though my own runtime pass on Long Arc Quad did not fully reproduce the same clipping state. User clarification after runtime mobile review: the layout problem is not exercise-specific and is not caused by Weight because the trailing unit selector stays very narrow ('lb'). The general failure mode is any paired row where both columns need meaningful width, such as Reps + Side and similar two-column field combinations. In the current iPhone 14 Pro Max emulation (430x932 @ DPR 3), the Log Set modal shows that wider two-column rows are the ones that crowd/clip while Weight + unit still fits. Treat this as a general two-column mobile grid defect in SessionLoggerModal rather than a single-exercise case. Relevant code: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, 107, 113, 155-159.
Additional runtime confirmation from isolated iPhone 14 Pro Max emulation using test-patient2@pttracker.app: the issue reproduces beyond Long Arc Quad. Example: Star Taps (Right Stance) -> Log Set shows the same Reps + Side paired-row problem. DOM measurement confirms the root cause is real overflow, not just perceived crowding: SessionLoggerModal_fieldGrid computes to two 173.6px columns, but the Reps input renders at 202.4px wide, so it extends into the neighboring Side column. Weight + unit continues to fit only because the trailing unit selector stays much narrower. This supports treating the bug as a general mobile two-column field-grid overflow affecting paired inputs across exercises. Relevant code: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, 107, 113, 155-159."}

new_value: {"description":"## Summary\\nDefine what should happen when a pt_category is deleted while exercises are still assigned to it.\\n\\n## Decision\\nPending. This bead exists to capture and resolve the decision before implementation.\\n\\n## Rationale\\nDeleting an in-use pt_category could leave active exercises in an invalid or confusing state, so the allowed behavior needs to be defined intentionally.\\n\\n## Alternatives Considered\\n- Prohibit deletion while active exercises are assigned\\n- Require reassignment before delete\\n- Allow deletion but bulk-reassign affected exercises during the flow\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n- The allowed delete behavior is explicitly decided\\n- Required safeguards or reassignment flow is documented\\n\\n## Notes\\nRequested as child 2.2.2 under the vocab editor deletion-behavior parent."}

Issue:
Dosage summaries such as 4 x 20 ft and 2 x 10 reps are currently ambiguous about whether the set count is per side or total. For sided exercises, the wording should reflect that sets are per side; for non-sided exercises, the wording should remain total.
Context:
User clarified the canonical rule: SxR (side) means S sets per side, while SxR (both) means S total sets. The same rule applies when distance replaces reps. Existing tracker/program summary strings show raw sets x target without saying whether the value is per side or total, which can mislead users reviewing dosage or logged-set summaries.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep the wording concise and readable on mobile
- Do not change the underlying stored dosage values while fixing labels
- Use the same terminology consistently across tracker and program surfaces
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Sided dosage summaries explicitly indicate per-side semantics where needed
- [ ] Non-sided summaries continue to read as total sets
- [ ] Distance-based and rep-based summaries use consistent wording
- [ ] Compact/mobile layouts remain readable

History/list surfaces also show distance and side as separate tokens rather than a combined semantic summary at pt-rebuild/components/HistoryList.js:23-24 and pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseHistoryModal.js:43-44.\nImplemented shared prescribed-dosage formatter in pt-rebuild/lib/dosage-summary.js and routed ExercisePicker plus ProgramDosageWorkspace through it. New wording uses canonical grammar: S x R reps, S x R reps x T sec hold, S x T sec, S x D ft, with 'per side' appended when pattern === 'side'. Verification: node --test test/dosage-summary.test.js (7 passing assertions). Scan confirmed no hidden third prescribed-dosage builder on the Next.js side; NextSetConfirmModal, HistoryList, ExerciseHistoryModal, and lib/timer-panel.js format logged sets or live targets rather than prescribed dosage summaries."}

Duration-based dosage summaries are not consistently formatted in the rebuilt tracker. duration_seconds replaces reps and should read as S x T sec, with per-side wording added when the exercise pattern is side. One current rebuilt summary path still renders duration as a duration label instead of the canonical dosage phrase.

Legacy static tracker formats duration as ${sets} ×  sec in pt-rebuild/public/index.html:2162. The rebuilt ExercisePicker already follows a similar shape at pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js:24, but ProgramDosageWorkspace currently renders ${sets} × s duration at pt-rebuild/components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js:9, which does not match the target wording clarified by the user.
Constraints/Caveats:
- duration_seconds replaces reps entirely
- Preserve the no-parentheses hold versus parentheses hold differences only where explicitly intended by the user for hold formatting parity
- Keep output concise and mobile-readable

- [ ] Duration dosage summaries read as S x T sec
- [ ] Sided duration dosage summaries read as S x T sec per side
- [ ] No duration summary path says duration after the numeric value

Dosage summaries such as 4 x 20 ft and 2 x 10 reps are currently ambiguous about whether the set count is per side or total. For sided exercises, the wording should reflect that sets are per side; for non-sided exercises, the wording should remain total.
Context:
User clarified the canonical rule: SxR (side) means S sets per side, while SxR (both) means S total sets. The same rule applies when distance replaces reps. Existing tracker/program summary strings show raw sets x target without saying whether the value is per side or total, which can mislead users reviewing dosage or logged-set summaries.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep the wording concise and readable on mobile
- Do not change the underlying stored dosage values while fixing labels
- Use the same terminology consistently across tracker and program surfaces
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Sided dosage summaries explicitly indicate per-side semantics where needed
- [ ] Non-sided summaries continue to read as total sets
- [ ] Distance-based and rep-based summaries use consistent wording
- [ ] Compact/mobile layouts remain readable

Validation: node --test test/dosage-summary.test.js. Follow-ups: none; shared helper now covers current prescribed-dosage summary surfaces.

Problem: prescribed-dosage wording on rebuilt tracker and program surfaces was ambiguous about per-side versus total semantics and drifted across components. Root Cause: dosage summary copy was duplicated in component-local helpers with inconsistent rules for hold, duration, distance, and side wording. Change Made: added shared lib/dosage-summary.js and routed ExercisePicker plus ProgramDosageWorkspace through it, standardizing S x R reps, S x R reps x T sec hold, S x T sec, S x D ft, and per-side suffixing when pattern is side. Files Touched: pt-rebuild/lib/dosage-summary.js; pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js; pt-rebuild/components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js; pt-rebuild/test/dosage-summary.test.js; pt-rebuild/README.md; pt-rebuild/docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md. - Do not change stored dosage values

new_value: {"notes":"Root cause confirmed in shared dosage-summary helper: plain object spread let null program fields overwrite valid exercise metadata from /index, so distance_feet and dosage_type could disappear while current_reps stayed 1. Fix changed the helper to resolve each field by first non-null value instead of raw object spread. Added regression test covering a program row with reps=1 plus null distance metadata and an exercise with distance dosage metadata.\nReopened after preview verification on 2026-03-23. Playwright on both the branch alias preview and the latest deployment still renders Monster Walk as 4 x 1 reps and Side Steps as 2 x 1 reps per side, so the prior fix did not resolve the live data/render path.\nLive preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change."}

new_value:
{"notes":"Root cause confirmed in shared dosage-summary helper: plain object spread let null program fields overwrite valid exercise metadata from /index, so distance_feet and dosage_type could disappear while current_reps stayed 1. Fix changed the helper to resolve each field by first non-null value instead of raw object spread. Added regression test covering a program row with reps=1 plus null distance metadata and an exercise with distance dosage metadata.
Reopened after preview verification on 2026-03-23. Playwright on both the branch alias preview and the latest deployment still renders Monster Walk as 4 x 1 reps and Side Steps as 2 x 1 reps per side, so the prior fix did not resolve the live data/render path.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change."}

Fix changed the helper to resolve each field by first non-null value instead of raw object spread. Added regression test covering a program row with reps=1 plus null distance metadata and an exercise with distance dosage metadata.
Reopened after preview verification on 2026-03-23. Playwright on both the branch alias preview and the latest deployment still renders Monster Walk as 4 x 1 reps and Side Steps as 2 x 1 reps per side, so the prior fix did not resolve the live data/render path.

Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/dosage-summary.js

- pt-rebuild/components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js
Issue:
After centralizing dosage summary wording, some distance-based exercises render as reps, for example showing 4 x 1 reps instead of 4 x 20 ft. The shared formatter or merge order is allowing fallback rep fields to win when distance should remain authoritative.
Context:
The rebuilt app stores some exercise metadata with fallback rep fields while distance_feet replaces reps for summary display. The new shared helper merged exercise and program data, and a regression was reported immediately after rollout: distance now shows as reps on at least one surface.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the approved dosage grammar from pt-l8q7
- Do not change stored dosage values
- Keep distance and duration as reps-replacing summaries
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Distance dosage renders as S x D ft
- [ ] Sided distance dosage renders as S x D ft per side
- [ ] Rep dosage still renders as S x R reps
- [ ] Hold and duration summaries remain correct

- discovered-from: pt-l8q7
- blocks: n/a
