Fix changed the helper to resolve each field by first non-null value instead of raw object spread. Added regression test covering a program row with reps=1 plus null distance metadata and an exercise with distance dosage metadata.
Reopened after preview verification on 2026-03-23. Playwright on both the branch alias preview and the latest deployment still renders Monster Walk as 4 x 1 reps and Side Steps as 2 x 1 reps per side, so the prior fix did not resolve the live data/render path.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change.
Implemented repo-side repair: db/migrate.js now reads legacy current.distanceFeet before current.distance for patient_program imports, and added supabase/migrations/20260324010000_backfill_distance_programs.sql to backfill Side Steps and Monster Walk program rows to dosage_type=distance, distance_feet=20, reps_per_set=null when they still have the legacy reps=1 placeholder shape.
Removed the earlier Firebase-import script change after user confirmed the import path is permanently retired. Remaining repo-side fix is the one-time Supabase backfill migration only: supabase/migrations/20260324010000_backfill_distance_programs.sql.
Repo change committed as a one-time Supabase backfill migration only. No deployment automation was found that runs supabase db push on Vercel; verifying post-push whether live data changes anyway.
Pushed commit bec4d7f to origin/nextjs. Re-ran live Supabase backup after push (backup-2026-03-24_00-51-32.json); Side Steps and Monster Walk patient_programs rows remain unchanged with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null. This confirms pushing the repo/migration file did not apply the DB backfill automatically.
Attempting direct remote migration apply via Supabase CLI using PT_Backup/supabase.env and SUPABASE_DB_URL after confirming project ref zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz is linked.
Supabase CLI reached the linked project successfully. Current blocker is migration history drift: remote has applied versions not present under local supabase/migrations. Proceeding with supabase migration fetch --linked to align local migration files with remote history before retrying db push."}

{"id":"pt-nngp","title":"Fix shared dosage summary selecting reps over distance","description":"Source Reference: pt-l8q7
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ui,dosage,distance,regression
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/dosage-summary.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js
Issue:
After centralizing dosage summary wording, some distance-based exercises render as reps, for example showing 4 x 1 reps instead of 4 x 20 ft. The shared formatter or merge order is allowing fallback rep fields to win when distance should remain authoritative.
Context:
The rebuilt app stores some exercise metadata with fallback rep fields while distance_feet replaces reps for summary display. The new shared helper merged exercise and program data, and a regression was reported immediately after rollout: distance now shows as reps on at least one surface.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the approved dosage grammar from pt-l8q7
- Do not change stored dosage values
- Keep distance and duration as reps-replacing summaries
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Distance dosage renders as S x D ft
- [ ] Sided distance dosage renders as S x D ft per side
- [ ] Rep dosage still renders as S x R reps
- [ ] Hold and duration summaries remain correct
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-l8q7
- blocks: n/a

{"id":"pt-9c5g","title":"Lunch intake: mobile and iPhone UI issues","description":"Source Reference: user lunch-time intake 2026-03-23
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ios,mobile,ui,intake
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/components
- pt-rebuild/hooks
Issue:
Track the newly reported iPhone/mobile issues affecting the Next.js tracker, pt-view, and program editor so they can be triaged and fixed as individual child beads.
Context:
User reported multiple issues while on lunch and asked that they be captured quickly: index log-modal overlap on phone, dark-mode timer/tap modal, timer modal dismissing on outside touch, heavy battery drain on iPhone 14 Pro Max, intermittent blank pt-view loading, and program editor hamburger placement on phone.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep each reported issue as a separate child bead for focused investigation and validation
- Preserve user-reported device context where provided
- Do not lose the exact symptom wording during intake
## Success Criteria
- Each reported issue is captured as a separate child bead
- Mobile/iPhone context is preserved on the relevant beads
- The intake parent provides one place to find the related child issues
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Each reported issue is captured as a separate child bead
- [ ] Mobile/iPhone context is preserved on the relevant beads
- [ ] The intake parent provides one place to find the related child issues
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if additional issues from this same lunch-time report need to be added.","notes":"Review-only intake summary added 2026-03-23: checked the nextjs preview in a 430x932 mobile viewport (iPhone 14 Pro Max-style sizing). Confirmed by runtime/code review: program hamburger is displaced to top left on phone; timer/tap panel uses hard-coded dark styling; timer/tap overlay closes on outside touch. PT-view blank did not reproduce in this pass, but pt-view.js has an authLoading null-render path worth investigating. Logger modal overlap did not reproduce on Long Arc Quad specifically, but SessionLoggerModal grid minimums look like the main risk area for tighter exercises with more fields. Battery-drain item now has likely contributor surfaces documented (polling, timer interval, speech/audio, frame scheduling).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"codex","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-23T16:42:40Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T16:49:36Z","labels":["ios","mobile","nextjs","ui"]}

{"id":"pt-xjb","title":"Explore custom issue types and browser_verify enforcement","description":"User wants to explore: 1) What custom types make sense for this project (verification, agent-message, etc). 2) Whether types.custom can be used to enforce browser_verify close criteria. 3) Whether bd mail / on_close hooks could be used for cross-agent notification when a bead closes. Review docs/messaging.md in PT_Backup beads clone — bd mail is a first-class feature using BEADS_ACTOR identity. Hook system (on_create, on_update, on_close) receives JSON on stdin — could be used to enforce status rules at close time. Explore with user before implementing anything.","notes":"Docs to read next session:
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CONFIG.md — types.custom section (custom issue types)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md — bd types command, custom types examples
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/messaging.md — bd mail setup (already read, notes below)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/PLUGIN.md — on_close hook details for enforcement

bd mail setup questions to investigate:
- messaging.md says bd mail delegates to an external provider via BEADS_MAIL_DELEGATE env var (e.g. 'gt mail' in an orchestrator context)
- 'gt mail' appears to be Gas Town / orchestrator-specific — may not apply here
- Need to check: is there a standalone bd mail setup that works without an orchestrator?
- Or can we use on_close hook directly for cross-agent notification instead of bd mail?
- BEADS_ACTOR env var sets sender identity (replaces deprecated BD_ACTOR)
- Check if bd mail works standalone: bd mail inbox / bd mail send — try with just BEADS_ACTOR set
bd mail requires Gas Town orchestrator — too much overhead, not applicable here. Cross-agent notification path: on_close hook writes bd note to other agent's AMC bead directly. Zero external dependencies. Pursue this instead of bd mail.
claude-handoff = no. Claude Code desktop only, not CLI, and doesn't help Codex anyway. beads-compound = no, Codex not supported. BeadHub = worth investigating for inter-agent messaging — agent agnostic bd wrapper, has async mail + sync chat. Check version compatibility with bd 0.62.0 first. Primary path remains on_close hook (fires for both agents). Secondary: BeadHub if version-compatible.
Community tools investigation complete.

WORTH PURSUING:
- BeadHub (bdh) — explicit Claude Code + Codex support. File locking prevents agent collisions on shared files. Async mail works for both. Codex limitation: cannot self-notify, human must prompt. Docker or hosted at beadhub.ai (free for OSS). Check 0.62.0 compat.
- Foolery — dispatches + monitors both agents from one UI. Supports Claude Code + Codex CLI explicitly. Routes task types per agent. Local server, Node.js only. v0.4.0 (2026-03-11), actively maintained.
- beads-web — human Kanban visibility layer. Windows x64 binary. Dolt-aware. beads-ui is lighter alternative (npm install -g).

RULED OUT: claude-handoff (Claude only), claude-protocol (hooks Claude only), beads-sdk (dev lib), lavra/beads-compound (no Codex).

CUSTOM COMMAND IDEAS from reviewing these tools:
1. /session-start — read + close previous AMC bead, create new one, run bd ready filtered by assignee. Replaces manual session startup ritual.
2. /session-end — bd list in_progress sweep, close done beads, final bd note on AMC bead, git push, bd dolt push. Replaces manual landing-the-plane.
3. /handoff — write structured context note to other agent's AMC bead via bd note. Lightweight version of claude-handoff without the file complexity.
4. /verify-queue — list all browser_verify beads assigned to claude, open preview URL. One command to start a verification pass.
5. on_close hook — check if closing bead was browser_verify, require verification evidence in close reason (enforcement not just convention).
6. file-lock pattern from BeadHub — before touching a shared file, check if other agent has it claimed. Could be a pre-coding step in AGENTS.md even without BeadHub installed.
BeadHub clarification: does NOT replace beads. bdh is a transparent wrapper over bd — calls bd commands under the hood. Existing beads database, Dolt sync, all beads untouched. Additive only. User runs Docker so server requirement is not a blocker.

FINAL INVESTIGATION CRITERIA before adopting BeadHub or Foolery:
1. Resource usage — how much CPU/RAM does the server consume at idle and under load? (Serena was rejected for this reason — too heavy)
2. MCP or not? — if it adds an MCP server, that means +++ tokens per tool call. Need to know if bdh/Foolery use MCP or pure CLI.
3. Token impact — does it add tokens to every message (like MCP system prompts do), or is it CLI-only (zero token overhead when not actively called)? Net token cost vs. any savings from better coordination.

Serena precedent: rejected because resource/token overhead wasn't worth the benefit. Apply same bar here.
CRITICAL resource bar: Serena caused a full machine lockup — not just slow, machine became unusable. User's laptop is older. This is a hard requirement, not a preference. BeadHub/Foolery must be confirmed lightweight before any recommendation to install.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-23T01:50:18Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-23T02:03:50Z"}

{"id":"pt-uf1","title":"Agent Ops Epic: tracker/tooling friction log","description":"Purpose: shared operational issue sink for Codex/Claude when tooling/process friction reduces delivery speed or reliability (Beads runtime issues, merge/handoff pain, tracker drift, branch hygiene friction, CI friction). Rules: (1) create discovered-from child issues for concrete incidents, (2) include root cause + mitigation, (3) close child after fix, (4) keep epic open as longitudinal signal for whether Beads remains best-fit. Scope: agent operations only, not product features.","notes":"2026-03-13 Beads process feedback from active multi-agent migration work:
- Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for carving off parity slices without bloating the main tasks; closure-block notes made recovery easy; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is already synced, which then blocks bd dolt pull with a 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until metadata is committed. Concurrent writers also make non-fast-forward auto-push failures common.
- Operational lesson: implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can run in parallel threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Decision input for later: revisit whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active.
2026-03-13 follow-up after bd 0.60.0 update: the doc/tooling refresh work is resolved via ptrebuild-3di. Local beads mirror was refreshed, repo workflow docs were updated, stale bd sync references were removed from the local workflow, and a derived BEADS_QUICKREF.md generator was added so future command drift is less likely. Active bd now resolves to 0.60.0 from C:\Users\cindi\go\bin. Remaining friction not yet resolved: Windows can still leave the old AppData bd.exe locked on disk even after PATH cleanup, and multi-writer Dolt metadata churn still needs observation in real usage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:27:30Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:06:21Z"}

{"description":"**Source Reference:** pt-hzij
**Status:** open
**Priority:** 2 (medium)
**Risk:** Low — read path change only, no schema migration, no data loss
**Scope:** pt-rebuild JS files only; DB column left intact
**Agent Owner:** codex
**Agent Eligible:** yes
**Tags:** exercise, lifecycle, archived, refactor
**File Scope:** api/roles.js, components/ExerciseForm.js, components/ExerciseRolesWorkspace.js, components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js, components/ProgramExerciseSelector.js, hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js, hooks/usePtViewData.js, lib/exercise-sort.js, lib/handlers/exercises.js, lib/handlers/programs.js, lib/program-optimistic.js, lib/pt-view.js

**Issue:**
Archived exercises appear in the exercise list because filtering code reads exercise.archived (boolean) but the Next.js archive action only writes lifecycle_status = 'archived' — the boolean is never updated.

**Context:**
archived is a legacy boolean column. lifecycle_status is the canonical field. The codebase should read only from lifecycle_status everywhere. A sync hack was added to updateExercise in lib/handlers/exercises.js (lines 586-587) — remove it.

**Constraints/Caveats:**
- Do NOT drop the archived column from the DB — leave for a separate migration
- Do NOT use bd edit — use bd update flags only
- Follow layer rules in AGENTS.md before writing to any file
- Read both local MEMORY.md files before starting (C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\claude-memory\MEMORY.md and C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\PT_Backup\agent_memory\MEMORY.md)

**Required Changes:**
- api/roles.js:31 — .eq('exercises.archived', false) to .neq('exercises.lifecycle_status', 'archived')
- components/ExerciseForm.js:11,57 — remove archived from initial state and form population
- components/ExerciseRolesWorkspace.js:40, ProgramDosageWorkspace.js:46, ProgramExerciseSelector.js:40 — exercise.archived to exercise.lifecycle_status === 'archived'
- hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js:7,16 — same
- hooks/usePtViewData.js:61,82 — p.exercises?.archived (exercises is an array — inspect actual shape first)
- lib/exercise-sort.js:88 — exercise?.archived to exercise?.lifecycle_status === 'archived'
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:90 — remove archived: ex.archived from assembled object
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:346,399 — remove archived from createExercise destructure and DB insert
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:528,583,586-587 — remove archived from updateExercise, remove sync hack
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:760 — archived: true to lifecycle_status: 'archived'
- lib/handlers/programs.js:200 — check if archived is exercise-related; fix if so
- lib/program-optimistic.js:19 — archived: payload.archived ?? false to derive from lifecycle_status
- lib/pt-view.js:171 — program.exercises?.archived to lifecycle_status check

**Validation Checklist:**
- [ ] Archived exercises do NOT appear in list when show archived is off
- [ ] Archived exercises DO appear when show archived is on
- [ ] [archived] label still shows in dropdowns
- [ ] Creating a new exercise still works
- [ ] npm run build passes with no new errors

**Dependencies:** none"}

{"id":"pt-hzij","title":"Replace archived boolean with lifecycle_status throughout codebase","description":"The `archived` boolean on exercises is a legacy field. The canonical source of truth is `lifecycle_status = 'archived'`. Currently the codebase reads `exercise.archived` in most places but the Next.js archive action only sets `lifecycle_status`, so archived exercises still appear in lists.

All reads of `exercise.archived` must be replaced with `exercise.lifecycle_status === 'archived'`, and all writes of `archived: true/false` must be removed (only `lifecycle_status` should be written going forward). The sync hack added to `updateExercise` (lines 586-587 in lib/handlers/exercises.js) should be removed.

**Files to change:**
- `api/roles.js:31` — `.eq('exercises.archived', false)` → `.neq('exercises.lifecycle_status', 'archived')`
- `components/ExerciseForm.js:11,57` — remove `archived` from initial state and form population; use `lifecycle_status`
- `components/ExerciseRolesWorkspace.js:40` — `exercise.archived` → `exercise.lifecycle_status === 'archived'`
- `components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js:46` — same
- `components/ProgramExerciseSelector.js:40` — same
- `hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js:7,16` — same
- `hooks/usePtViewData.js:61,82` — `p.exercises?.archived` logic (also likely broken — exercises is an array)
- `lib/exercise-sort.js:88` — `exercise?.archived` → `exercise?.lifecycle_status === 'archived'`
- `lib/handlers/exercises.js:90` — remove `archived: ex.archived` from assembled object
- `lib/handlers/exercises.js:346,399` — remove `archived` from createExercise destructure and insert
- `lib/handlers/exercises.js:528,583,586-587` — remove `archived` from updateExercise destructure and updates object; remove sync hack
- `lib/handlers/exercises.js:760` — `archived: true` in delete/archive handler → `lifecycle_status: 'archived'`
- `lib/handlers/programs.js:200` — check if `archived` here is exercise-related
- `lib/program-optimistic.js:19` — `archived: payload.archived ?? false` → use lifecycle_status
- `lib/pt-view.js:171` — `program.exercises?.archived` → lifecycle_status check

Do NOT drop the `archived` column from the DB schema — leave that for a separate migration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T22:41:17Z","created_by":"vouser123","updated_at":"2026-03-24T22:41:17Z"}

- lib/handlers/programs.js:200 — check if archived is exercise-related; fix if so
- lib/program-optimistic.js:19 — archived: payload.archived ?? false to derive from lifecycle_status
- lib/pt-view.js:171 — program.exercises?.archived to lifecycle_status check

Archiving an exercise sets `lifecycle_status = 'archived'` but the boolean is not always written consistently. All read paths (filters, display labels, sort) still check `exercise.archived`, so exercises archived via lifecycle_status alone still appear in lists.

The fix is to stop treating `archived` as the source of truth. All filtering and display logic should check `lifecycle_status === 'archived'`, and all write paths should stop setting the `archived` boolean (or only set `lifecycle_status`). The boolean column can be left in the DB for now and dropped in a follow-up migration.

**Affected files:**
- `api/roles.js:31` — `.eq('exercises.archived', false)` → `.neq('exercises.lifecycle_status', 'archived')`

- `components/ProgramExerciseSelector.js:40` — label check

Issue:
Archived exercises appear in the exercise list because filtering code reads exercise.archived (boolean) but the Next.js archive action only writes lifecycle_status = 'archived'. The boolean is never updated, so archived exercises still surface in lists and selectors.

Context:
archived is a legacy boolean column. lifecycle_status is the canonical field. The codebase should read only from lifecycle_status everywhere. A sync hack exists in the updateExercise handler to keep the two in sync — remove it. Do NOT implement until pt-t1qr.1 (search) has added its inventory as a note on this bead.

- Do NOT start implementation until pt-t1qr.1 notes are present on this bead
- Do NOT drop the archived column from the DB — leave for pt-v4k4
- Do NOT use bd edit — use bd update flags only
- Read actual file contents before every change — no line numbers from memory
- Follow layer rules in AGENTS.md before touching any file

- [ ] Archived exercises DO appear when show archived is on
- [ ] Archived label still shows correctly in dropdowns
- [ ] Creating a new exercise still works
- [ ] npm run build passes with no new errors

MEASURED DATA (2026-03-24): 19% battery drain over 43 minutes on iPhone 14 Pro Max. 24% drain over 60 minutes. Vercel shows 0% cache hit rate — all requests hitting origin. These are the only confirmed data points. Prior notes in this bead are code-read speculation only — no runtime profiling has been performed.
MEASURED DATA (not speculation): Battery drain is consistent ~0.4%/min — 19% in 43 min and 24% in 60 min on iPhone 14 Pro Max. Consistent rate rules out one-time load spike; points to something running continuously. PRIMARY SUSPECT: /api/logs had 891 requests in 6 hours on Preview (~1 request every 24 seconds). All other endpoints were 10x lower (/api/users 92, /api/exercises 40, /api/programs 46). This is almost certainly a polling loop. ADDITIONAL DATA: INP 800ms on mobile (red/poor in Speed Insights) — main thread blocking on interaction. Vercel shows 3.9K Supabase API calls in 6 hours from preview (small user count). Data Cache and Runtime Cache both 0 — expected, app uses client-side Supabase JS, bypasses Next.js data cache entirely. Speed Insights shows no mobile data — likely PWA standalone mode not firing beacon. EXISTING NOTES ON BEAD ARE SPECULATION from code read, not profiling data. NEXT STEP: Find what is calling /api/logs on ~24s interval.
MEASURED DATA (not speculation):
- Battery drain: ~0.4%/min sustained — 19% in 43min and 24% in 60min on iPhone 14 Pro Max. Consistent rate points to continuous activity, not a one-time load spike.
- Vercel Fast Data Transfer (Preview, 6hrs): /api/logs had 891 requests — 10x more than any other endpoint (/api/users 92, /api/exercises 40, /api/programs 46). One request every ~24 seconds = likely a polling loop.
- Supabase: ~3.9K API calls in 6 hours from preview (small user count).
- Vercel Speed Insights (Mobile): INP 800ms (red/poor). Main thread is blocked 800ms on interactions. Combined with polling = CPU pegged continuously.
- Vercel Data Cache: 0 reads/0 writes — expected, app uses Supabase client-side not Next.js server fetch.
- Speed Insights not reporting on Mobile/PWA: standalone mode beacon may not fire. Desktop reports fine. Data untrustworthy for mobile until resolved.
PRIMARY SUSPECT: something polling /api/logs every ~24 seconds. Find and fix this first before investigating other contributors. Notes above this line are MEASURED. Notes below (if any from prior sessions) are SPECULATION from code read only."}

new_value: Problem: Beads still treated pttracker as the old rukuba repo and auto-push targeted the wrong Dolt remote. Root Cause: tracker metadata and Dolt origin were carried over during the repo move without being updated for pttracker. Change Made: updated the Beads repository fingerprint, set beads.role to maintainer, installed recommended git hooks, repointed Dolt origin to git+https://github.com/vouser123/pttracker.git, and added the missing .beads-credential-key Git ignore entry. Files Touched: .gitignore; Beads metadata/config. Validation: bd doctor returns 0 errors; bd dolt show reports pttracker origin; bd dolt push succeeds. Follow-ups: custom status value browser_verify:active is still invalid and can be normalized later if desired.

new_value: Problem: bd dolt pull failed after the pttracker migration even after the remote was repointed. Root Cause: the Dolt working set still had an uncommitted config row (kv.memory.browser-automation-mcp-guide-1-vercel-mcp-mcp), so pull aborted with 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes'. Change Made: diagnosed the exact dirty config row, committed the pending config state in Dolt, synced origin/main to the new pttracker remote, and verified both Beads wrapper commands and raw Dolt commands succeed. Files Touched: Beads/Dolt config state only. Validation: bd dolt pull succeeds; bd dolt push succeeds; bd doctor returns 0 errors. Follow-ups: the custom status value browser_verify:active still triggers a config warning and can be normalized separately.

old_value: {"id":"pt-g4vi","title":"Fix blank screen on offline reopen: trust warmed session without network getUser() call","description":"When app is opened offline after a prior signed-in session, useAuth calls getUser() which is a network request. Offline, this fails and is treated as an invalid token, signing the user out and leaving a blank screen. Fix: check isOfflineSignInError() before deciding to sign out — if the failure is a network error, trust the locally-stored getSession() result instead.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"perfidy-slicers-7n@icloud.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T22:10:36Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-28T22:10:36Z"}

new_value: Fixed in hooks/useAuth.js: when getUser() fails offline, isOfflineSignInError() now guards the sign-out path so a warmed session is trusted from IndexedDB instead of being cleared.
